To our knowledge, this is the first study to compare individual estimates of amounts of served food with the actual weight of the food served in a laboratory setting, where less disruptive data collection techniques are applied. Other studies assessing the accuracy of estimated portion sizes have used photographs as the reference for the assessment (Huybregts et al, 2007, Turconi et al, 2005), fake food items (Bucher et al, 2011, Bucher et al, 2012, Bucher et al, 2013) or average portion size weights based on repeated measures (Ngigi, Verbeke, & Pérez-Cueto, 2011). In contrast, the present study was performed using actual food items. In addition, the use of the Intelligent Buffet allowed the exact weight of food served by each participant to be recorded in a relatively undisturbed manner.

There are a few potential sources of bias in this study, one being measurement bias. An attempt was made to limit this by calibrating the equipment used for data collection before use and by following standard laboratory procedures. Therefore, if any measurement bias were to occur, it would be systematic throughout all observations. Secondly, the sample size may have been insufficient. However, the calculated power of 0.99 for the observed mean differences of both vegetables and whole grains suggests that the sample was enough to support the rejection of the null hypothesis. Previous studies have used a similar sample size with sufficient power (Mekhmoukh, Chapelot, & Bellisle, 2012). Thus, we assume that the result of the Wilcoxon signed-rank test was accurate (absence of type II error), and that it was correct to reject the null hypothesis since there was a significant difference between the two variables – the estimated and actual amount of food served.

Our findings are in agreement with similar studies conducted in a number of different countries and settings, including Norway (Uglem et al., 2008), the Netherlands (Lechner et al., 1997), Australia (Pollard et al., 2008) and Denmark (Sørensen et al., 2013), suggesting that individuals commonly have difficulties estimating food portion sizes with accuracy. The findings of a statistically significant difference between participants’ self-estimation and the actual weight of the vegetables served led to the rejection of the null hypothesis (Table 1). The participants in this study were also asked to estimate the weight of a whole grain serving in a relatively complex meal. It would be of interest to determine whether the results would have been more accurate if a simpler meal had been served (e.g. a bowl of cereal). However, the everyday diet commonly consists of prepared meals or ready-to-eat foods and individuals are often not aware of the actual composition of the foods they consume. Therefore, serving a “complex meal” is comparable with a real life situation, where participants are expected to estimate the amount of food served (Combet et al, 2013, Loh et al, 2013).

This study presents the question of whether food intake estimations that are independent of actual weighing are valid, particularly in the evaluation of adherence to healthy eating recommendations (Pérez-Cueto et al., 2012). This also underscores the importance of using alternative equipment and techniques, such as an Intelligent Buffet, in order to obtain actual data in studies measuring factors such as food intake (Bucher et al., 2012). The Intelligent Buffet presents many opportunities to conduct other studies or further research on this particular topic. In combination with video cameras, this equipment could be used for choice architectural experiments researching behaviour related to a buffet setting. For example, it could be used to investigate whether individual consumption differs in relation to the order of food items or the size of serving trays.

As seen in Table 1, there is no statistical correlation between measured and estimated values; they are independent variables meaning that there is no predictive relationship between the two measurements.

Consequently, if authors were to compare the difference between means they would probably find that the estimations are so different from the actual intake that they are neither reliable nor valid, thus supporting the use of alternative techniques such as the Intelligent Buffet when accuracy is a requirement of the research.

Generalizability of the findings is limited due to the study design and sample size. The use of a convenience sample from a university setting introduced a sample mainly consisting of higher educated participants aged between 20 and 29 years. Consequently, the results measure perception of serving size against the weight of the food served for this survey sample. A larger sample size and a different study design would need to be undertaken to explore if these findings extrapolate to the general University student community or to specific socio-economic groups within that community. The literature and the current study findings suggest the likelihood of an overestimation of portion sizes. However, further studies should be carried out before making conclusions about whether the general population is capable of accurately estimating food portion sizes and about the effects this could have on the degree of validity in studies involving self-estimation as a data collection method.

Considering that the majority of participants were highly educated people and nevertheless unable to correctly estimate the amounts of vegetables and whole grain served, and previous research highlighting that lower socioeconomic groups may find it difficult to interpret serving size recommendations, these findings may be of concern for nutrition educators (Drewnowski, Specter, 2004, Kamp et al, 2010, Robinson, 2008, Sinclair et al, 2013). Studies show that individuals from higher socio-demographic groups have a greater knowledge and comprehension of nutrition than individuals from low socio-demographic groups (Drewnowski, Specter, 2004, Pollard et al, 2008, Sinclair et al, 2013).

Furthermore, this study raises the question about whether self-estimation of portion sizes would be an appropriate way to evaluate intake of vegetables and whole grain cereals, particularly in self-serving settings, such as University Canteens, since it appears that the participants had very little ability to estimate their own intake (estimated serving vs actual serving were independent measures). Probably, this will be applicable to most other food groups, and thus this study challenges the way for instance communication towards healthy eating is delivered. It also calls for innovative ways to make the population aware of what portions are if the intention is to rely on the general ability to estimate quantities of food served or eaten.

Conclusion
The present study was the first to assess the accuracy of self-estimates of vegetables and whole grain portion sizes in a self-served meal conducted in a laboratory setting using an Intelligent Buffet combined with a quantitative questionnaire. When comparing the self-estimated amount of vegetables and whole grains with the actual amount, the analysis revealed a significant difference between the means (P < 0.001) where students’ self-reported portions were about three times bigger than the actual servings.

The study supports the use of innovative techniques such as the Intelligent Buffet. Further it supports the use of triangulation in order to obtain more precise estimations on intake and to better evaluate the real adherence to nutritional recommendations.

The questionnaires provided information on the profile of the participants and it appeared that there was little variety within the sample. Further limitations could be linked to the sample size and the methodological approach, particularly the equipment used for the data collection and data management, which had never been utilised prior to the study.

This study suggests that at least for this survey sample individuals do not provide accurate estimates of the amount of vegetables and whole grains they serve themselves on a plate. Our study findings lead us to question individuals’ ability to assess the amounts of vegetables and whole grain they serve themselves from a buffet. The degree of underestimation of servings within this small experimental group is high. On average participants assessed that they served themselves about three times what they actually did. Although there was a high awareness of the Danish 6 A Day recommendation among study participants, their ability to accurately estimate their serving size appeared to be a limited. These findings highlight the need for further research into the accuracy of self-estimated intake across the population and the implications of the overestimation of current consumption for nutrition promotion interventions.

Based on the findings of this study it is recommended that policy makers and social marketing campaign creators consider the development of more specific serving size measurements or that explicit communications e.g. regarding serves of plant foods used in nutrition education are needed to assist consumers in estimating own portions sizes in relation to general health recommendations.

ABSTRACT 
In recent years, driven by the spread of the COVID-19 pandemic, digital transformation in the healthcare sector is 
becoming increasingly important. Digital healthcare technologies, if adopted in a targeted manner and implemented in a cost-effective way, make it possible to reduce healthcare inequalities, improve the quality of 
healthcare provided and increase the well-being of citizens. The importance of digital transformation in 
healthcare is also attracting interest from academics. However, at present, there are few studies aimed at 
examining the degree of digital transformation in healthcare and the drivers of the adoption and implementation 
of digital solutions by healthcare facilities. This study aims to fill this gap by analysing the level of digital 
transformation of Italian hospitals and the factors that can affect this level of digital transformation. The analysis, 
conducted on a sample of 103 hospitals, shows a positive and significant impact of the hospital size, hospital age 
and hospital teaching status on the level of digital transformation. Furthermore, in relation to hospital 
complexity, it shows a positive effect of the presence of the emergency room and a non-significant influence of 
the number of hospital departments on the level of digital transformation.

summarise this: There are three forms of the personal name based on Hermanubis in papyri and inscriptions from Egypt, in addition to two "proper" polytheophoric names. (In four instances [1, 5, 17, 21] the ending of the name is abbreviated or not preserved.) The form Ἑρμάνουβιϲ, attested six times (2, 9, 11, 13, 14, 15), simply replicates the god's name without further ado, through the phenomenon of "direct theonymy" (the giving to humans of unaltered divine names) that became widespread in the Roman period.[8] In an odd instance (9) the form Ἑρμανουβιϲ in the nominative is borne by a woman, an Alexandrian nurse (ἡ τροφόϲ).[9] It is highly unusual for female theophoric names to be unmarked for gender in this way.[10] I suspect that because the element -ανουβιϲ in Ἑρμάνουβιϲ was often declined with a dental declension in -ιδ-, it might have been conflated with the similar declension of Greek feminine names in -ίϲ, -ίδοϲ.[11] If this is correct, it may be preferable to read the nurse's name as an oxytone (Ἑρμανουβίϲ). There are two further forms based on the god Hermanubis: Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (six instances: 3, 4, 7, 8, 12, 20) and Ἑρμανουβίων (four instances: 3, 6, 18, 19). -ᾶϲ and -ίων were, in fact, the commonest Greek suffixes for personal names based on the Egyptian god Ἄνουβιϲ.[12] It is interesting that in a business letter (3) the two forms Ἑρμανουβίων (l.38) and Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (l.41) are used interchangeably to designate the same individual. In this case, Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ is probably a hypocoristic of Ἑρμανουβίων, since the suffix -ᾶϲ often served this purpose.[13]

Corona pandemic has changed the world. We all witnessed the spread of the virus and have experienced various measures by the officials to stop the pandemic. In this problem you will see how fast a virus can spread under certain assumptions. You are going to implement a function "coronaSpread" which takes a population size "n" (number of people) and an array of infected individuals. We assume each person has a unique index from 1 to "n" and the parameter "infected" contains an array of indices of the infected individuals before the start of the simulation. You will implement 2 different simulations for this problem represented by 2 nested functions. Before we set the random seed to be "randSeed". (Already done for you in template) For the first simulation you will need to code a nested function "fullPandemic()" that will calculate how many days it takes for a virus to spread across the entire populations given that each infected person can infect at most 1 random person per day. You need to take into account the following: We start with some number of infected population. Remember an array "infected" contains indices of infected individuals. In each day: each of the infected individuals randomly infects at most one other person among "n" person in our simulation. That "other" individual can be the person itself as well which would mean that that person didnt infect anyone on that day. Also that random "other" person can be someone already infected. That means that in each iteration each person infects every other individual with the probability 1/"n". Your simulation stops once the entire peopulation is infected and you will store the number of days it took in the variable "day". Consider the following example that illustrates the algorithm for n=5 and infected=[2,5] Day 1: Infected individuals: [2,5]. person 2 randomly infects person 3 and person 5 randomly infects person 2. Note here that person 2 is already infected so nothing changes for that individual. Day 2: Infected individuals: [2,3,5]. person 2 randomly infects person 5. Person 3 randomly infects person 1. Person 5 randomly infects person 5. Day 3: Infected individuals: [1,2,3,5]. Person 1 randomly infects person 2. Person 2 randomly infects person 4. Person 3 randomly infects person 4. Person 5 randomly infects person 3. Here our simulation stops and we know that the entire population has been infected in 3 days.For the second simulation we assume that infected individuals will infect at most one person per day (as before) but this time they will self-isolate. That means that they will no longer infect others in the future. They can still be chosen randomly by someone else but their situation will not change. For the "selfIsolation()" nested function you will need to take into account the following: 
We start as before with the original number of infected population (given to you by the parameter "infected"). This population is not isolated at the moment. For each day: Each infected (and not isolated) person infects at most one other random individual. After that all the infected persons go into self-isolation (they will no longer infect others). However the newly infected individuals will infect others in the next iteration. You stop once the number of newly infected individuals in an iteration is 0 and return the total number of infected individuals for the entire simulation. Consider again the following example that illustrates the algorithm for n=6 and infected = [2,5] Day 1: Infected individuals: [2,5]. isolated individuals=[]. # Global variables
pause_monitoring = False
dark_mode = False
translated_dict = {}
previous_translated_content = ""

def translate_text(text_to_translate, prompt_template):
    prompt = prompt_template.format(text_to_translate=text_to_translate)
    completion = openai.ChatCompletion.create(
        model="gpt-3.5-turbo-0301",
        temperature=1,
        messages=[
            {
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"{prompt}",
            }
        ],
    )
    translated_text = (
        completion["choices"][0]
        .get("message")
        .get("content")
        .encode("utf8")
        .decode()
    )
    
    if log_translations:
        try:
            with open(translation_logfile, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                f.write(f"{translated_text}\n")
        except FileNotFoundError:
            pass
            
    return translated_text

def toggle_pause_monitoring():
    global pause_monitoring
    pause_monitoring = not pause_monitoring
    pause_button.config(
        text="Resume" if pause_monitoring else "Pause",
        relief=tk.SUNKEN if pause_monitoring else tk.RAISED
    )

def clipboard_monitor():
    global prompt_text
    old_clipboard = ""

    while True:
        if not pause_monitoring:
            new_clipboard = pyperclip.paste()

            # Remove line breaks from new_clipboard
            # new_clipboard = new_clipboard.replace('\n', '').replace('\r', '')

            # Compare new_clipboard with old_clipboard to detect changes
            if new_clipboard != old_clipboard and new_clipboard.strip() != untranslated_textbox.get(1.0, tk.END).strip():
                old_clipboard = new_clipboard
                if new_clipboard in translated_dict:
                    translated_text = translated_dict[new_clipboard]
                else:
                    try:
                        prompt_text = prompt_textbox.get(1.0, tk.END).strip()
                        translated_text = translate_text(new_clipboard, prompt_text)
                    except Exception as e:
                        root.after(0, create_error_window, f"Connection Error: {e}")
                        continue

                    translated_dict[new_clipboard] = translated_text

                update_display(untranslated_textbox, new_clipboard)
                update_display(translated_textbox, translated_text)

        time.sleep(1)

Chapter 1: A New Beginning

The sun was setting in the Alola region, painting vivid colors across the sky as the warm ocean breeze rustled through the palm trees. In a small, cozy home nestled on the coast, Iolana stared excitedly at the calendar pinned to the wall of her colorful bedroom. It was hard to believe that tomorrow, she would embark on her adventure as a Pokémon trainer.

“I can’t believe it’s finally happening!” she exclaimed to no one in particular, her long grey hair bouncing as she jumped up and down with excitement. As she spun around, her bright cyan eyes caught a glimpse of the framed photograph over her desk.

The photograph was of Iolana’s adoptive parents, a young couple who had found her wandering alone in Unova almost a decade earlier. They had been on their honeymoon at the time, and had no idea of the secrets hidden within the little girl they had brought home. With no one else to care for her, they had taken her in and raised her as their own. Iolana had never known her past or the atrocities that she had unwittingly committed. She had grown up with no knowledge of her connection to the mysterious “Project Infinity” and the powerful Pokémon who had played a role in her creation.

As the darkness of night fell over the beautiful island, Iolana’s adoptive father knocked gently on her bedroom door. “Iolana, are you ready for dinner?” he asked, peeking in to see her sitting on the floor, surrounded by books and magazines about Pokémon.

“Just a minute, dad,” she replied, quickly organizing the scattered papers and books as she got to her feet. She joined her parents at the dinner table, her thoughts still filled with excitement and anticipation for the journey that lay ahead of her.

Throughout dinner, Iolana’s parents listened with pride as she detailed her hopes and dreams for her future as a Pokémon trainer. They were happy to see her so passionate about the adventure that lay ahead, but deep down, they knew that she would inevitably one day learn the truth about herself, the Unova government, and the dark history that had brought her to Alola.

After dinner, Iolana’s father handed her a neatly wrapped gift. “We wanted to give you something special before you head out on your big adventure,” he said, smiling warmly. Iolana excitedly tore the wrapping paper away, revealing a brand-new Pokédex and a letter of recommendation from the local Professor. Her eyes filled with tears of joy as she hugged her parents tightly. “Thank you both so much! This means the world to me.”

As the night wore on, Iolana laid in bed, unable to fall asleep, her mind racing with thoughts of her journey ahead. She couldn’t have been more thrilled to go and capture her own Pokémon, train them, and become the best Pokémon trainer she could be.

But far away from the peaceful island, hidden in the depths of the Unova government’s secrets archives, lay the knowledge of a catastrophic project and the quiet stirrings of a forgotten ambition. An ancient power long dormant within Iolana, waiting for the right moment to once again awake, even as the unsuspecting girl lay dreaming of her future.

The next day marked the beginning of Iolana’s greatest adventure, one filled with friendship, trials, and self-discovery. But it would also be an adventure that would lead her into a dangerous world of secrets and lies, where the power of an ancient Pokémon would threaten to change everything she thought she knew about herself and the world around her.


Chapter 2: A New Companion

The morning sun shone brightly over Alola as Iolana set out on her new adventure. Excited to meet her first partner Pokémon, she hurried to the laboratory of the local Pokémon Professor.

As Iolana arrived at the lab, she found her best friend, Kai, already waiting there for her, his face beaming with enthusiasm. They had grown up together, and despite Kai’s troubled past, he had remained a cheerful, upbeat boy with an unwavering love for Pokémon.

“Hey, Iolana!” Kai called, waving energetically. “I got here early so we could choose our starter Pokémon together! I’ve been dreaming about this moment for as long as I can remember.”

Smiling, Iolana replied, “Me too, Kai! Let’s go meet our new partners!”

The two friends entered the laboratory, eager to begin their adventure together. Inside, they were greeted by the Pokémon Professor. “Ah, you must be Iolana and Kai!” the Professor said warmly. “I’m Professor Koa, and I’ve been studying Pokémon here in Alola for many years. You must be excited to begin your journey. Now, let’s see which Pokémon you’ll be starting with.”

As Iolana and Kai examined the available Pokémon, they were instantly drawn to two very different creatures: Cyndaquil, a small fire-type Pokémon, and Oshawott, an adorable water-type. They knew they had made their choices as soon as their eyes met their future partners.

Excitedly, they accepted their new Pokémon partners from Professor Koa, who then handed them each a Pokédex. “This will help you keep track of the Pokémon you encounter and learn important information about them. Good luck on your journey, and remember to always treat your Pokémon with love and respect.”

With their new partners – Oshawott for Kai, and Cyndaquil for Iolana – and their trusty Pokédexes in hand, the two friends began their journey together in high spirits.

Despite the challenges brought on by Kai’s family situation, he had held onto his boundless enthusiasm and passion for Pokémon. As Iolana and Kai embarked on their adventure, they knew they could count on each other to make this a journey they would never forget.

However, as the two trainers set out to explore the world and grow stronger with their Pokémon, the shadows of Iolana’s past and Kai’s difficult family life continued to loom over their friendship. The trials that awaited them would challenge their bond and force them to confront the dark secrets buried deep within their own lives.

But for now, Iolana and Kai had each other, and their loyal Pokémon companions would accompany them every step of the way. Together, they would face whatever challenges awaited them with courage and an unbreakable bond of friendship.


Chapter 3: The First Battle

The sun blazed over the beautiful island of Alola as Iolana and Kai began their journey as Pokémon trainers. With their new partners by their side, they agreed that the best way to start their adventure was to test themselves in a friendly Pokémon battle. The excitement in the air was palpable as they found a suitable spot on the soft sands of a nearby beach.

“Alright, Iolana,” said Kai, his eyes filled with determination as he looked at his best friend. “Let’s promise that no matter what happens, we’ll help each other to grow stronger and become great Pokémon trainers.”

Iolana smiled, her eyes shimmering with confidence. “Absolutely, Kai! Let’s do this!”

With a nod of agreement, they took their positions on the sandy battleground. “Go, Cyndaquil!” Iolana shouted, releasing her new partner Pokémon from its Pokéball.

In response, Kai threw his Pokéball into the air, calling out his beloved Oshawott. “Alright, Oshawott, it’s showtime!”

As their Pokémon faced each other, Iolana and Kai couldn’t help but feel a sense of pride in their new partnership. “Cyndaquil, use Tackle!” Iolana commanded, watching as her Pokémon charged toward its opponent.

Kai quickly countered, “Oshawott, dodge and use Water Gun!” Oshawott swiftly sidestepped Cyndaquil’s attack and unleashed a powerful stream of water from its mouth, hitting the fire-type Pokémon with surprising accuracy.

The battle continued, with both trainers giving their all as they directed their Pokémon’s movements. With each attack, dodge, and block, Iolana and Kai learned more about their partners’ strengths, weaknesses, and potential.

After a heated exchange of moves, the young trainers eventually called a truce, realizing that their Pokémon were exhausted from their first battle. However, both Iolana and Kai had grown stronger through the experience, and they were more determined than ever to help each other grow.

As they tended to their weary Pokémon, Iolana looked at Kai and said, “We promised that we’d help each other become great trainers, and I know we’ll keep that promise. I’m so glad I have you beside me on this journey.”

Kai smiled back at her. “I feel the same way, Iolana. With you and our Pokémon, I know we’ll face any challenge that comes our way.”

As the friends continued their adventure, they were reminded of their unwavering commitment to each other and their Pokémon. Together, they would face countless battles, difficult trials, and the shadows of their own pasts.

But through it all, Iolana and Kai knew that no matter what obstacles they encountered, they could count on their friendship, and the love and support of their Pokémon, to see them through.


Chapter 4: A Bittersweet Moment

As Iolana and Kai ventured deeper into the Alolan landscape, they found themselves wandering through lush forests and exploring winding trails, all while strengthening their bond with each other and their Pokémon.

During a break in their journey, the two friends found themselves sitting down below the shade of a tall, leafy tree. They began a heartfelt conversation about their dreams, their pasts, and how much their friendship meant to each of them.

“I can’t imagine doing this without you, Iolana,” Kai confessed as they watched their Pokémon play nearby. “There have been so many times in my life where I felt completely alone, but having you by my side has made all the difference.”

Iolana’s heart ached for her friend, knowing all too well the pain of losing loved ones and the turmoil it had caused in his life. “We’ve always been there for each other, Kai. You’re like family to me, and I know that we can face anything together.”

The friends shared a quiet, comforting moment as they basked in the warmth of their bond. However, their peace was suddenly interrupted by the sound of Kai’s PokéGear ringing. He glanced at the screen and hesitated before answering the call.

“Hey, Dad,” Kai greeted tentatively, trying his best to sound cheerful. He listened intently for a moment before his face fell, disappointment creeping into his voice. “Oh, you can’t come home today? I understand… No, it’s alright. Don’t worry about me. I’ll see you soon.”

As he hung up the call, Kai bit his lip, struggling to contain his disappointment. He had been looking forward to showing his father his new partner, Oshawott, and sharing his excitement about his new journey as a Pokémon trainer.

Iolana looked at Kai with concern, sensing that their conversation had not gone well. “Is everything okay, Kai?” she asked gently.

With a shaky breath, Kai forced a smile onto his face, trying to mask his hurt. “Yeah, it’s fine. I was just hoping that my dad could see Oshawott, but he’s really busy with work at the Aether Foundation. It’s not a big deal.”

Iolana squeezed her friend’s hand reassuringly. “I’m sure your dad is proud of you, Kai. He just has a lot on his plate right now. And don’t forget, you’ve got me right here to share all of your journey’s best moments with.”

Kai’s eyes shimmered with gratitude as he genuinely smiled back at Iolana. “You’re right, Iolana. Thanks for always being there for me.”

As their journey continued, Iolana and Kai would find that their greatest strength lay not only in their Pokémon partners but also in the depth of their friendship and their unwavering support for one another. Amid the challenges of battles, the shadows of their pasts, and the unexpected trials that awaited them, they knew they could face anything, as long as they did it together.


Chapter 5: A Hidden Connection

Meanwhile, in the bustling city of Castelia, in the Unova region, a talented young journalist named Aelfrun fought to make a name for herself among the competitive throngs of the metropolis. With her keen intuition and ambition, Aelfrun had managed to carve out a fulfilling life for herself, albeit one weighed down by tragic memories and a haunting past.

Aelfrun’s parents had been brilliant scientists, working on something they referred to as “Project Infinity.” But beneath the mysterious name, they had never revealed the true nature of their work to their young daughter. When she was a baby, Aelfrun had suffered from a life-threatening heart condition, Tetralogy of Fallot. Desperate to save their daughter’s life, her parents had infused her with the very same experimental serum they had been studying—cells from the legendary Pokémon, Xerneas.

This dangerous procedure had left Aelfrun with not only a healthy heart but also the latent power of the Mythical Pokémon. What her parents never expected, however, was the unanticipated consequences of their experiment: Project Infinity had sown seeds of great tragedy in the lives of everyone it touched.

As a child, Aelfrun had dreamed of becoming a Pokémon Master. At the age of twelve, she had even managed to collect all of the elusive badges from Pokémon gyms throughout the Unova region. But when Aelfrun returned home from her journey, she was greeted with heartbreak. Her parents had perished in a devastating accident at the Research and Incubation Facility 06—the very same incident that had brought chaos to Iolana’s life.

The loss of her parents shattered Aelfrun’s dreams of becoming a Pokémon Master, and she had eventually turned her focus to journalism in an attempt to heal her grief-stricken heart. Yet, unknowingly, her life remained entangled with the remnants of Project Infinity and the fate of Iolana.

As Aelfrun continued her work, she began to uncover hints of secrets hidden within the Unovan government. Something drew her to investigate the matter further, a deep-rooted intuition that these secrets would lead her to the truth about the tragic fate of her parents and herself. Little did Aelfrun know that her investigation would connect her to Iolana’s past and uncover the buried connection between them—their shared legacy as the unwitting children of Project Infinity.

Through their shared journey towards self-discovery, Iolana, Kai, and Aelfrun would soon find themselves entwined in a powerful tale of destiny, friendship, and the ultimate courage required to embrace their true potential.

As they delved deeper into the mysteries of life, choices would have to be made, sacrifices faced, and only the most indomitable bond of friendship would see them through the challenges that lay ahead.


Chapter 6: Encountering the Unknown

The hustle and bustle of Castelia city never seemed to slow down, even as the evening sun dipped below the towering skyscrapers. Aelfrun’s life as a newspaper reporter in Unova’s largest metropolis left her little time to ponder the mysteries of her past or the suspicions lingering in her mind about Project Infinity.

One autumn evening, Aelfrun found herself sitting at her desk in the cramped office she shared with other ambitious reporters, sifting through a stack of stories and leads in search of something compelling to investigate. As she combed through the articles, one particular headline caught her eye: “UFO Sighting Reported in District 54—A Mysterious Connection?”

Intrigued, Aelfrun began to read the eyewitness accounts of strange lights seen streaking across the sky in the remote outskirts of Castelia. Those who had witnessed the phenomenon whispered rumors of government cover-ups, secret experiments, and “visitors” from other worlds. This was a far cry from Aelfrun’s usual investigative subjects, but something about the story spoke to her, resonating with a buried instinct she couldn’t quite grasp.

Following her intuition, she decided to investigate the UFO sightings and the urban legend surrounding District 54. Perhaps it would lead her to some answers about the elusive Project Infinity and the mysterious events that had upturned so many lives.

Over the following weeks, Aelfrun delved deeper into the tale of District 54, tirelessly scanning old newspapers, scouring local archives, and conducting interviews with those who claimed to have seen the mysterious lights. Slowly, she began to piece together a fragmented puzzle that hinted at hidden truths and long-forgotten connections.

As she delved further, she discovered that District 54 had once been home to a government research facility, which had been destroyed in a catastrophe—an event that had marked the end of Project Infinity. Aelfrun couldn’t help but feel a prickle run down her spine at the realization that this shared past with Iolana was no coincidence, and that their fates were somehow inextricably linked.

Unbeknownst to her, the threads of Aelfrun’s investigation were reaching across the seas to the Alolan region, weaving an intricate web that would soon entangle the lives of Iolana, Kai, and herself in a tumultuous tapestry of secrets, friendship, and an epic exploration of life’s true essence.

As Aelfrun continued to unravel the mysteries of District 54, she found herself drawn closer and closer to the truth about her own origins, the powerful bond that connected her with Iolana, and the destiny that awaited them both. Yet, as Aelfrun would soon learn, the path to clarity is seldom a straight one, and only the most steadfast hearts can navigate the treacherous turns that lay ahead.


Chapter 7: The Heart of Darkness

Aelfrun’s search for the hidden truths of District 54 brought her to the remains of Research and Incubation Facility 06. The abandoned structure loomed ominously, a haunting reminder of the tragedy and devastation that had occurred within its walls. Undeterred, Aelfrun ventured through the crumbling corridors, her heart pounding with each step further into the darkness.

The air was thick with an eerie silence, pierced only by Aelfrun’s ragged breath and anxious footfalls. As she delved deeper into the heart of the facility, Aelfrun stumbled upon a hidden chamber, its entrance concealed by the ruins and debris. Overcoming her overwhelming anxiety, Aelfrun approached the chamber cautiously.

Inside, she found it—the blood-red glowing feather, a symbol of power, despair, and a long-buried secret. As she reached out to touch it, an immense energy surged into her being, consuming her vision with searing images of the tragic day when both Project Infinity and her parents had met their tragic end.

In that moment of chaos and pain, Aelfrun’s connection to Iolana and the experiment that had bound them since childhood grew ever stronger. The intensity of the energy seared her skin, and she instinctively dropped the feather, gasping for breath amid the swirling shadows of her memories. Every fiber of her being ached to take the feather from this forsaken place and bring it to Iolana, but fate had other plans.

As Aelfrun turned her back on the dark chamber and the secrets that still lingered within, she was met with a sudden and violent force. The shock of the taser stunned her, and she crumpled to the ground, the pain washing over her like a crashing wave.

As the agents of the Ministry of Defence descended upon her, Aelfrun’s heart raced—filled with dread and determination. Her mind swam with questions and the enormity of what she had just discovered, but one thing was clear: she would not allow the feather or the secrets hidden within District 54 to remain hidden.

As the black hood descended over Aelfrun’s face, her fate intertwined ever more closely with that of Iolana and Kai. The trials to come would test their strength, courage, and their unshakeable bond, but together they would uncover the ultimate truth behind the mysteries that had shaped their lives—and perhaps, through their shared destiny, break the curse that had haunted them since the day their hearts had first begun to beat.


Chapter 8: Interrogation and Revelation

Aelfrun’s body ached with the intensity of the shock as she was hauled to her feet by her captors. She could feel the beads of sweat forming on her brow, her breathing shallow and labored under the black hood covering her face. Her mind raced with questions and fear but, beneath it all, a spark of indignant anger stoked a fire within her heart.

The agents of the Ministry of Defence guided Aelfrun through the winding facility, dragging her into a sterile, windowless room. As they forcibly seated her in a cold metal chair, the cacophony of their footsteps reverberated on the solid concrete floor. The slap of a floodlight snapping on pierced the darkness, even as her eyes remained shrouded under the hood.

A booming voice echoed throughout the room, “Miss Aelfrun Wyndsor, do you know where you are?” The smooth, abrasive tone put her on edge instantly.

For a brief moment, Aelfrun hesitated and considered remaining silent. But ultimately, she knew that to understand the depths of the mysteries that had plagued her entire life, she needed to respond. Her voice, defiant but trembling ever so slightly, responded, “Yes.”

A cruel laugh reverberated throughout the room. “You’ve come a long way in search of secrets, Miss Wyndsor,” the voice taunted. “But you’ve ventured too far and now find yourself imprisoned by those you sought to expose.”

The agents roughly lifted the hood from Aelfrun’s face. As her eyes struggled to adjust to the sudden influx of light, she squinted at the man before her—a tall man with balding gray hair, wearing a crisp military uniform. His eyes held an unsettling mixture of amusement and malice.

“You must know that digging into the Ministry’s buried secrets will only lead to pain and suffering,” he continued calmly. “So, tell me, Miss Wyndsor, what would you hope to gain by delving into the past?”

Aelfrun’s eyes flashed with determination, her voice unwavering as she responded, “Answers. I deserve to know the truth about Project Infinity, the research my parents were involved in, and how it has shaped my own existence. This tragedy has haunted me for years, and I am tired of living in the shadow of unanswered questions.”

The man smiled menacingly, leaning in closer to Aelfrun’s face. “Perhaps,” he said, his voice dripping with malice, “your questions are better left unanswered, Miss Wyndsor. The truth can sometimes be more devastating than the lies we cling to.”

For a moment, Aelfrun considered the possibility that he could be right, that the truth might be a burden she could not bear. But as she thought of Iolana and the journey that had led her to this moment, Aelfrun knew that she couldn’t allow the past to remain hidden any longer.

“I will face the truth, whatever it may be,” Aelfrun declared, her resolve unwavering. “I owe it to myself and everyone affected by Project Infinity to uncover the reality of what transpired and the secrets it still holds.”

The agent’s smile widened, a sinister gleam in his eyes. “Very well,” he conceded. “Let us see if you are truly prepared to face the darkness that has shaped your life.”

In the hours that followed, Aelfrun would face her deepest fears and most haunting memories, even as Kai and Iolana journeyed ever closer to the destiny that awaited them. The time for revelations had come, and the intertwining destinies of these three friends would finally collide in an epic struggle for truth, understanding, and redemption.


Chapter 9: The Chosen One

The cold steel of the metal chair chilled Aelfrun to the bone as they continued on with the interrogation. As the questions grew harsher, the chilling nature of the secret files laid bare before her, she began to realize she had stumbled upon far more than she bargained for. The Ministry of Defence’s involvement with Project Infinity went beyond the realm of anything she could have imagined.

The agent, leaning back against the wall, scrutinized Aelfrun with a penetrating gaze. “Do you want to know why you’re still alive, Miss Wyndsor?” he asked, his voice menacingly calm. “We’ve been monitoring your progress for a while now. Ever since you first touched the feather, we knew you were different. Others who have come into contact with it have either died from the energy radiation or have descended into madness from the hallucinations.”

Aelfrun shuddered at the words as a cold sense of dread crept over her. She had survived where others had perished, yet now found herself ensnared in the most dangerous web she could have imagined.

The agent continued, the smile never leaving his lips. “You see, Miss Wyndsor, you are the only one chosen by the feather. Your unique connection to it has protected you, and this makes you… valuable.”

He paused, allowing the weight of his revelation to sink in, and then added, “Our director would like to extend to you an offer—an opportunity to join our ranks and become an agent of the DHS Special Office of Project Infinity.”

Aelfrun’s heart pounded as she considered the appalling proposition. Was it possible she was meant to play a role in the very project that had shaped her life and haunted her existence? Could she face the darkness that it represented?

Yet, she knew that she needed to understand more about what had happened to her, and perhaps by joining the DHS, she would be able to gather their hidden knowledge and learn the truth she had been so desperately seeking.

With a defiant glint in her eyes, she nodded in agreement. “I will join you. I need to know the truth about my past and the real reason behind Project Infinity, no matter the cost.”

The agent’s smirk widened, and he extended a hand to help her up, his grip icy and unwavering. As Aelfrun rose to her feet, she knew that the path she had chosen would be fraught with danger and deception, and that her journey had only just begun.

Chapter 10: Dark Alliances

It was not long before Aelfrun found herself thrust into the secretive world of the DHS, working alongside the very people responsible for the tragedy that had befallen her family. As she delved deeper into the world of Project Infinity, she began to uncover secrets, lies, and the depths of the corruption within the Ministry of Defence.

Meanwhile, with each passing day, the thread connecting Aelfrun to Iolana and Kia grew stronger, causing a sense of foreboding to consume her. It was as if their fates were tied together, anchored by the very evil she was working against, and ultimately destined to clash in a battle for the truth.

Through her newfound position within the DHS, Aelfrun used the knowledge and resources at her disposal to continue her search for answers, and with each piece of the puzzle that fell into place, she felt a glimmer of hope for herself and her friends.

As the days turned to weeks, and the weeks to months, Aelfrun’s skills as an agent grew more refined, her determination unwavering as she pursued her ultimate goal. Despite the darkness that surrounded her, she clung desperately to the hope that one day, she would uncover the truth they had fought so hard to hide, and finally bring light to the lives of those who had suffered for so long.

But with each step closer to the truth, Aelfrun began to realize that the intertwined destinies of herself, Iolana, and Kai could no longer be ignored. Soon, they would face the depths of their own pasts and confront the secrets that had shaped their lives, and only the devotion of their friendship and the strength of their will could guide them through the darkness that awaited them in the heart of the Ministry’s web.

On Disk IO, Part 3: LSM Trees
If you like the series, check out my book on Database Internals!

New series on Distributed System concepts in Databases can be found here.

In the first and second parts, we’ve discussed underlying Operating System mechanisms that help to perform writes on disk. Now, it’s time to start moving towards higher level concepts.

Today we’re going to explore one of the types of storage often used in the databases. Each of them have their own advantages and disadvantages, so building a database system is always about trade-offs and we’ll try to address some of those as well.

Mutable vs Immutable data structures
One of the differences between the data structures we’re going to be discussing over the next chapters is mutability (or lack of thereof). Here, we’ll be talking about the mutability on disk, so semantics of construction and functional programming related concepts aren’t relevant for the purpose of our discussions.

The obvious advantage of the immutable data structures is that the storage overhead can be minimized: we do not have to reserve any extra space for data that is going to be inserted later or for the cases when the updated records require more space than the originally written ones.

Keeping the data structure immutable favors the sequential writes: data is written on disk in a single pass, append-only. The mutable data structure will be pre-allocated in a single pass, but the subsequent writes will be random. Some structures require node splitting, that will relocate already written parts. After some time, randomly written file might require defragmentation.

Some databases, instead of doing in-place updates, just mark the outdated record as “deleted” (so that it will eventually be garbage-collected) and append new records to the specially designated update area of the file. While this is a nice compromise, sometimes writes fill up all the designated space and overflow areas have to be created. All of this might slow down both subsequent reads and writes.

Another advantage of immutable files is that data can be read from the disk without any segment locking between operations, which significantly simplifies concurrent access. In contrast, mutable data structures employ hierarchical locks and latches in order to ensure on disk data structure integrity, allow multiple readers at the same time but give exclusive ownership for parts of tree to writers.

Both mutable and immutable data structures require some housekeeping in order to optimize performance but for different reasons. Since amount of allocated files constantly grows, immutable data structures have to merge and rewrite files in order to make sure that the least amount of files is hit during the query, as the requested record might be spread across multiple files. On the other hand, mutable files may have to be rewritten partially or completely to decrease fragmentation, merge overflow areas and reclaim space occupied by updated or deleted records (as their new contents were written elsewhere). Of course, the exact scope of work done by the housekeeping process heavily depends on the concrete implementation.

We’ll be discussing the examples of mutable and immutable storage when looking at LSM-Trees (Log Structured Merge Trees) and B-Tree variants.

LSM Trees
Let’s start with the Log Structured Merge Trees, as the concept is quite straightforward. The seminal LSM paper suggests an implementation of the disk resident trees similar to the B-Tree, with the difference that it’s optimized for sequential disk access and the nodes can have a full occupancy (we’ll discuss the occupancy in more details when discussing B-Trees). In this light, it’s worth mentioning that, even though LSM Trees are often contrasted with B-Trees, this comparison is not strictly accurate, my highlight here is that LSM Trees allow immutable, mergeable files and the nature of the primary index for the table is a matter of implementation (therefore, even B-Trees can be used as index data structure here).

Stating that something is implemented as an LSM Tree doesn’t necessarily say anything about the lookup complexity, or even the internal file layout, only about the conceptual structure. However, it’s worth to point out that many modern LSM implementations in the database field have something in common: Sorted String Tables.

Sorted String Tables
The advantage of the Sorted String Tables is their simplicity: they are easy to write, search and read. SSTables are a persistent ordered immutable map from keys to values, where both keys and values are arbitrary byte strings. They have some nice properties like, for example, the random point-queries (i.e. finding a value by key) can be done quickly by looking up the primary index, sequential scans (i.e. iterating over all key/value pairs in a specified key range) can be done efficiently by just reading the records one after the other.

Usually the SSTable has two parts: index and data blocks. Data block consists from the key/value pairs concatenated one after another. The index block contains primary keys and offsets, pointing to the offset in the data block where the actual record can be found. Primary index can be implemented using a format optimised for quick searches, like a B-Tree, for example.


SSTable is a persistent ordered immutable data structures. It usually consist of Index and Data blocks, where index block can be represented by a quick lookup data structure, holding offsets to the values in the Data block; the Data block holds concatenated key/value pairs, enabling fast sequential range scans.
Since SSTable is immutable, insert, update or delete operations would require rewriting the whole file, since it’s optimised for reads, written sequentially and has no reserved empty space that would allow any in-place modifications. That’s where the LSM Trees come into play.

Anatomy
In LSM Trees, all the writes are performed against the mutable in-memory data structure (once again, often implemented using a data structure allowing logarithmic time lookup, such as a B-Tree or a SkipList). Whenever the size of the tree reaches a certain threshold (or after some predefined time period elapses, whichever comes first), we write the data the disk, creating a new SSTable. This process is sometimes called “flush”. Retrieving the data may require searching all SSTables on disk, checking the in-memory table and merging their contents together before returning the result.


Structure of an LSM Tree: a memory-resident table, used for writes. Whenever the memory table is large enough, it’s sorted contents are written on disk, becoming an SSTable. Reads are served, hitting all SSTables and the memory-resident table, requiring a merge process to reconcile the data.
The merge step during the read is required, since the data can be split in several parts (for example, an insert followed by delete operation, where delete would shadow the originally inserted record; or an insert, followed by the update operation, where a new field is added to the record).

Every data item in SSTable has a timestamp associated with it. For inserts it specifies the write time, for updates — an update time and removal time for deletes.

Compaction
Because amount of disk-resident table keeps growing, data for the key located in several files, multiple versions of the same record, redundant records that got shadowed by deletes), and the reads will get more and more expensive over the time. In order to avoid that, LSM Trees require a process that would read complete SSTables from disk and perform the merge, similar to one that we have to do during the read. This process is sometimes called compaction. Because of the SSTable layout, this operation is very efficient: records are read from several sources in sequential manner and can be appended to the output file right away, since all the inputs are sorted and merged, the resulting file will have the same property. Constructing an index file might be a more expensive operation (in terms of complexity).


Compaction takes multiple SSTables and merges them into one. Some database systems logically group the tables of the same size to the same “level” and starts the merge process whenever there are enough tables on the certain level.
Merge
There are two things important to discuss in terms of merge: complexity guarantees and shadowing logic.


In terms of complexity, merging SSTables is same as merging sorted collections. It has O(M) memory overhead, where M is amount of SSTables being merged. Sorted collection of heads of iterators over each SSTable is maintained (log(n)). On each step, minimum item is taken from sorted collection and re-filled from corresponding iterator.

Same merge process is used during read operations and during compaction process. During compaction, sequential source SSTable reads and sequential target SSTable write help to maintain nice performance guarantees.

Shadowing is necessary to ensure that the updates and deletes work: deletes in LSM Tree insert placeholders, specifying which key was marked for deletion. Similarly, an update is just a record with a bigger timestamp. During the read, the records that get shadowed by deletes will not returned to the client. The same thing happens with the updates: out of the two records with the same key, the one with the later timestamp is returned.


Merge step reconciles the data stored in separated tables for the same key: the record for Alex here was written with timestamp 100 and updated with a new phone and timestamp 200; the record for John got deleted. The other two entries are taken as-is as they’re not shadowed.
Summing Up
Using immutable data structures can often simplify the work of programmer. When using immutable on-disk structures, you trade the need to occasionally merge your tables for better space management (by avoiding overflow pages and boosting space occupancy to 100%), concurrency (because readers and writers are never compete over the same file, therefore requiring no mutual exclusion) and potentially simpler implementations. LSM Tree databases are typically write-optimized, since all the writes are performed against the write-ahead log (for durability and fail over) and memory resident tables. Reads are usually slower, because of the merge process and a need to check multiple files on disk.

Because of the maintenance, LSM-Trees might result into worse latency, since both CPU and IO bandwidth is spent re-reading and merging tables instead of just serving reads and writes. It’s also possible, under a write-heavy workload, to saturate IO by just writes and flushes, stalling the compaction process. Lagging compaction results into slower reads, increasing CPU and IO pressure, making the matters worse. This is something to watch out for.

LSM-Trees cause some write amplification: data has to be written to the write-ahead log, then flushed on disk, where it will be eventually re-read and written again during the compaction process. That said, mutable B-Tree structures also suffer from write amplification, so I’d prefer to leave the cost analysis until after we discuss B-Trees and a conjecture that helps understanding that we are just trading read performance against write performance and memory overhead.

Many databases use SSTables: RocksDB and Cassandra, just to name a few, but there are plenty other examples. Cassandra, starting with version 3.4, incorporated SSTable Attached Secondary Indexes, a concept built on top SSTables and LSM Trees, that simplifies the secondary index maintenance by coupling the index building to memory-resident table flush and SSTable merge process.

In the next post, we’ll discuss several B-Tree variants and will move to the access patterns and the previously promised parts after that.

summarise this: Geographically, names based on Hermanubis are attested in papyri from various areas of Egypt, but it is important to separate, where possible, the provenance of the papyrus from that of the individuals named therein. When this is done, most of the individuals bearing Hermanubis names for whom we can establish an origin turn out to be Alexandrian or Cynopolite (three instances each). This concentration again coincides with the documentary and archaeological evidence for the god Hermanubis. Thus, in the precinct of the Alexandrian Serapeum an inscription of uncertain date was found (SB I 3482) dedicated to "Hermanubis, great god, who listens to prayers and grants favours" (Ἑρμανούβιδι θεῶι μεγάλωι | ἐπηκόωι καὶ εὐχαρίϲτωι).[18] Not far from this dedication a statuette of the anthropomorphic "Hermanubis" was discovered.[19] It is in a suburb of Alexandria, in the sanctuary of Ras el-Soda, that the most famous statue of "Hermanubis" was found (LIMC n. 14). One of the Alexandrian coins representing the god shows him standing inside a temple (LIMC nos. 5a, b), which suggests the existence of such an edifice in the city, probably in the Serapeum precinct given the provenance of the just-mentioned dedication and statuette from there and the frequent iconographical associations between "Hermanubis" and Sarapis in Alexandrian coins.[20] Alexandria, then, clearly emerges as the cult center and probably the birthplace of the fully anthropomorphic Hermanubis, which is consonant with the three Alexandrians bearing his name (5, 6, 9).[21] For the Cynopolite nome, the chief centre of the Egyptian god Anubis, the onomastic evidence is more suggestive than the archaeological and the iconographical. Among the three individuals with a name based on Hermanubis (11, 15, 16), there stands out the father of an underage priest of "Anubis, Leto and associated gods" in the Cynopolite village of Laura (15). Since priesthood was usually hereditary, the father Hermanubis was probably himself a priest of these gods. This onomastic connection of Hermanubis with the Cynopolite nome also has an echo in the iconography: one or two Alexandrian coins personify the Cynopolite nome with a representation of the anthropomorphic "Hermanubis" on their reverse (LIMC nos. 3, 22).

Introducing the Glaciwild Region!

Geography:
The Glaciwild Region is centered around the Antarctic Peninsula, stretching from the tropical seas in the north to the frozen tundra further south. Due to unique weather patterns and geographical features, this region has a surprising variety of climates and biomes. It is divided into four main areas:

1. Tropical Strand: A warm, tropical area with beautiful sandy beaches, lush jungles, and crystal-clear waters. This area is home to a variety of water, grass, and bug-type Pokémon, as well as rare regional variants of ice-type Pokémon that have adapted to the hot climate.

2. Boreon Peaks: A mountainous region with chilly weather, breathtaking sights, and rocky terrain. Cave systems and tall peaks make this area a playground for rock, ground, and ice-type Pokémon.

3. Frostvale: A snow-covered valley and the heart of Glaciwild Region civilization. The bustling Frostvale City is the region’s main hub, where trainers can find the Pokémon League, Grand Research Lab, Battle Tower, and an array of festivals and cultural events.

4. Tundraglade: The southernmost and coldest region in Glaciwild, Tundraglade is a vast frozen wasteland with powerful blizzards, glaciers, and ice floes. This area is home to many powerful, rare ice-type Pokémon that can only be found in these extreme conditions.

Culture and History:
Glaciwild Region was initially colonized by people from all over the Pokémon world, making it an incredibly diverse and vibrant culture. This fusion of cultures is seen in everything from the regional cuisine to its architecture and even its Pokémon Trainer styles. Many ancient ruins are scattered throughout the region and are filled with mysteries about the past of Glaciwild.

Pokémon League and Gyms:
The Glaciwild Pokémon League consists of eight gyms, each with a focus on a different Pokémon type. These gym leaders are experts of their respective types and have challenged trainers from all over the region for years:

1. Marina (Water)
2. Oliver (Grass)
3. Ember (Fire)
4. Sylvia (Psychic)
5. Rex (Rock)
6. Skadi (Ice)
7. Finn (Flying)
8. Luna (Dark)

After defeating each gym leader, trainers will face the Elite Four, which consists of champions (each with a mixed team of Pokémon) and the Pokémon Champion. This final challenge will determine who can claim the title of Glaciwild’s Pokémon Master!

Unique Pokémon:
Glaciwild Region is home to over 50 unique Pokémon that cannot be found in any other region. Many of these Pokémon have adapted to the cold and harsh environment of the Antarctic Peninsula, while others have evolved to thrive in the region’s warmer climates. Some examples include:

1. Frostfawn (Ice/Fairy)
2. Tropicseal (Water/Ice)
3. Terracold (Ground/Ice)
4. Solargale (Fire/Flying)

In conclusion, the Glaciwild Region is a unique Antarctic region that offers a rich variety of cultures and biomes alongside a challenging Pokémon League and unique Pokémon to discover. Trainers far and wide are welcome to explore everything this one-of-a-kind region has to offer!


Welcome to the Glaciwild Region! Here is a guide to its various areas, roads, and towns:

1. Sunreach Port (Starting Point): A gateway town standing at the northernmost part of the Tropical Strand. The warm climate and picturesque beaches welcome trainers to the region. Home to the Beginner’s School for budding trainers and Professor Maple’s Pokémon Lab.

2. Route 1: A scenic coastal route connecting Sunreach Port to Alcove Town. Home to many grass and water-type Pokémon, trainers often begin their journey here.

3. Alcove Town (First Gym): A peaceful, coastal village surrounded by lush jungles. Gym Leader Marina’s Water-type Gym can be found here. Alcove Town is also famous for its delicious tropical fruit gardens.

4. Route 2: A dense jungle path leading to the Boreon Peaks. Ground, bug, and grass-type Pokémon inhabit this area.

5. Peakside City (Second Gym): A bustling city at the base of the Boreon Peaks. The Grass-type Gym, led by Oliver, awaits trainers alongside the Cable Car station to reach Mount Glaciwild.

6. Route 3: A rocky mountainside pathway to the peak of Mount Glaciwild. Features a mix of rock, ground, and ice-type Pokémon.

7. Mount Glaciwild: The summit of the Boreon Peaks houses an ancient ruin that holds clues to the region’s mysterious past. Trainers may encounter legendary Pokémon atop this peak.

8. Route 4: A thrilling downhill course where trainers can reach Frostvale City. Flying-type Pokémon are common in this area.

9. Frostvale City (Third and Fourth Gyms): The largest city in the Glaciwild Region and the Pokémon League headquarters. Fire-type Gym Leader Ember and Psychic-type Gym Leader Sylvia test trainers’ skills here. Frostvale City is also known for its shopping district and hall of research.

10. Route 5: A snowy, forested pathway filled with ice-type Pokémon. It connects Frostvale City to Aurora Village.

11. Aurora Village (Fifth Gym): A small, charming village famous for its dazzling auroras that light up the night sky. Rock-type Gym Leader Rex resides here. This village is also a hotspot for specialized ice-type move tutors.

12. Route 6: An icy, slippery path that takes trainers across frozen rivers and lakes. Water and ice-type Pokémon frequent this route.

13. Polarstone Town (Sixth Gym): A mining town renowned for its gemstones and ice-sculptures. Skadi, the Ice-type Gym Leader, embraces the town’s history by designing an ice-themed gym.

14. Route 7: A frigid, snow-covered route with powerful blizzards encompassing the path to Tundraglade. Trainers will encounter both rare and powerful ice-type Pokémon here.

15. Glacierway Village: A remote village in Tundraglade, known for its ice fishing and close-knit community. No gym here, but a helpful NPC teaches trainers how to navigate blizzards.

16. Route 8: An expansive frozen wasteland blanketed in snowstorms; trainers may have to traverse icy caves and glaciers here while battling aggressive ice and dark-type Pokémon.

17. Aurora Gate City (Seventh Gym): Located in the southernmost part of the Glaciwild Region, this city is built around an imposing frozen fortress. Gym Leader Finn, with his Flying-type Pokémon, battles inside the fortress’ wind tunnels.

18. Route 9: A dark, frostbitten path filled with ghost and dark-type Pokémon. It connects Aurora Gate City to the final gym destination.

19. Eclipse City (Eighth Gym): A Gothic-inspired city shrouded in darkness from the surrounding cliffs. Dark-type Gym Leader Luna challenges trainers under the city’s moonlit sky. Trainers who seek to challenge the Champion must pass through the ominous Dark Woods before the Pokémon League.

20. Glaciwild Pokémon League: Located atop a majestic plateau, the final challenge awaits. Upon defeating the Elite Four and Pokémon Champion, trainers will carve their names into the annals of the Glaciwild Region’s history.

With a variety of towns, cities, and routes to experience, the Glaciwild Region attracts trainers seeking adventure, challenges, and camaraderie! Good luck on your journey!


The Twilight Zone: The Final Frontier of Glaciwild Region

Geography and Climate:
The Twilight Zone is a vast, mysterious, and treacherous area that stretches beyond the Aurora Gate City, dominating more than 50% of the Glaciwild Region. It is unlike any other place in the Pokémon world, as it is an uncharted, forbidden territory within the Antarctic ice cap. This region is characterized by its harsh weather conditions, perpetually shifting ice formations, and an eerie twilight glow that perpetually shrouds the landscape.

The climate of the Twilight Zone is extremely cold and unforgiving, with blizzards that can emerge without warning, whiteout conditions, and jagged ice crevasses waiting to catch the unwary. Only the bravest and most skilled trainers dare to venture into this ominous land to uncover its hidden secrets and face the powerful Pokémon that call this place home.

Notable Locations:

1. Glimmering Caves: A sprawling network of ice crystal-lined caves often illuminated by the ethereal light of the twilight. Some parts of the cave have warmer areas that harbor unique Pokémon species not found anywhere else.

2. Permafrost Pillars: A surreal, otherworldly forest of towering ice formations that can reach staggering heights. This area is known for its powerful ice-type Pokémon and the icy mazes created by the natural pillars.

3. Shadow’s Basin: A vast, frozen lake covered in a perpetual fog, teeming with rare water and ice-type Pokémon. It is said that ancient Pokémon legends lie hidden beneath the ice.

4. Frostsunder Mountains: A daunting mountain range with peaks of pure ice, home to numerous flying-type Pokémon that thrive in the high-altitude, frigid winds. The mountains also house a hidden observatory where trainers can learn about legendary Pokémon sightings.

5. Aurora Citadel: Shrouded in mystery, this ancient, ice-encased fortress is said to be the resting place of a powerful legendary Pokémon. Trainers seeking answers must solve intricate puzzles and prove their worth in strength and courage.

Pokémon Encounters:
The Twilight Zone is home to rare, powerful, and even never-before-seen Pokémon species. Many of these Pokémon have adapted to the extreme cold and constant twilight conditions. Examples include:

1. Shadowfrost (Ice/Dark)
2. Glaciark (Ice/Steel)
3. Ethereon (Ice/Ghost)
4. Frostcarnyx (Ice/Dragon)

Challenges:
The Twilight Zone is not for the faint of heart. With its dangerous weather conditions, powerful wild Pokémon, and treacherous landscapes, only the strongest trainers will be able to survive here. Players who dare to venture into the Twilight Zone must be prepared for potential hazards, including:

1. Blizzard: Trainers must navigate through whiteout conditions that can reduce visibility to almost zero, and which can lead to getting lost or falling into hidden crevasses.
2. Ice puzzles: Challenging ice slide puzzles and frozen mazes to test trainers’ wits and navigation skills.
3. Wild Pokémon battles: Players will face powerful wild Pokémon, many of which are unique to the Twilight Zone and much stronger than those found in other regions.
4. Legendary Pokémon encounters: Multiple side quests and challenges await trainers who wish to uncover the hidden legendary Pokémon and uncover their connection to the Twilight Zone.

The Twilight Zone is the ultimate test for trainers in the Glaciwild Region. Conquer the elements, navigate the treacherous terrain, and discover the secrets of this awe-inspiring and enigmatic corner of the Pokémon world.


Introducing the Mythical Pokémon, Glaciesire!

Name: Glaciesire (Glacier + Sire)
Type: Ice/Psychic
Classification: Banished Apostle Pokémon
Ability: Frozen Aura (Increases the power of Ice and Psychic-type moves once below 50% HP.)

Pokédex Information:
Glaciesire was once widely regarded as the most powerful among the Lake Guardians. As the embodiment of desires, Glaciesire had a profound impact on the human world, granting people the beauty of dreams, aspirations, and passions. Some even believe that this mighty Pokémon helped shape human civilization as we know it.

However, the powerful force of desires can be a double-edged sword, leading people towards temptation, greed, and destruction. When Glaciesire aligned itself with Giratina, the god of the Distortion World, Arceus saw this as an act of defiance and banished Glaciesire from the Lake Guardians’ ranks.

Upon its banishment, Glaciesire withdrew to the isolated and desolate reaches of the Glaciwild Region’s Twilight Zone. Within the cold embrace of Shadow’s Basin, this powerful Pokémon found solace in the frozen lake, seemingly locked away from the world forever.

Lore:
Whispers of the Banished Apostle, Glaciesire, have fascinated trainers in the Glaciwild Region for generations. According to legend, Glaciesire maintains its formidable power and awaits the arrival of a worthy trainer who will prove themselves capable of unlocking the mysterious Pokémon’s icy tomb.

It is said that whoever unearths the enigmatic Glaciesire must first undergo a series of trials to prove they can harness the duality of desires, embracing ambition and altruism without succumbing to darkness. Once a trainer demonstrates their dedication to balance and empathy, they will be able to unlock the true potential of Glaciesire and bring forth its mighty powers for the greater good.

Perhaps one day, a worthy trainer will unlock Glaciesire’s frozen prison beneath the icy depths of Shadow’s Basin, and its awe-inspiring presence will echo throughout the Glaciwild Region once again. Only then will Glaciesire reclaim its rightful place among the legends of the Pokémon world.

Legendary Story:
In the early days of the world, the Original One, Arceus, created Glaciesire, the Banished Apostle, as the embodiment of desires. Tasked with sparking emotions, dreams, and aspirations within humanity, Glaciesire became one of the revered Lake Guardians. Alongside Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf, Glaciesire guided and watched over the world with its potent powers.

Over time, Glaciesire sensed an imbalance in humanity’s desires. Corrupted dreams and unbridled ambition clouded the world, eroding the delicate balance. Seeking guidance, Glaciesire found solace in the counsel of Giratina, the Pokémon of the Distortion World. However, Arceus saw the alliance between the Banished Apostle and Giratina as a transgression, leading to it banishing Glaciesire from the Lake Guardians.

In exile, Glaciesire retreated to the uncharted Twilight Zone within the Glaciwild Region. Hidden beneath the icy waters of Shadow’s Basin, Glaciesire’s power lay dormant, awaiting a worthy trainer to restore balance to the world.

Centuries later, rumors spread of a fabled icy tomb containing a Pokémon of unimaginable power. This legend inspired trainers from around the world to venture into the Twilight Zone, facing its treacherous conditions and enigmatic riddles as they sought to uncover the truth behind Glaciesire’s legend.

The story of Glaciesire’s redemption unfolds as a chosen trainer embarks on a journey to restore balance to humanity’s desires. This noble quest involves the retrieval of four ancient relics representing the realms of emotions, knowledge, willpower, and desires. Each relic, guarded by powerful Pokémon, can only be obtained by trainers with pure hearts and unwavering determination.

Upon finding the four relics, the trainer—armed with newfound wisdom—returns to Shadow’s Basin, unlocking the path to Glaciesire’s icy tomb. The Banished Apostle emerges from the frozen prison, testing the trainer’s strength in a battle that will determine the world’s fate.

Displaying talent, resilience, and empathy, the trainer emerges victorious, their bond with Glaciesire reaffirmed. Glaciesire is restored to its legendary status, its power harnessed once more to bring balance to the human world.

With Glaciesire’s return, the world witnesses the dawning of a new era, as aspirations and emotions harmonize, bringing forth the true potential of humanity. The legend of the Banished Apostle, Glaciesire, echoes through time, forever shaping the annals of Pokémon history.

Title: The Banishment of Glaciesire and the Accord with Giratina

In ancient times, deep within the sacred halls of Sinnoh’s stelae, legends spoke of a powerful Pokémon, Glaciesire - The Banished Apostle. Blessed with the ability to grant desires and aspirations, Glaciesire stood as one of the four Lake Guardians entrusted with guiding the hearts of the mortal world.

Yet, as humanity sought unrestrained dreams and ambition, the delicate balance of emotions fractured. Glaciesire, uncertain of how to restore this harmony, sought wisdom in the counsel of the great Giratina, ruler of the Distortion World.

Giratina, feared and revered in equal measure, saw potential in Glaciesire’s vast power. Together, they conspired to bridge the unseen realms and harness the chaotic energy to amend the hearts of man. But in their endeavors, they unwittingly awakened dark desires that threatened to corrupt the world.

The Great Creator, Arceus, beheld the alliance between Glaciesire and Giratina as betrayal. In an act of divine wrath, Arceus banished Glaciesire from the ranks of the Lake Guardians, severing the bond that connected them to their brethren.

Glaciesire, now exiled and despondent, retreated to the icy reaches of the Glaciwild Region, where they found solace in the perpetual twilight of the forbidden land.

From the primal etchings upon the Sinnoh stelae, the tale of Glaciesire’s banishment and accord with Giratina serves as a testament to the unfathomable powers that govern our world, and the delicate balance that must be upheld to preserve the heart of all creation.
