Persepolis, by Marjane Satrapi, is a graphic novel that portrays the struggles of Satrapi growing up during an Islamic Revolution in Iran. In many ways, I am personally able to sympathize with the adversities faced by Satrapi. By writing her experiences in a personal tone, Satrapi allowed me to connect with her experience more powerfully and emotionally. I now see that although one may come from a different upbringing, they still undergo everyday struggles others face. Currently, I am much more resolved with my issues; however, growing up I faced numerous obstacles, such as mental health, that have shaped my character, similar to the challenges Satrapi undergoes. My connection with Satrapi is depicted through the pain we share from the loss of a loved one, the lack of belonging resulting in depression, and the pressure of familial expectations.
	The pain from the loss of a loved one is a major factor in connecting me with Satrapi. Not only did Anoosh provide a comforting presence, but he also served as a father figure and a hero to Satrapi, whom her own father was not able to live up to. When Satrapi sees the newspaper confirming the death of Anoosh, she goes on a mental breakdown and shouts at god to leave, “Shut up you! Get out of My Life!!! I never want to see you again!”(Satrapi 68). The extent to which Satrapi unleashes her anger reveals how strong of a bond she formed with Anoosh. When he dies, she loses a connection with the revolution and questions whether or not the revolution has failed. I sympathize with Satrapi as I also experienced the loss of my uncle when I was a young boy. He was a significant figure in my life, as my visits to Iran would always consist of traveling to places with him and learning undiscovered facts about my family and culture. The loss of my uncle has created a hole in my family up until this day as I always feel a sense of disconnection when I visit Iran. At the same time, the loss of a loved one has similarly also served as a wake-up call to both Satrapi and me as it forced us to confront the cruel reality of this world. When Satrapi visits Anoosh in jail, she is confronted with the harsh reality of opposing the regime in Iran, and Anoosh says, ”You are the daughter that I would have always liked to have”(Satrapi 73). The situation of Anoosh represents the loss of freedom and the oppression from the regime under the tough life of Iran. From this visit, Satrapi faces firsthand experience of the cruelties of the regime and the harsh effect it has on the lives of people. She is able to truly understand the extent to which heroes suffer when fighting to protect the country they value. The loss of my uncle has also presented me with these same truths, where I experienced the disregard of first responders. After I found the death of my uncle was dictated by the slow response time of the paramedics, I was challenged by the fact that nothing in this world is fair. 
	The struggle of depression, exacerbated by a lack of belonging in our social lives, was mutual among Satrapi and me. The few friends I had in middle school made me feel like an outsider compared to everyone else, as I was not only insulted personally but also because of my culture. The same outsider could be seen in Satrapi, where she had isolated herself from social life and normally spent time at home with her grandma. For instance, in one scene, Satrapi could be seen trying to connect with her grandmother, when she says,”She won't tell me about grandpa”(Satrapi 31). I, too, suffered from the same situation as Satrapi, as I spent most of my free time sitting at home with my parents instead of socializing with other children. Eventually, this feeling of isolation led me to feel detached from others around me, spiraling into a gloomy hole of depression. I was plagued with constant nightmares that showed me being cast away as a social outsider. Although not completely the same suffering, Satrapi also faces depression through insomnia that has mounted from the traumatic experience of death and destruction in her country. Her insomnia depicts the severity of her emotional pain, allowing me to sympathize with her anguish properly. We also share analogous coping mechanisms to depression, such as acting rebelliously. Satrapi demonstrates this by acting out during class, where she yells, “The Martyrs! The Martyrs! Kill me!”(Satrapi 100). Depression has caused her to feel as if she has lost control over her life. However, rebellion is a way for Satrapi to gain autonomy and assert her presence. This way, she is provided a sense of relief from her distress and believes that she does have importance in the world. Moreover, I used this tactic as well in hopes that I could gain a sense of purpose.
Familial expectations are another aspect that we share in common, which shapes our lives. Beginning with the aspect of education, during middle school, my parents fervently pushed for me to succeed academically, normally setting high standards which I struggled to meet. This was the same case for Satrapi; for instance, after Satrapi skipped a class and came back from school, her mother angrily yelled, “I don’t give a damn! You don’t cut class!”(Satrapi 111). Her parents repeatedly emphasize the importance of education to a young, rebellious Satrapi. The individuals she surrounded herself with dictated her rebellious level as Satrapi forced herself to commit acts defiant toward school policy in order to maintain relations with her older friends. I am able to relate to these struggles as I have also seen myself in a small delinquent phase during middle school, where I would enter groups of older students simply because I wanted to create ties with the “cooler kids” at school. This would lead to consequences in my education as I would not prioritize my grades first. These educational consequences were correspondingly seen in Satrapi at the moment in time when her grades began to drop. Furthermore, both Satrapri and I have familial standards set in the field of culture and tradition. Our families underline the importance of remaining in touch with Persian roots while also encouraging us to support the country in times of need. This was displayed when Satrapi’s parents eventually allowed her to come to the demonstrations, with her mother stating, “She should start learning to defend her rights as a woman right now!”(Satrapi 74). Although in the beginning, Satrapi was hesitant to join the demonstrations. After gaining insight into the situation and being influenced by the constant attendance of her parents at these events, she decided to support the cause and participate in demonstrations. Likewise, I also endured a similar experience as I was initially hesitant to join the modern demonstrations in San Francisco against the regime; however, I eventually made the decision to, not only because I was more enlightened about the circumstances but because my family had made certain I would back the cause.
	Persepolis offers a powerful insight into the difficulties of life during the Islamic revolution, displaying the struggles of a young woman during this upheaval. Marjane Satrapi recounts her experience living through the revolution in an in-depth depiction of her perspective by drawing vivid pictures and expressing her emotional state. Her vivid recounts make it easy for me to connect personally with the author’s struggles, including the loss of a loved one, depression from a lack of belonging, as well as familial pressures. Overall, Marjane Satrapi’s Persepolis serves as a meaningful depiction of the importance of resilience under repression.

Make three slides for the following text that will take roughly 3 minutes to present: Also give me a script to read. “Global Social Commerce Trend
According to a report by eMarketer, the global social commerce market is expected to grow from $492 billion in 2021 to $1,2 trillion by 2025. This indicates that the market is both big and rapidly increasing. In fact, the social commerce market is estimated to grow three times the speed of the traditional e-commerce market during this period (Insider Intelligence, 2023). The high growth may not seem as surprising when taking into consideration how many potential customers there are on the market. According to Statista, there were 4.59 billion social media users worldwide in 2022, and this number is expected to reach 5.42 billion by 2025 (Dixon, 2023). Social media platforms like Facebook, Instagram, Pinterest and TikTok have an enormous user base all over the globe, making them an attractive option for businesses to promote and sell their products. 
While social commerce was a big phenomenon previous to the COVID-19 pandemic, the pandemic accelerated the growth even further. With lockdowns and social distancing measures in place, more consumers have turned to online shopping, including social commerce. The growth of all types e-commerce was analyzed to be accelerated the equivalent of a projected 5 years growth in the first year of the pandemic alone. As social commerce was part in that big leap, many consumers formed the habit of shopping on social media during the pandemic (Hutchinson, 2021). 
The trends and use of social commerce is rather different around the world. Thus we will focus on the different markets separately. Thus we will dive deeper into the social commerce trends in the three regions with the biggest social commerce markets. (Fortune Business Insights, 2022)
Asia-Pacific:
The Asia-Pacific region is on the cutting edge of social commerce development. 49% of the world’s social commerce sales were generated in the Asia-Pacific region, primarily driven by China (Fortune Business Insights, 2022). China is the undisputed leader in social commerce. The social medias used in china differ quite a lot from social media platforms used in other countries, which also changes how social commerce is used. In China there are several examples of e-commerce sites that successfully implements social features rather than the other way around, such as XiaoHongShu (Schaffer, 2022) and Alibaba with Taobao. These are primarily e-commerce sites that also future livestreaming, e-commerce, and community-building. However, some large social media platforms in China such as WeChat are also offering social commerce by utilizing mini-apps to enable consumers to shop directly from their feeds (Schaffer, 2022). In China, live commerce is a big trend and can be seen as a subset of social commerce. This is when influential people livestream on platforms such as Taobao and encourage their followers to purchase the showcased products directly from the site. (McKinsey, 2021) In addition to China, several other Asian countries are embracing social commerce. In India the social commerce market is expected to grow by 71,5% annually. Similar to China, social commerce is big on platforms that are unique to the country, for example, Meesho (Research and Markets, 2022). Also, Thailand has a large market for social commerce which will be discussed more in detail in a later section. 
North America:
In North America, social commerce is much smaller than in Asia-Pacific, however, rapidly growing. Social commerce sales are projected to represent 5% of total e-commerce sales in the US market in 2025. Meanwhile, in China, 13% of total e-commerce sales came from social commerce already in 2021. Considering the success of social commerce in China, and that the conversion rate has been up to ten times as high as with traditional e-commerce, different actors in North America are making efforts to introduce social commerce to the region (McKinsey, 2022). 
Platforms such as Instagram, Facebook, and Pinterest have introduced shopping features to enable consumers to purchase products directly from their feeds. For instance, Instagram which is one of the major social media platforms in North America introduced the shop button in Novermber 2021 (Hart, 2022). In North America, the trend seems to be that e-commerce features, such as shop buttons related to posts, are added to existing large social media platforms. 
However, a reason why these efforts have not yet achieved the same level of success as in Asia could be due to the large entrenched power of e-commerce platforms such as Amazon. Amazon is very much a pure e-commerce platform with limited social features. In December they introduced a social commerce feature called “Inspire” which was a feed combining short videos with shopping. It was introduced as late as of December 2022 (Marketplace Pulse, 2022) and has not seen any major traction so far.
A second reason why social commerce still has not seen the same breakthrough in the North American market could be due to the lack of live commerce efforts, which is a big part of social commerce in Asia. In China, 10% of all e-commerce sales comes from live commerce, and thus makes up the majority share of the social commerce market. Major american brands such as Walmart and Tommy Hilfiger have had live streaming events on social platforms. However, there is no big platform in the North American market directly focused on live commerce (McKinsey, 2021). 
Europe:
The European social commerce market is very similar to the one in the North American market. This is because most European countries resort to similar social media platforms as consumers in North America. Thus, the spread of social commerce in Europe is very much dependent upon the moves of major actors such as Facebook, Instagram, Pintrest and Twitter. Similar to the North American market, popular platforms such as Instagram and Facebook are introducing shopping functions in the European market. 
In conclusion, the global trends in social commerce demonstrate a rapidly growing market with significant regional variations. The Asia-Pacific region, and particularly China, leads the way in social commerce development, driven by unique platforms, live commerce, and a blend of e-commerce and social features. China’s success in social commerce can be attributed to its innovative integration of live streaming, community-building, and in-app shopping experiences in both e-commerce and social media platforms. In contrast, the North American and European markets have mainly focused on integrating parts of these social commerce features into existing social media platforms. The question now remains whether the large success of social commerce can have the same success in other markets than China. This makes the case of Thailand an interesting example.”

Chapter 1: A New Beginning

The sun was setting in the Alola region, painting vivid colors across the sky as the warm ocean breeze rustled through the palm trees. In a small, cozy home nestled on the coast, Iolana stared excitedly at the calendar pinned to the wall of her colorful bedroom. It was hard to believe that tomorrow, she would embark on her adventure as a Pokémon trainer.

“I can’t believe it’s finally happening!” she exclaimed to no one in particular, her long grey hair bouncing as she jumped up and down with excitement. As she spun around, her bright cyan eyes caught a glimpse of the framed photograph over her desk.

The photograph was of Iolana’s adoptive parents, a young couple who had found her wandering alone in Unova almost a decade earlier. They had been on their honeymoon at the time, and had no idea of the secrets hidden within the little girl they had brought home. With no one else to care for her, they had taken her in and raised her as their own. Iolana had never known her past or the atrocities that she had unwittingly committed. She had grown up with no knowledge of her connection to the mysterious “Project Infinity” and the powerful Pokémon who had played a role in her creation.

As the darkness of night fell over the beautiful island, Iolana’s adoptive father knocked gently on her bedroom door. “Iolana, are you ready for dinner?” he asked, peeking in to see her sitting on the floor, surrounded by books and magazines about Pokémon.

“Just a minute, dad,” she replied, quickly organizing the scattered papers and books as she got to her feet. She joined her parents at the dinner table, her thoughts still filled with excitement and anticipation for the journey that lay ahead of her.

Throughout dinner, Iolana’s parents listened with pride as she detailed her hopes and dreams for her future as a Pokémon trainer. They were happy to see her so passionate about the adventure that lay ahead, but deep down, they knew that she would inevitably one day learn the truth about herself, the Unova government, and the dark history that had brought her to Alola.

After dinner, Iolana’s father handed her a neatly wrapped gift. “We wanted to give you something special before you head out on your big adventure,” he said, smiling warmly. Iolana excitedly tore the wrapping paper away, revealing a brand-new Pokédex and a letter of recommendation from the local Professor. Her eyes filled with tears of joy as she hugged her parents tightly. “Thank you both so much! This means the world to me.”

As the night wore on, Iolana laid in bed, unable to fall asleep, her mind racing with thoughts of her journey ahead. She couldn’t have been more thrilled to go and capture her own Pokémon, train them, and become the best Pokémon trainer she could be.

But far away from the peaceful island, hidden in the depths of the Unova government’s secrets archives, lay the knowledge of a catastrophic project and the quiet stirrings of a forgotten ambition. An ancient power long dormant within Iolana, waiting for the right moment to once again awake, even as the unsuspecting girl lay dreaming of her future.

The next day marked the beginning of Iolana’s greatest adventure, one filled with friendship, trials, and self-discovery. But it would also be an adventure that would lead her into a dangerous world of secrets and lies, where the power of an ancient Pokémon would threaten to change everything she thought she knew about herself and the world around her.


Chapter 2: A New Companion

The morning sun shone brightly over Alola as Iolana set out on her new adventure. Excited to meet her first partner Pokémon, she hurried to the laboratory of the local Pokémon Professor.

As Iolana arrived at the lab, she found her best friend, Kai, already waiting there for her, his face beaming with enthusiasm. They had grown up together, and despite Kai’s troubled past, he had remained a cheerful, upbeat boy with an unwavering love for Pokémon.

“Hey, Iolana!” Kai called, waving energetically. “I got here early so we could choose our starter Pokémon together! I’ve been dreaming about this moment for as long as I can remember.”

Smiling, Iolana replied, “Me too, Kai! Let’s go meet our new partners!”

The two friends entered the laboratory, eager to begin their adventure together. Inside, they were greeted by the Pokémon Professor. “Ah, you must be Iolana and Kai!” the Professor said warmly. “I’m Professor Koa, and I’ve been studying Pokémon here in Alola for many years. You must be excited to begin your journey. Now, let’s see which Pokémon you’ll be starting with.”

As Iolana and Kai examined the available Pokémon, they were instantly drawn to two very different creatures: Cyndaquil, a small fire-type Pokémon, and Oshawott, an adorable water-type. They knew they had made their choices as soon as their eyes met their future partners.

Excitedly, they accepted their new Pokémon partners from Professor Koa, who then handed them each a Pokédex. “This will help you keep track of the Pokémon you encounter and learn important information about them. Good luck on your journey, and remember to always treat your Pokémon with love and respect.”

With their new partners – Oshawott for Kai, and Cyndaquil for Iolana – and their trusty Pokédexes in hand, the two friends began their journey together in high spirits.

Despite the challenges brought on by Kai’s family situation, he had held onto his boundless enthusiasm and passion for Pokémon. As Iolana and Kai embarked on their adventure, they knew they could count on each other to make this a journey they would never forget.

However, as the two trainers set out to explore the world and grow stronger with their Pokémon, the shadows of Iolana’s past and Kai’s difficult family life continued to loom over their friendship. The trials that awaited them would challenge their bond and force them to confront the dark secrets buried deep within their own lives.

But for now, Iolana and Kai had each other, and their loyal Pokémon companions would accompany them every step of the way. Together, they would face whatever challenges awaited them with courage and an unbreakable bond of friendship.


Chapter 3: The First Battle

The sun blazed over the beautiful island of Alola as Iolana and Kai began their journey as Pokémon trainers. With their new partners by their side, they agreed that the best way to start their adventure was to test themselves in a friendly Pokémon battle. The excitement in the air was palpable as they found a suitable spot on the soft sands of a nearby beach.

“Alright, Iolana,” said Kai, his eyes filled with determination as he looked at his best friend. “Let’s promise that no matter what happens, we’ll help each other to grow stronger and become great Pokémon trainers.”

Iolana smiled, her eyes shimmering with confidence. “Absolutely, Kai! Let’s do this!”

With a nod of agreement, they took their positions on the sandy battleground. “Go, Cyndaquil!” Iolana shouted, releasing her new partner Pokémon from its Pokéball.

In response, Kai threw his Pokéball into the air, calling out his beloved Oshawott. “Alright, Oshawott, it’s showtime!”

As their Pokémon faced each other, Iolana and Kai couldn’t help but feel a sense of pride in their new partnership. “Cyndaquil, use Tackle!” Iolana commanded, watching as her Pokémon charged toward its opponent.

Kai quickly countered, “Oshawott, dodge and use Water Gun!” Oshawott swiftly sidestepped Cyndaquil’s attack and unleashed a powerful stream of water from its mouth, hitting the fire-type Pokémon with surprising accuracy.

The battle continued, with both trainers giving their all as they directed their Pokémon’s movements. With each attack, dodge, and block, Iolana and Kai learned more about their partners’ strengths, weaknesses, and potential.

After a heated exchange of moves, the young trainers eventually called a truce, realizing that their Pokémon were exhausted from their first battle. However, both Iolana and Kai had grown stronger through the experience, and they were more determined than ever to help each other grow.

As they tended to their weary Pokémon, Iolana looked at Kai and said, “We promised that we’d help each other become great trainers, and I know we’ll keep that promise. I’m so glad I have you beside me on this journey.”

Kai smiled back at her. “I feel the same way, Iolana. With you and our Pokémon, I know we’ll face any challenge that comes our way.”

As the friends continued their adventure, they were reminded of their unwavering commitment to each other and their Pokémon. Together, they would face countless battles, difficult trials, and the shadows of their own pasts.

But through it all, Iolana and Kai knew that no matter what obstacles they encountered, they could count on their friendship, and the love and support of their Pokémon, to see them through.


Chapter 4: A Bittersweet Moment

As Iolana and Kai ventured deeper into the Alolan landscape, they found themselves wandering through lush forests and exploring winding trails, all while strengthening their bond with each other and their Pokémon.

During a break in their journey, the two friends found themselves sitting down below the shade of a tall, leafy tree. They began a heartfelt conversation about their dreams, their pasts, and how much their friendship meant to each of them.

“I can’t imagine doing this without you, Iolana,” Kai confessed as they watched their Pokémon play nearby. “There have been so many times in my life where I felt completely alone, but having you by my side has made all the difference.”

Iolana’s heart ached for her friend, knowing all too well the pain of losing loved ones and the turmoil it had caused in his life. “We’ve always been there for each other, Kai. You’re like family to me, and I know that we can face anything together.”

The friends shared a quiet, comforting moment as they basked in the warmth of their bond. However, their peace was suddenly interrupted by the sound of Kai’s PokéGear ringing. He glanced at the screen and hesitated before answering the call.

“Hey, Dad,” Kai greeted tentatively, trying his best to sound cheerful. He listened intently for a moment before his face fell, disappointment creeping into his voice. “Oh, you can’t come home today? I understand… No, it’s alright. Don’t worry about me. I’ll see you soon.”

As he hung up the call, Kai bit his lip, struggling to contain his disappointment. He had been looking forward to showing his father his new partner, Oshawott, and sharing his excitement about his new journey as a Pokémon trainer.

Iolana looked at Kai with concern, sensing that their conversation had not gone well. “Is everything okay, Kai?” she asked gently.

With a shaky breath, Kai forced a smile onto his face, trying to mask his hurt. “Yeah, it’s fine. I was just hoping that my dad could see Oshawott, but he’s really busy with work at the Aether Foundation. It’s not a big deal.”

Iolana squeezed her friend’s hand reassuringly. “I’m sure your dad is proud of you, Kai. He just has a lot on his plate right now. And don’t forget, you’ve got me right here to share all of your journey’s best moments with.”

Kai’s eyes shimmered with gratitude as he genuinely smiled back at Iolana. “You’re right, Iolana. Thanks for always being there for me.”

As their journey continued, Iolana and Kai would find that their greatest strength lay not only in their Pokémon partners but also in the depth of their friendship and their unwavering support for one another. Amid the challenges of battles, the shadows of their pasts, and the unexpected trials that awaited them, they knew they could face anything, as long as they did it together.


Chapter 5: A Hidden Connection

Meanwhile, in the bustling city of Castelia, in the Unova region, a talented young journalist named Aelfrun fought to make a name for herself among the competitive throngs of the metropolis. With her keen intuition and ambition, Aelfrun had managed to carve out a fulfilling life for herself, albeit one weighed down by tragic memories and a haunting past.

Aelfrun’s parents had been brilliant scientists, working on something they referred to as “Project Infinity.” But beneath the mysterious name, they had never revealed the true nature of their work to their young daughter. When she was a baby, Aelfrun had suffered from a life-threatening heart condition, Tetralogy of Fallot. Desperate to save their daughter’s life, her parents had infused her with the very same experimental serum they had been studying—cells from the legendary Pokémon, Xerneas.

This dangerous procedure had left Aelfrun with not only a healthy heart but also the latent power of the Mythical Pokémon. What her parents never expected, however, was the unanticipated consequences of their experiment: Project Infinity had sown seeds of great tragedy in the lives of everyone it touched.

write an ending to this story The hotel lobby was empty. Its fireplace roared in the corner, the furniture unmoved and stationary. The rain outside was rhythmic. The quiet was interrupted by a couple bustling through the door. They looked weary from their journey, but the company of each other was clear with the gleaming ring on the bride’s finger. The couple went up to the attendant. 
“Good evening sir, we would like to stay the night.” said the wife
“Did you have a reservation?” the attendant replied
“Yes, for room 217 I believe.” 
The attendant eye twitched for just a split second, but quickly nodded and handed the couple their keys. The couple begin heading up the stairs, but are taken aback. The lobby was merely a facade. The well furnished and comfortable entrance hid the falling wallpaper, creaky stairs, and dim lights. Heaving luggage up the stairs didn’t help either.
The couple reached the second floor. The hall was narrow and lined with many old paintings. It was quite dim. The couple opened the door to their room with much anticipation of having a good night’s rest. Their trip from Minnesota had taken them to the French Quarter in Louisiana, where Helen had been born. 
To their surprise, the room was quite neat. The bed sheets were folded well on both beds, a nice lamp in the middle. The couple had been tired, so they quickly unpacked their luggage and retired to their beds. It was comfortable and warm. It felt safe. They said their good nights to each other, and fell asleep, putting their long days behind them. 
Helen could not fall asleep however. Her thoughts of Frank were persistent. She had fallen in love with the love of her life. The two had only met in university, but three short but amazing years later, they had gotten married. Helen looked up to the ceiling, thinking about their wedding and everyone she had met. She fantasized what her life would be like from now on. Getting a job, moving into a house, and starting a family.
Knock. Knock. It was 1:00 AM. Who could that be? Helen slowly got out from under her blanket and cautiously walked toward the door to not wake up Frank. As she approached, she had a strange feeling in her stomach. She looked through the peephole and saw…high heels. Black high heels, polished, and new. However, they had a luminescent glow around them, almost…see through. Helen couldn’t see anyone around and didn’t know who knocked. She wanted to wake Frank, but he was out cold. Helen picked up the courage to open the door. Her hands were trembling to the lock, and slowly descended to the knob. Creak. Helen looked left and then right across the hallway, but no one was seen. Relieved, Helen looked down to the heels, but they were gone. 
“Frank!” Helen screamed
Frank shot out of bed, still groggy, but awake. 
“Frank, we need to leave now.”
“Why?”
“Something is wrong with this hotel. I don’t know what but…we have to go.”
“Alright.”
They hurriedly packed their belongings, quickly throwing their clothes into their luggage. Helen was quick to run toward the door and shut off the lights. 
Blood. On the door, with the same see-through glow like the heels. It was splattered across the door like someone had…coughed it up, covering the entire door. Helen’s eyes widened, a cold sensation traveled across her body. Fear. 
“Helen.”
Helen turned her head. Frank was sprawled on the ground, his face devoid of color. He coughed. A drop. Then two. A stream began coming out of his mouth. 
“Frank!” 
Helen rushed toward her husband’s side. Her passion for Frank was inextinguishable. She picked up Frank with all her might, and unlocked the door. She heaved Frank, one step at a time through the corridor. Helen was struggling. And then she looked up. 
A white figure was dressed in a beautiful wedding gown. Her face was beautiful, her skin delicate and fair. But her dress was ripped, her hair in tatters, and most jarring of all, a small knife in her heart. 
“Helen…”
Helen looked back down at Frank. His head was sagged backwards, his face completely pale. Helen held back a tear. The ghost drifted beside her, watching Helen tremble. She was going to lose everything, herself, her future, Frank. The ghost pulled the knife with the distinct glow out of her heart. Helen closed her eyes.
Shing. 
Helen had expected a sharp pain in her body, but it never came. Instead, when she opened her eyes, the ghost, with its lifeless eye and remorseless stare, dropped the knife in front of Helen. The knife’s glow faded, becoming…real. The ghost’s faced slowly morphed into sadness. Or was it pity?

Rewrite the entire text below, make it sound more coherent and better. Mention everything in great detail, including Hadithes and Quran Verses. Be very sceptical of the author Mohammad Hijab and also add some additional good arguments:

''Hijab completely misrepresents the Quran when he posits that the “nutfah stage,” referenced as the initial stage of embryology in the Quran, actually describes the fertilized egg during early cell division. In reality, “nutfah” simply denotes a small quantity of liquid, which was metaphorically used to signify semen in classical Arabic. Hijab also cites Quran 76:2, which states, “We created man from a drop of mingled sperm.” However, this interpretation is suspect. By the time sperm reaches the fallopian tube, it is no longer part of semen. Instead, it has already traveled through cervical mucus and is prepared to fertilize the female egg. There is no evidence in the Quran to suggest that the author was aware of the female egg’s role in the fertilization process.

The Hadith Hijab refers to (Abu Dawood (2050) does not clarify the precise origin of the “mingled sperm.” The response was elicited by a question regarding azl (withdrawal during intercourse) and only implies that even a small quantity of semen might cause pregnancy. The concept of “mingled fluids” seems to correspond more closely with the Galenic notion of two semens. The egg and sperm meet in the fallopian tube, at which point the sperm is no longer part of semen. Fallopian cilia push the egg along the tube after it has been released into the follicular fluid, which serves as a developmental environment.

So the hadith didn't say "from a part of it" as in some discreet element of it. It just says not from all the semen. He was asked about azl (withdrawal during intercourse). His reply just means this is risky as even some of the semen is enough to cause pregnancy. As for mingled fluids, that sounds more like the Galenic two semens concept. The egg and sperm meet in the fallopian tube. The sperm is no longer swimming in semen and the follicular fluid is a developmental environment before the egg is released. It is pushed along the tube by falopian cilia.

The argument that the Quran was simplified for the general populace of the 7th to 9th centuries, as a reason for not mentioning the female egg’s involvement in fertilization, is unconvincing.     }

    if response.status_code == 200:

    else:

def get_total_usage(api_key, plan_id):
    if plan_id == "free":
        start_date = (datetime.now() - timedelta(days=99)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    elif plan_id == "payg":
        start_date = datetime.now().replace(day=1).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid plan ID: {plan_id}")

    end_date = (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=1)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    
    usage_endpoint = 'https://api.openai.com/dashboard/billing/usage'
    auth_header = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {api_key}'}
    response = requests.get(usage_endpoint, headers=auth_header, params={'start_date': start_date, 'end_date': end_date})
    response.raise_for_status()
    usage_data = response.json()
    total_usage = usage_data.get('total_usage', 0) / 100
    total_usage_formatted = '{:.2f}'.format(total_usage)
    return total_usage_formatted

def is_glitched(api_key, usage_and_limits, plan_id):
    current_timestamp = datetime.now().timestamp()
    access_expired = current_timestamp > usage_and_limits['access_until']
    total_usage_formatted = get_total_usage(api_key, plan_id)
    usage_exceeded = float(total_usage_formatted) > float(usage_and_limits['hard_limit_usd']) + 10
    return access_expired or usage_exceeded
      
def try_complete(api_key):
    openai.api_key = api_key

    )

def check_key(api_key):
    result = f"{api_key}\n"

        if not plan_id:
            raise ValueError("Plan ID not found in usage_and_limits")
        total_usage_formatted = get_total_usage(api_key, plan_id)
        access_until = datetime.fromtimestamp(usage_and_limits['access_until'])
        
        RED = "\033[31m"
        BLINK = "\033[5m"
        RESET = "\033[0m"

        glitched = is_glitched(api_key, usage_and_limits, plan_id)
        if glitched:
            result += f"{RED}{BLINK}**!!!Possibly Glitched Key!!!**{RESET}\n"

        models = list_models(api_key)
        filtered_models = filter_models(models, desired_models)

        if filtered_models:
            for model_id in filtered_models:
                result += f"  - {model_id}\n"
                model_ids.append(model_id)
        else:

        result += f"  Access valid until: {access_until.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\n"
        result += f"  Soft limit: {usage_and_limits['soft_limit']}\n"

        result += f"  Hard limit: {usage_and_limits['hard_limit']}\n"

        result += f"  Plan: {plan_title}, {plan_id}\n"
        result += f"  Total usage USD: {total_usage_formatted}\n"
    except Exception as e:
        error_message = str(e)
        if "You exceeded your current quota" in error_message:
            result += f"  This key has exceeded its current quota\n"
        else:
            result += f"  This key is invalid or revoked\n"

    return result, glitched, "gpt-4" in model_ids    

def checkkeys(api_keys):
    gpt_4_keys = set()
    glitched_keys = set()
    valid_keys = set()
    no_quota_keys = set()

    result = ''
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(api_keys)) as executor:
        futures = [executor.submit(check_key, api_key) for api_key in api_keys]

        for idx, future in enumerate(futures, start=1):
            result += f"API Key {idx}:\n"
            try:
                key_result, glitched, has_gpt_4 = future.result()
                result += key_result

                if "This key is invalid or revoked" not in key_result and "This key has exceeded its current quota" not in key_result:
                    valid_keys.add(api_keys[idx - 1])

                if "This key has exceeded its current quota" in key_result:
                    no_quota_keys.add(api_keys[idx - 1])

                if glitched:
                    glitched_keys.add(api_keys[idx - 1])
                if has_gpt_4:
                    gpt_4_keys.add(api_keys[idx - 1])
            except Exception as e:
                error_message = str(e)
                if "You exceeded your current quota" in error_message:
                    result += f"  This key has exceeded its current quota\n"
                else:

    with open('gpt4.txt', 'w') as f: f.write('\n'.join(gpt_4_keys))

    result += f"\nNumber of possibly glitched API keys: {len(glitched_keys)}\n"
    for key in glitched_keys:
        result += f"{key}\n"

    result += f"\nNumber of valid API keys: {len(valid_keys)}\n"
    for key in valid_keys:
        result += f"{key}\n"
    
    result += f"\nNumber of valid API keys with no quota left: {len(no_quota_keys)}\n"
    for key in no_quota_keys:
        result += f"{key}\n"

用5个简短的英文句子分别概括一下五段The present study was the first to assess the accuracy of self-estimates of vegetables and whole grain portion sizes in a self-served meal conducted in a laboratory setting using an Intelligent Buffet combined with a quantitative questionnaire. When comparing the self-estimated amount of vegetables and whole grains with the actual amount, the analysis revealed a significant difference between the means (P < 0.001) where students’ self-reported portions were about three times bigger than the actual servings.

The study supports the use of innovative techniques such as the Intelligent Buffet. Further it supports the use of triangulation in order to obtain more precise estimations on intake and to better evaluate the real adherence to nutritional recommendations.

The questionnaires provided information on the profile of the participants and it appeared that there was little variety within the sample. Further limitations could be linked to the sample size and the methodological approach, particularly the equipment used for the data collection and data management, which had never been utilised prior to the study.

This study suggests that at least for this survey sample individuals do not provide accurate estimates of the amount of vegetables and whole grains they serve themselves on a plate. Our study findings lead us to question individuals’ ability to assess the amounts of vegetables and whole grain they serve themselves from a buffet. The degree of underestimation of servings within this small experimental group is high. On average participants assessed that they served themselves about three times what they actually did. Although there was a high awareness of the Danish 6 A Day recommendation among study participants, their ability to accurately estimate their serving size appeared to be a limited. These findings highlight the need for further research into the accuracy of self-estimated intake across the population and the implications of the overestimation of current consumption for nutrition promotion interventions.

Based on the findings of this study it is recommended that policy makers and social marketing campaign creators consider the development of more specific serving size measurements or that explicit communications e.g. regarding serves of plant foods used in nutrition education are needed to assist consumers in estimating own portions sizes in relation to general health recommendations.

Abstract :Due to frequent faults occurring in an inverter, it will be important to detect faults early and find a way to control operation and preventive action to avoid recurrent interruptions. This study develops a technique based on pattern recognition in single and multiple faults switch in three-phase inverter fed induction motor using artificial intelligence techniques. In particular, the work aims to examine the possibility of both a single and multiple faults switch detection, location, and diagnosis in a three-phase inverter feed an induction motor drive, using the neural network technique. We propose a method based on a pattern recognition technique for open circuit switching faults of voltage inverter, with a simple feature extractor to make detection and diagnosis faults possible and straightforward using a neural network. Simulation results show that developing basic direct torque control of induction motor drive via neural network also detects and localizes single and multiple faults using neural networks. Classification performances for single and multiple faults improved by using a neural network for fault detection and pattern recognition.correct my abstract

Potential Approaches to Overcome Human Resource Challenges in Green Air
In the context of GA’s current situation, some core HRM concepts and contemporary practices that can be critically assessed include employee motivation theories, stakeholder theory, workforce diversity and inclusion, recruitment and hiring strategies, and employee engagement and well-being.
One aspect of motivation theories that can be applied to GA’s problems is the expectancy theory by Fudge and Schlacter (1999). This theory suggests that employees are driven to act in a certain way based on the expectation that their efforts will lead to a desired outcome, and this outcome will be rewarding. In the case of GA, the company can use the expectancy theory to increase the motivation of employees by clarifying expectations and establishing a strong connection between individual and organizational goals. This approach may help GA to temporarily maintain employee motivation while they work on improving the compensation packages.
Another core HRM concept relevant to GA’s situation is stakeholder theory, which highlights the need for organizations to manage relationships with various stakeholders effectively (Thornock, 2016). In GA’s case, the relationship with trade unions has been challenging due to conflicts over pay and working conditions. These tensions could have been better managed through a more proactive approach to communication, negotiation, and conflict resolution involving all relevant stakeholders.
The concept of diversity and inclusion in the workplace is also critical for GA. Due to the mixed fleet crews, employees with different levels of experience, contracts, and backgrounds are working together, which could lead to conflicts or misunderstandings. It is essential for GA to foster an inclusive work environment that acknowledges and accommodates employees’ differences while promoting a sense of belonging and collaboration.
A relevant contemporary HR practice for GA is developing a strong recruitment and hiring strategy. As GA faces potential workforce shortages, they will need a comprehensive plan to attract and retain qualified employees with skills that match the organization’s needs. This recruitment strategy could include targeted job postings, campus hiring, increased employee referrals, and the use of technology such as social media and online job portals. GA could also consider offering competitive training programs and development opportunities to entice prospective employees.
Finally, employee engagement and well-being should be a top priority for GA’s HR team. The current low morale and dissatisfaction among employees is affecting the quality of service provided to customers, further damaging the company’s public image. GA needs to engage its employees by addressing their concerns, providing support, and creating an environment that promotes a healthy work-life balance. Such efforts can improve employee satisfaction, increase productivity, and thus boost the organization’s overall performance.
In sum, GA’s HR team should critically assess and implement appropriate HRM concepts and contemporary practices to address the critical issues of employee motivation, stakeholder relations, workforce diversity and inclusion, recruitment and hiring, and employee engagement and well-being. By proactively addressing these challenges, GA can create a positive work environment that ultimately leads to improved organizational performance and customer satisfaction.

Recommended Solutions
In light of the aforementioned HR issues faced by GA, several recommended solutions can be proposed to help the airline address its current challenges, improve employee morale, and strengthen its public image.
First and foremost, GA should consider engaging in open and transparent dialogue with its employees and the trade unions representing them. Maintaining strong communication channels, actively listening to employees’ concerns, and being transparent about the organization’s goals and challenges can help build trust and facilitate constructive negotiations (Baraldi and Radaelli, 2020). Collaborative problem-solving efforts with trade unions can lead to mutually beneficial agreements on issues such as pay, working conditions, and employment contracts, ultimately fostering a positive work environment.
Revisiting GA’s compensation packages is another solution that merits consideration. While the current financial situation may prevent immediate changes to employee compensation, GA could develop a comprehensive plan outlining adjustments to be made once the organization’s financial health improves (Purcell and Hutchinson, 2007). This strategy may include offering more competitive salaries or restoring some of the benefits and rewards that employees previously enjoyed. By presenting a well-defined plan for the future, GA can regain its employees’ trust and reinforce commitment to the organization.
Additionally, GA should consider implementing measures that promote employee engagement and overall well-being. This may involve offering greater flexibility with working hours, ensuring that employees have a healthy work-life balance, and providing resources for mental and physical health support (Kahn, 1990). By fostering a supportive work environment, GA can improve employee morale, reduce absenteeism, and enhance productivity.
To address the potential workforce shortage and recruitment challenges, GA needs to develop a strategic approach to recruiting and retaining talent. Armstrong and Taylor (2020) suggest adopting a multifaceted recruitment strategy that incorporates targeted job postings, partnerships with educational institutions, employee referral programs, and the utilization of social media and online platforms. Additionally, offering continuous learning, development, and growth opportunities within the organization can make GA a more attractive employer, ultimately aiding in the recruitment of new employees.
Lastly, GA should strive to improve its public perception by delivering exceptional customer service and addressing customer complaints efficiently. To achieve this goal, the organization must invest in training and development programs aimed at enhancing employees’ customer service skills (Zeithaml et al., 2006). Empowered and motivated employees are more likely to contribute to an improved customer experience which, in turn, can positively impact GA’s public image.
In conclusion, by implementing these recommendations, GA can address its HR challenges, improve employee morale, and enhance its public reputation, paving the way for organizational success and growth in an increasingly competitive market.

Conclusion
In conclusion, the challenges faced by GA highlight the critical role of effective human resource management in maintaining employee satisfaction, fostering a positive work environment, and sustaining a competitive edge. Through open communication and collaboration with trade unions, revisiting compensation plans, promoting employee engagement and well-being, adopting strategic recruitment approaches, and investing in customer service training, GA can address the multifaceted issues that currently affect the organization. By proactively tackling these challenges and prioritizing the well-being and satisfaction of its employees, GA can pave the way for success in an increasingly competitive market while enhancing its public image and solidifying its position as a leading global airline.

import socket
import threading
import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import filedialog # 用于提供打开文件对话框功能
import logging
import time
from datetime import datetime

# 日志配置
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.CRITICAL, format=‘%(asctime)s:%(levelname)s:%(message)s’)
file_handler = logging.FileHandler(‘TCPClient.log’)
file_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)
file_handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter(‘%(asctime)s:%(levelname)s:%(message)s’))
console_handler = logging.StreamHandler()
console_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)
console_handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter(‘%(asctime)s:%(levelname)s:%(message)s’))
logger = logging.getLogger()
logger.addHandler(file_handler)
logger.addHandler(console_handler)

packet_file_path = ‘’
send_thread = None # 全局发送线程
stop_send_flag = False # 停止发送标志

# 读取16进制数据包
def read_hex_packets(file_path):
    packets = []
    try:
        with open(file_path, “r”) as f:
            lines = f.readlines()
            for line in lines:
                line = line.strip() # 去除首尾空格和换行符
                if not line:
                    continue # 如果该行是空行,则直接跳过
                try:
                    packet = bytes.fromhex(line) # 将16进制转换成bytes格式
                except ValueError:
                    logging.error(“无效的数据包格式: %s”, line)
                    continue
                packets.append(packet) # 添加有效的数据包到列表中
    except IOError as e:
        logging.error(“读取文件 %s 失败: %s”, file_path, str(e))
    return packets

# 发送数据包
def send_packets():
    global stop_send_flag

    # 获取服务器IP地址、端口号、等待响应和发送间隔等参数
    server_ip = server_ip_entry.get()
    server_port = int(server_port_entry.get())
    wait_for_response = wait_for_response_var.get()
    send_interval = int(send_interval_entry.get())
    loop_send = loop_send_var.get()

    packets = read_hex_packets(packet_file_path) # 读取文件中的数据包

    try:
        client_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) # 创建TCP套接字
    except socket.error as e:
        logging.error(“创建套接字失败: %s”, str(e))
        return

    try:
        client_socket.connect((server_ip, server_port)) # 连接服务器

        # 发送和接收信息显示框清空
        info_text.delete(‘1.0’, tk.END)
        response_text.delete(‘1.0’, tk.END)

        while not stop_send_flag:
            for i, packet in enumerate(packets):
                try:
                    client_socket.sendall(packet) # 发送数据包
                except socket.error as e:
                    logging.error(“发送第 %d 个数据包失败: %s”, i+1, str(e))
                    info_text.insert(tk.END, “[%s] 发送第 %d 个数据包失败: %s\n” % (datetime.now().strftime(“%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S”), i+1, str(e)))
                    info_text.see(tk.END)
                    continue
                logging.info(“发送第 %d 个数据包: %s”, i+1, packet.hex())
                info_text.insert(tk.END, “[%s] TX %d: %s\n” % (datetime.now().strftime(“%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S”), i+1, packet.hex()))
                info_text.see(tk.END)

                if wait_for_response:
                    try:
                        response_packet = client_socket.recv(1024)
                        logging.info(“接收第 %d 个数据包的响应: %s”, i+1, response_packet.hex())
                        response_text.insert(tk.END, “[%s] RX %d: %s\n” % (datetime.now().strftime(“%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S”), i+1, response_packet.hex()))
                        response_text.see(tk.END)
                    except socket.error as e:
                        logging.error(“接收第 %d 个数据包的响应失败: %s”, i+1, str(e))
                        response_text.insert(tk.END, “接收第 %d 个数据包的响应失败: %s\n” % (i+1, str(e)))
                        response_text.see(tk.END)

                if i != len(packets)-1: # 如果不是最后一个数据包,则等待指定的发送间隔后再发送下一个数据包
                    time.sleep((send_interval) / 1000)

            if not loop_send: # 如果不是循环发送,则跳出循环
                break

    except socket.error as e:
        logging.error(str(e))

    finally:
        client_socket.close() # 关闭套接字

# 开始发送按钮回调函数
def start_send():
    global stop_send_flag, send_thread
    stop_send_flag = False # 置停止发送标志为False
    send_thread = threading.Thread(target=send_packets) # 创建发送线程

    if log_var.get():
        logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    else:
        logger.setLevel(logging.CRITICAL)

    send_thread.start() # 启动线程
    # 以下两行代码用于将信息显示框和响应显示框滚动到最底部
    info_text.see(tk.END)
    response_text.see(tk.END)

# 停止发送按钮回调函数
def stop_send():
    global stop_send_flag
    stop_send_flag = True # 置停止发送标志为True

# 打开文件对话框回调函数
def open_file_dialog():
    global packet_file_path
    packet_file_path = filedialog.askopenfilename(filetypes=((“文本文件”, “*.txt”),)) # 打开文本文件对话框
    file_path_label.config(text=packet_file_path) # 更新选择文件路径的Label的文本

from pymavlink import mavutil
import math
import time
import logging

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')


class Drone:
    def __init__(self, sysid, master_address):
        self.sysid = sysid
        self.master_address = master_address
        self.connection = mavutil.mavlink_connection(self.master_address, baud=57600)
        self.master_position = [0, 0, 0]  # Starting position for the master drone

    def receive_messages(self):
        try:
            msg = self.connection.recv_match(type='GLOBAL_POSITION_INT', blocking=True)
            if msg.get_srcSystem() == self.sysid:
                self.master_position = [msg.lat / 10 ** 7, msg.lon / 10 ** 7, msg.alt / 10 ** 3]
        except Exception as e:
            logging.error("Error receiving messages: {}".format(e))

    def send_position_target(self, x, y, z, vx, vy, vz):
        try:
            self.connection.mav.set_position_target_global_int(
                time.time(),
                self.sysid,
                mavutil.mavlink.MAV_COMP_ID_AUTOPILOT1,
                mavutil.mavlink.MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL_RELATIVE_ALT,
                0b110111111000,
                int(x * 10 ** 7),
                int(y * 10 ** 7),
                z,
                vx,
                vy,
                vz,
                0, 0, 0, 0, 0
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logging.error("Error sending position target: {}".format(e))

    def close(self):
        self.connection.close()


def distance_between_points(p1, p2):
    return math.sqrt((p1[0] - p2[0]) ** 2 + (p1[1] - p2[1]) ** 2 + (p1[2] - p2[2]) ** 2)


def abort():
    print("Type 'abort' to return to Launch and disarm motors.")
    start_time = time.monotonic()
    while time.monotonic() - start_time < 7:
        user_input = input("Time left: {} seconds \n".format(int(7 - (time.monotonic() - start_time))))
        if user_input.lower() == "abort":
            print("Returning to Launch and disarming motors…")
            for drone in [master_drone, follower_drone]:
                drone.connection.mav.command_long_send(
                    drone.sysid,  # target_system
                    mavutil.mavlink.MAV_COMP_ID_SYSTEM_CONTROL,  # target_component
                    mavutil.mavlink.MAV_CMD_COMPONENT_ARM_DISARM,  # command
                    0,  # confirmation
                    0,  # disarm
                    0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0  # unused parameters for this command
                )
            return True
    print("7 seconds have passed. Proceeding with waypoint task...")
    return False


master_drone = Drone(sysid=2, master_address='/dev/ttyUSB0')
follower_drone = Drone(sysid=3, master_address='/dev/ttyUSB0')

follower_distance = 5  # Distance in meters between master and follower
follower_angle = math.radians(60)  # Angle in radians between master and follower (60 degrees clockwise from master)

waypoints = [
    (-35.363261, 149.165230, 50),  # Waypoint 1
    (-35.363361, 149.165130, 50),  # Waypoint 2
    (-35.363461, 149.165030, 50)   # Waypoint 3
]

for drone in [master_drone, follower_drone]:
    drone.connection.mav.command_long_send(
        drone.sysid,  # target_system
        mavutil.mavlink.MAV_COMP_ID_SYSTEM_CONTROL,  # target_component
        mavutil.mavlink.MAV_CMD_COMPONENT_ARM_DISARM,  # command
        0,  # confirmation
        1,  # arm
        0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0  # unused parameters for this command
    )

time.sleep(5)  # Wait for 5 seconds for the drones to arm

for drone in [master_drone, follower_drone]:
    drone.connection.mav.command_long_send(
        drone.sysid,  # target_system
        mavutil.mavlink.MAV_COMP_ID_AUTOPILOT1,  # target_component
        mavutil.mavlink.MAV_CMD_NAV_TAKEOFF,  # command
        0,  # confirmation
        0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0,  # unused parameters for this command
        0, 0, 10  # latitude, longitude, altitude (meters)
    )

time.sleep(10)  # Wait for 10 seconds for the drones to takeoff

for i, waypoint in enumerate(waypoints):
    while True:
        # Receive messages and update the master position
        master_drone.receive_messages()

        # Calculate the target position for the follower drone
        follower_x = master_drone.master_position[0] + follower_distance * math.cos(follower_angle)
        follower_y = master_drone.master_position[1] + follower_distance * math.sin(follower_angle)
        follower_z = master_drone.master_position[2]

        # Send the target position to the master and follower drones
        master_drone.send_position_target(master_drone.master_position[0], master_drone.master_position[1], master_drone.master_position[2], 3, 0, 0)  # Constant speed of 3 m/s in the x direction
        follower_drone.send_position_target(follower_x, follower_y, follower_z, 3, 0, 0)  # Constant speed of 3 m/s in the x direction

        # Wait for 1 second before sending the next set of commands
        time.sleep(1)

        # Check if the user wants to abort the mission
        if abort():
            break

        # Check if the follower drone has reached the current waypoint
        if distance_between_points([follower_x, follower_y, follower_z], waypoint) < 1:
            print("Reached waypoint {}.".format(i + 1))
            break

for drone in [master_drone, follower_drone]:
    drone.connection.mav.command_long_send(
        drone.sysid,  # target_system
        mavutil.mavlink.MAV_COMP_ID_SYSTEM_CONTROL,  # target_component
        mavutil.mavlink.MAV_CMD_COMPONENT_ARM_DISARM,  # command
        0,  # confirmation
        0,  # disarm
        0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0  # unused parameters for this command
    )

master_drone.close()
follower_drone.close()

What are the Best Practices for I.T./System Administration Professionals to Handle Data and Maintain Confidentiality and Security Structures in Organization?
Exploratory Narrative
	My intellectual journey began when I realized there were specific aspects to the I.T./System Administration profession that I still needed to fully comprehend or be knowledgeable about. I wanted to avoid doing anything technical, leading me to remember confidentiality and security structures from classes and certification practices. Delving further into the field, I became particularly interested in confidentiality, security structures, and proper procedures and ethics when handling data and confidential information. Being well-versed in best practices for data handling and maintaining security structures within an organization is crucial. This prompted me to investigate the following research question, “What is the appropriate conduct for I.T./System Administration professionals in their interaction with and access to data?”
           Diving into various sources such as the EBSCO and U.H. library search, I found more information to help me form a thesis and ultimately support what I believe are guidelines that can be implemented in most organizations. I mainly found codes of ethics, business suggestions, and studies and reports on security guidelines and risk management. I learned that I.T./System Administration professionals must adhere to specific security protocols to preserve the integrity of their organization’s data. These protocols usually consisted of not breaking established security fields within an organization and implementing procedures and policies that maintain security without disrupting workflow. This necessitates a dedication to ongoing learning and professional growth and a comprehension of the ethical consequences of their work. This necessitates dedication to ongoing learning, accreditations, professional growth, and comprehending their work’s ethical and regulatory consequences.
           As I progressed in gathering information for my thesis, I became increasingly aware of the crucial role ethics and conducted play in the technology sector. I think it is essential for organizations to put guidelines in place that ensure their I.T./System Administration professionals are acting by the organization’s values. These guidelines should be grounded in best practices while considering the unique needs and challenges of the organization. Additionally, I identified the significance of cultivating a culture of accountability and responsibility within organizations. IT/System Administration professionals must remain vigilant and recognize the potential risks of mishandling sensitive data. Companies, in turn, would benefit from fostering a supportive environment that encourages open communication and feedback, empowering employees to advance their knowledge and improve their practices.
           My research has given me a more profound understanding of the significance of confidentiality, security structures, and professional conduct within the technology industry. Through research, I understand and can appreciate the more intricate issues involved and the importance of continuous learning and development in this field. I hope that my thesis will contribute to a better understanding of best practices for I.T./System Administration professionals and subsequently result in enhanced security and ethical standards for those in adjacent fields along with me in future preparation.
	As I prepare to enhance my knowledge further, I remain committed to promoting the necessity of comprehensive training programs and professional development opportunities. By encouraging individuals to explore new trends, emerging technologies, and innovative approaches, we can strive towards creating a more secure, ethical, and proficient technology landscape.

Reflection and Tentative Thesis
	In conclusion, the complex landscape of data privacy, confidentiality, and technological security presents numerous ethical challenges for I.T. and System Administration professionals.     display.insert(tk.END, text)

def retranslate():
    untranslated_text = untranslated_textbox.get(1.0, tk.END).strip()
    if untranslated_text:
        pyperclip.copy(untranslated_text)

def toggle_pause_monitoring():
    global pause_monitoring
    pause_monitoring = not pause_monitoring
    pause_button.config(
        text="Resume" if pause_monitoring else "Pause",
        relief=tk.SUNKEN if pause_monitoring else tk.RAISED
    )

def clipboard_monitor():
    global prompt_text
    old_clipboard = ""

    while True:
        if not pause_monitoring:
            new_clipboard = pyperclip.paste()

            # Remove line breaks from new_clipboard
            new_clipboard = new_clipboard.replace('\n', '').replace('\r', '')

            # Compare new_clipboard with old_clipboard to detect changes
            if new_clipboard != old_clipboard:
                old_clipboard = new_clipboard
                if new_clipboard in translated_dict:
                    translated_text = translated_dict[new_clipboard]
                else:
                    try:
                        prompt_text = prompt_textbox.get(1.0, tk.END).strip()
                        translated_text = translate_text(new_clipboard, prompt_text)
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(e, "Connection Error")
                        continue

                    translated_dict[new_clipboard] = translated_text
                    if KEEP_LOGS:
                        with open(jsonfile, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                            json.dump(translated_dict, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=4)

                update_display(untranslated_textbox, new_clipboard)
                update_display(translated_textbox, translated_text)

        time.sleep(1)

def toggle_translated():
    current_bg = translated_textbox.cget("background")
    if current_bg == "black":
        translated_textbox.configure(background="white")
        translated_textbox.delete(1.0, tk.END)
        try:
            translated_textbox.insert(tk.END, translated_dict[untranslated_textbox.get(1.0, tk.END).strip()])
        except Exception as e:
            pass
        toggle_button.config(text="Hide", relief=tk.RAISED)
    else:
        translated_textbox.configure(background="black")
        translated_textbox.delete(1.0, tk.END)
        toggle_button.config(text="Show", relief=tk.SUNKEN)

def save_prompt():
    global prompt_text
    current_prompt_text = prompt_textbox.get(1.0, tk.END).strip()
    if current_prompt_text != prompt_text:
        # Save any prompt_text changes to the log file
        prompt_text = current_prompt_text
        with open(prompt_logfile, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write(prompt_text)

def set_always_on_top():
    root.attributes("-topmost", always_on_top_var.get())

def increase_opacity():
    opacity = root.attributes("-alpha")
    opacity = min(opacity + 0.1, 1.0)
    root.attributes("-alpha", opacity)

def decrease_opacity():
    opacity = root.attributes("-alpha")
    opacity = max(opacity - 0.1, 0.1)
    root.attributes("-alpha", opacity)
    
def toggle_dark_mode():
    global dark_mode
    dark_mode = not dark_mode
    bg_color = "black" if dark_mode else "white"
    fg_color = "white" if dark_mode else "black"

    untranslated_textbox.configure(bg=bg_color, fg=fg_color)
    translated_textbox.configure(bg=bg_color, fg=fg_color)
    prompt_textbox.configure(bg=bg_color, fg=fg_color)
    root.configure(bg=bg_color)
    options_menu.configure(bg=bg_color, fg=fg_color)
    menu_bar.configure(bg=bg_color, fg=fg_color)

def font_window():
    global current_font
    current_font = {
        "family": font.Font(font=untranslated_textbox["font"]).actual("family"),
        "size": font.Font(font=untranslated_textbox["font"]).actual("size"),
        "color": untranslated_textbox.cget("foreground")
    }

    font_window = tk.Toplevel()
    font_window.title("Font Settings")
    font_window.geometry("150x150")

    font_family = tk.StringVar()
    font_family_label = tk.Label(font_window, text="Font Family")
    font_family_label.pack()
    font_family_entry = tk.Entry(font_window, textvariable=font_family)
    font_family_entry.insert(0, current_font["family"])
    font_family_entry.pack()

    font_size = tk.StringVar()
    font_size_label = tk.Label(font_window, text="Font Size")
    font_size_label.pack()
    font_size_entry = tk.Entry(font_window, textvariable=font_size)
    font_size_entry.insert(0, current_font["size"])
    font_size_entry.pack()

    font_color = tk.StringVar()
    font_color_label = tk.Label(font_window, text="Font Color")
    font_color_label.pack()
    font_color_entry = tk.Entry(font_window, textvariable=font_color)
    font_color_entry.insert(0, current_font["color"])
    font_color_entry.pack()

    def apply_changes():
        new_font_family = font_family.get() or current_font["family"]
        new_font_size = font_size.get() or current_font["size"]
        new_font_color = font_color.get() or current_font["color"]
        current_font = {"family": new_font_family, "size": new_font_size, "color": new_font_color}

        untranslated_textbox.configure(font=(current_font["family"], current_font["size"]), fg=current_font["color"])
        translated_textbox.configure(font=(current_font["family"], current_font["size"]), fg=current_font["color"])
        font_window.destroy()

    apply_button = tk.Button(font_window, text="Apply", command=apply_changes)
    apply_button.pack()

    cancel_button = tk.Button(font_window, text="Cancel", command=font_window.destroy)
    cancel_button.pack()

root = tk.Tk()
root.title("Clipboard Translator")
root.geometry("400x200")

menu_bar = Menu(root)

# Add Options menu
options_menu = Menu(menu_bar, tearoff=0)
menu_bar.add_cascade(label="Options", menu=options_menu)
options_menu.add_command(label="Save Prompt", command=save_prompt)
always_on_top_var = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
options_menu.add_checkbutton(label="Always on top", variable=always_on_top_var, command=set_always_on_top)
dark_mode_var = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
options_menu.add_checkbutton(label="Dark Mode", variable=dark_mode_var, command=toggle_dark_mode)
options_menu.add_command(label="Increase opacity", command=increase_opacity)
options_menu.add_command(label="Decrease opacity", command=decrease_opacity)

root.config(menu=menu_bar)

paned_window = tk.PanedWindow(root, sashrelief="groove", sashwidth=5, orient="horizontal")

paned_window.add(untranslated_textbox)

translated_textbox = tk.Text(paned_window, wrap="word")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("Starting Clipboard Translator…\n")
    clipboard_monitor_thread = threading.Thread(target=clipboard_monitor)
    clipboard_monitor_thread.start()
    root.mainloop()

summarise this: There are three forms of the personal name based on Hermanubis in papyri and inscriptions from Egypt, in addition to two "proper" polytheophoric names. (In four instances [1, 5, 17, 21] the ending of the name is abbreviated or not preserved.) The form Ἑρμάνουβιϲ, attested six times (2, 9, 11, 13, 14, 15), simply replicates the god's name without further ado, through the phenomenon of "direct theonymy" (the giving to humans of unaltered divine names) that became widespread in the Roman period.[8] In an odd instance (9) the form Ἑρμανουβιϲ in the nominative is borne by a woman, an Alexandrian nurse (ἡ τροφόϲ).[9] It is highly unusual for female theophoric names to be unmarked for gender in this way.[10] I suspect that because the element -ανουβιϲ in Ἑρμάνουβιϲ was often declined with a dental declension in -ιδ-, it might have been conflated with the similar declension of Greek feminine names in -ίϲ, -ίδοϲ.[11] If this is correct, it may be preferable to read the nurse's name as an oxytone (Ἑρμανουβίϲ). There are two further forms based on the god Hermanubis: Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (six instances: 3, 4, 7, 8, 12, 20) and Ἑρμανουβίων (four instances: 3, 6, 18, 19). -ᾶϲ and -ίων were, in fact, the commonest Greek suffixes for personal names based on the Egyptian god Ἄνουβιϲ.[12] It is interesting that in a business letter (3) the two forms Ἑρμανουβίων (l.38) and Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (l.41) are used interchangeably to designate the same individual. In this case, Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ is probably a hypocoristic of Ἑρμανουβίων, since the suffix -ᾶϲ often served this purpose.[13]
