Make three slides for the following text that will take roughly 4 minutes to present: Also give me a script to read. “Global Social Commerce Trend
According to a report by eMarketer, the global social commerce market is expected to grow from $492 billion in 2021 to $1,2 trillion by 2025. This indicates that the market is both big and rapidly increasing. In fact, the social commerce market is estimated to grow three times the speed of the traditional e-commerce market during this period (Insider Intelligence, 2023). The high growth may not seem as surprising when taking into consideration how many potential customers there are on the market. According to Statista, there were 4.59 billion social media users worldwide in 2022, and this number is expected to reach 5.42 billion by 2025 (Dixon, 2023). Social media platforms like Facebook, Instagram, Pinterest and TikTok have an enormous user base all over the globe, making them an attractive option for businesses to promote and sell their products. 
While social commerce was a big phenomenon previous to the COVID-19 pandemic, the pandemic accelerated the growth even further. With lockdowns and social distancing measures in place, more consumers have turned to online shopping, including social commerce. The growth of all types e-commerce was analyzed to be accelerated the equivalent of a projected 5 years growth in the first year of the pandemic alone. As social commerce was part in that big leap, many consumers formed the habit of shopping on social media during the pandemic (Hutchinson, 2021). 
The trends and use of social commerce is rather different around the world. Thus we will focus on the different markets separately. Thus we will dive deeper into the social commerce trends in the three regions with the biggest social commerce markets. (Fortune Business Insights, 2022)
Asia-Pacific:
The Asia-Pacific region is on the cutting edge of social commerce development. 49% of the world’s social commerce sales were generated in the Asia-Pacific region, primarily driven by China (Fortune Business Insights, 2022). China is the undisputed leader in social commerce. The social medias used in china differ quite a lot from social media platforms used in other countries, which also changes how social commerce is used. In China there are several examples of e-commerce sites that successfully implements social features rather than the other way around, such as XiaoHongShu (Schaffer, 2022) and Alibaba with Taobao. These are primarily e-commerce sites that also future livestreaming, e-commerce, and community-building. However, some large social media platforms in China such as WeChat are also offering social commerce by utilizing mini-apps to enable consumers to shop directly from their feeds (Schaffer, 2022). In China, live commerce is a big trend and can be seen as a subset of social commerce. This is when influential people livestream on platforms such as Taobao and encourage their followers to purchase the showcased products directly from the site. (McKinsey, 2021) In addition to China, several other Asian countries are embracing social commerce. In India the social commerce market is expected to grow by 71,5% annually. Similar to China, social commerce is big on platforms that are unique to the country, for example, Meesho (Research and Markets, 2022). Also, Thailand has a large market for social commerce which will be discussed more in detail in a later section. 
North America:
In North America, social commerce is much smaller than in Asia-Pacific, however, rapidly growing. Social commerce sales are projected to represent 5% of total e-commerce sales in the US market in 2025. Meanwhile, in China, 13% of total e-commerce sales came from social commerce already in 2021. Considering the success of social commerce in China, and that the conversion rate has been up to ten times as high as with traditional e-commerce, different actors in North America are making efforts to introduce social commerce to the region (McKinsey, 2022). 
Platforms such as Instagram, Facebook, and Pinterest have introduced shopping features to enable consumers to purchase products directly from their feeds. For instance, Instagram which is one of the major social media platforms in North America introduced the shop button in Novermber 2021 (Hart, 2022). In North America, the trend seems to be that e-commerce features, such as shop buttons related to posts, are added to existing large social media platforms. 
However, a reason why these efforts have not yet achieved the same level of success as in Asia could be due to the large entrenched power of e-commerce platforms such as Amazon. Amazon is very much a pure e-commerce platform with limited social features. In December they introduced a social commerce feature called “Inspire” which was a feed combining short videos with shopping. It was introduced as late as of December 2022 (Marketplace Pulse, 2022) and has not seen any major traction so far.
A second reason why social commerce still has not seen the same breakthrough in the North American market could be due to the lack of live commerce efforts, which is a big part of social commerce in Asia. In China, 10% of all e-commerce sales comes from live commerce, and thus makes up the majority share of the social commerce market. Major american brands such as Walmart and Tommy Hilfiger have had live streaming events on social platforms. However, there is no big platform in the North American market directly focused on live commerce (McKinsey, 2021). 
Europe:
The European social commerce market is very similar to the one in the North American market. This is because most European countries resort to similar social media platforms as consumers in North America. Thus, the spread of social commerce in Europe is very much dependent upon the moves of major actors such as Facebook, Instagram, Pintrest and Twitter. Similar to the North American market, popular platforms such as Instagram and Facebook are introducing shopping functions in the European market. 
In conclusion, the global trends in social commerce demonstrate a rapidly growing market with significant regional variations. The Asia-Pacific region, and particularly China, leads the way in social commerce development, driven by unique platforms, live commerce, and a blend of e-commerce and social features. China’s success in social commerce can be attributed to its innovative integration of live streaming, community-building, and in-app shopping experiences in both e-commerce and social media platforms. In contrast, the North American and European markets have mainly focused on integrating parts of these social commerce features into existing social media platforms. The question now remains whether the large success of social commerce can have the same success in other markets than China. This makes the case of Thailand an interesting example.”

The name Hermanubis is a “polytheophoric” name, combining the names of two deities, and is popular among males of the middle and upper classes in Roman Egypt. However, unlike other polytheophoric names, Hermanubis refers to a specific deity that is independently attested in Roman Egypt. The article discusses the onomastic evidence of the name and compares it to the deity’s iconographical and documentary attestations.
The name Ἑρμάνουβιϲ is primarily found in Roman period literature and inscriptions, and is believed to be a result of the assimilation of the Egyptian god Anubis to the Greek god Hermes. This resulted in depictions of Anubis with attributes of Hermes, and later, a fully anthropomorphic young god was depicted with Anubis’ attributes and a calathos headdress. This type is traditionally labeled “Hermanubis” by art historians, but no inscription identifying it as such has been found.
There are different forms of the personal name based on Hermanubis, including Ἑρμάνουβιϲ, Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ, and Ἑρμανουβίων, which were commonly used in papyri and inscriptions from Egypt. In some cases, the names were abbreviated or not preserved, and in one instance, a woman bore the nominative form of the name. It is possible that the feminine ending -ανουβιϲ was conflated with similar endings of Greek feminine names. Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ and Ἑρμανουβίων were commonly used suffixes for personal names based on the Egyptian god Ἄνουβιϲ. In one business letter, the two forms were used interchangeably to designate the same individual. It is possible that Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ is a hypocoristic of Ἑρμανουβίων.
The name “Hermanubis” is different from other polytheophoric names because it refers to a single god, not a combination of two deities. Names like “Ἑρμανοβάμμων” and “Ἡρακλερμάνουβιϲ” combine the name of Hermanubis with other gods, but they are not triple theophoric names, as they only combine two gods.
The majority of the names based on Hermanubis appear in papyri from the second and third centuries AD. The earliest example is possibly from an ostracon dated to AD 94, but the name is abbreviated. There are eight instances from the second century, eight from the third, and three from the fourth. The name disappears from evidence after the fourth century, corresponding to the chronological distribution of iconographical representations of Hermanubis. These representations range from the reign of Trajan to the Tetrarchic period and have all been assigned to the second or third century AD.
This article discusses the geographical distribution of names based on Hermanubis found in papyri from all over Egypt. Most individuals bearing these names are found to be from Alexandria or Cynopolite, which coincides with the evidence of Hermanubis being a cult center in Alexandria and a connection to Anubis in Cynopolite. The article also mentions various archaeological and iconographic evidence found in these areas, such as an inscription and statuette of Hermanubis in the Alexandrian Serapeum precinct, a famous statue found in the sanctuary of Ras el-Soda in Alexandria, and Alexandrian coins showing Hermanubis standing in a temple believed to be the Serapeum precinct.
The onomastic survey reveals three conclusions: Hermanubis names are distinct from other polytheophoric names because they refer to a specific god, Hermanubis, who is a hybrid-named version of Anubis; the overlap between the onomastic evidence and representations of Hermanubis supports art historians’ designation of the latter as “Hermanubis”; and Hermanubis is closely associated with the traditional Anubis, suggesting that they should not be separated too forcefully.
The name Hermanubis is found once outside of Egypt in an inscription from Dion in Macedonia. The form of the name there is not known from Egypt, and is likely related to the cult of Hermanubis attested in Thessalonike. This suggests that the cult of Hermanubis was popular outside of its original home in the Roman period.

The names in 838-839 may be fictional and part of a satire to associate Egyptians with their pagan past. Most of the names are Egyptian except for Sippas and Rhodon. The occurrence of Anubis near Hermanubis suggests their near equivalence, but the use of the adjective “kunodeis” in 840 does not necessarily mean Hermanubis is a canine-headed god.
The name Hermanubis is used for satirical effect in a poorly executed anonymous poem in the Greek Anthology, which is a diatribe against a strategos.
Νῦν ὁ ϲτρατηγὸϲ Ἑρμανούβηϲ ἐγένετο
κύων ἀδελφοὺϲ ϲυλλαβὼν Ἑρμᾶϲ δύο

The strategos Hermanoubes became a dog after capturing two thieves and the place does not determine a person’s character, but rather their character reveals the place.
The word Ἑρμανούβηϲ is not found in any documents, leading some to suggest it should be emended to Ἑρμάνουβιϲ. It is unclear whether the word is a subject or predicate in the poem, but if the general was actually named Hermanoubes/Hermanubis, the satire would be stronger due to associations with Anubis and Hermes. The meaning of the word κύων is also unclear, as well as the relationship between the strategos and the two thievish Hermeses.
This passage discusses the origins and parallels of Hermanubis, a syncretic god combining the Egyptian god Anubis and the Greek god Hermes. It notes that while it was not common in traditional Greek religion to combine the names of two gods in this manner, there were similar examples in earlier periods, including the god Hermaphroditos (combining Hermes and Aphrodite) and the Carian god Zeus Osogoa (combining Zeus and Poseidon). The passage also explores the association of Anubis with Hermes in literary and epigraphic sources, and discusses the possible origins of the name Hermanubis.
There are three exceptions to the rule that place names in Greek ending in -cleia or -polis are masculine, two of which end in -hērakleios and one in -apollōnios.
The ending “-άμμων” was commonly added to the names of deities in theophoric names, making it unlikely that the combination of “Ἑρμάνουβιϲ” had any specific significance. The name “Ἡρακλερμάνουβιϲ” is more difficult to explain, as it may simply juxtapose the chief god of a nome with Hermanubis without implying an affinity between the two. The form “Ἑρμανούβιοϲ” does not appear in Egyptian documents, and the Macedonian inscription is the only instance of this form. There are numismatic representations of “Hermanubis” with a radiating crown and the horn of Ammon, as well as representations of the dog-headed Anubis with a club. The acting epistrategus Hermanubis mentioned may have originated from Alexandria. A female name “Ἑρμανουβίαινα” was also recently discovered. More research needs to be conducted on the iconography of Hermanubis.
The passage discusses various ancient Greek and Egyptian names, some of which may have been invented or satirical. It also references Gregory’s contrasting views on Egypt’s past animal worship and its conversion to Christianity. The passage cites multiple sources and scholars, including studies on theophoric names, syncretism in Egyptian religion, and Greek personal names.

I research startup Anthropic aims to raise as much as $5 billion over the next two years to take on rival OpenAI and enter over a dozen major industries, according to company documents obtained by TechCrunch.

A pitch deck for Anthropic’s Series C fundraising round discloses these and other long-term goals for the company, which was founded in 2020 by former OpenAI researchers.

In the deck, Anthropic says that it plans to build a “frontier model” — tentatively called “Claude-Next” — 10 times more capable than today’s most powerful AI, but that this will require a billion dollars in spending over the next 18 months.

When contacted for comment, an Anthropic spokesperson said: “We are planning additional product announcements and will be talking about them soon.”

The Information reported in early March that Anthropic was seeking to raise $300 million at $4.1 billion valuation, bringing its total raised to $1.3 billion. The deck confirms that target number, though only half was raised at the time of the document’s creation from a “confidential investor.”

Anthropic describes the frontier model as a “next-gen algorithm for AI self-teaching,” making reference to an AI training technique it developed called “constitutional AI.” At a high level, constitutional AI seeks to provide a way to align AI with human intentions — letting systems respond to questions and perform tasks using a simple set of guiding principles.

Anthropic estimates its frontier model will require on the order of 10^25 FLOPs, or floating point operations — several orders of magnitude larger than even the biggest models today. Of course, how this translates to computation time depends on the speed and scale of the system doing the computation; Anthropic implies (in the deck) it relies on clusters with “tens of thousands of GPUs.”


This frontier model could be used to build virtual assistants that can answer emails, perform research and generate art, books and more, some of which we have already gotten a taste of with the likes of GPT-4 and other large language models.

“These models could begin to automate large portions of the economy,” the pitch deck reads. “We believe that companies that train the best 2025/26 models will be too far ahead for anyone to catch up in subsequent cycles.”

The frontier model is the successor to Claude, Anthropic’s chatbot that can be instructed to perform a range of tasks, including searching across documents, summarizing, writing and coding, and answering questions about particular topics. In these ways, it’s similar to OpenAI’s ChatGPT. But Anthropic makes the case that Claude is — thanks to constitutional AI — “much less likely to produce harmful outputs,” “easier to converse with” and “more steerable.”

Anthropic released Claude commercially in March following a closed beta late last year, allowing around 15 partners initial access. It counts among its beta users and potential customers the following industries (with the asterisk indicating that a human is in the loop to supervise the model):

Legal document summary and analysis*
Medical patient records and analysis*
Customer service emails and chat
Coding models for consumers and B2B
Productivity-related search, document editing and content generation*
Chatbot for public Q&A and advice
Search employing natural language responses
HR tasks like job descriptions and interview analysis*
Therapy and coaching
Virtual assistants*
Education at all levels*
Dario Amodei, the former VP of research at OpenAI, launched Anthropic in 2021 as a public benefit corporation, taking with him a number of OpenAI employees, including OpenAI’s former policy lead Jack Clark. Amodei split from OpenAI after a disagreement over the company’s direction, namely the startup’s increasingly commercial focus.

Anthropic now competes with OpenAI as well as startups like Cohere and AI21 Labs, all of which are developing and productizing their own text-generating — and in some cases image-generating — AI systems. OpenAI has by far raised the most in terms of capital, recently securing a reported $10 billion from Microsoft at a $29 billion.

“Anthropic has been heavily focused on research for the first year and a half of its existence, but we have been convinced of the necessity of commercialization, which we fully committed to in September [2022],” the pitch deck reads. “We’ve developed a strategy for go-to-market and initial product specialization that fits with our core expertise, brand and where we see adoption occurring over the next 12 months.”

The pitch deck reveals that Alameda Research Ventures, the sister firm of Sam Bankman-Fried’s collapsed cryptocurrency startup FTX, was a “silent investor” in Anthropic with “non-voting” shares — responsible for spearheading Anthropic’s $580 million Series B round. Anthropic expects Alameda’s shares to be disposed of in bankruptcy proceedings within the next few years.

Google is also among Anthropic’s investors, having pledged $300 million in Anthropic for a 10% stake in the startup. Under the terms of the deal, which was first reported by the Financial Times, Anthropic agreed to make Google Cloud its “preferred cloud provider” with the companies “co-develop[ing] AI computing systems.”

Other Anthropic backers include James McClave, Facebook and Asana co-founder Dustin Moskovitz, former Google CEO Eric Schmidt and founding Skype engineer Jaan Tallinn.

import socket
import threading
import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import filedialog
import logging
import time

logging.basicConfig(filename='TCPClient.log', level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s:%(levelname)s:%(message)s')

packet_file_path = ''

# 读取文件中的16进制报文
def read_hex_packets(file_path):
    packets = []
    try:
        with open(file_path, "r") as f:
            lines = f.readlines()
            for line in lines:
                # 缩进块要注意缩进的空格数,通常为四个空格
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                try:
                    packet = bytes.fromhex(line)
                except ValueError:
                    logging.error("无效的报文格式: %s", line)
                    continue
                packets.append(packet)
    except IOError as e:
        logging.error("读取文件 %s 失败: %s", file_path, str(e))
    return packets

# 发送报文并打印发送和接收的报文
def send_packets():
    # 获取输入的服务器ip和端口号
    server_ip = server_ip_entry.get()
    server_port = int(server_port_entry.get())

    # 获取是否开启"发送每一帧报文之后都等待服务器回传数据"功能的勾选状态
    wait_for_response = wait_for_response_var.get()

    # 获取报文发送间隔
    send_interval = int(send_interval_entry.get())

    # 读取16进制报文
    packets = read_hex_packets(packet_file_path)

    # 创建TCP客户端socket
    try:
        client_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    except socket.error as e:
        logging.error("创建socket失败: %s", str(e))
        return

    try:
        # 连接服务器
        client_socket.connect((server_ip, server_port))

        # 清空发送和接收框
        text.delete(1.0,tk.END)
        response_text.delete(1.0,tk.END)

        for i, packet in enumerate(packets):
            # 发送报文
            try:
                client_socket.sendall(packet)
            except socket.error as e:
                logging.error("发送第%d帧报文失败: %s", i+1, str(e))
                text.insert(tk.END, "发送第%d帧报文失败: %s\n" % (i+1, str(e)))
                continue
            logging.info("发送第%d帧报文: %s", i+1, packet.hex())
            text.insert(tk.END, "发送第%d帧报文: %s\n" % (i+1, packet.hex()))

            # 等待服务器回传
            if wait_for_response:
                try:
                    response_packet = client_socket.recv(1024)
                    logging.info("收到第%d帧回传报文: %s", i+1, response_packet.hex())
                    response_text.insert(tk.END, response_packet.hex() + "\n")
                except socket.error as e:
                    logging.error("接收第%d帧回传报文失败: %s", i+1, str(e))
                    response_text.insert(tk.END, "接收第%d帧回传报文失败: %s\n" % (i+1, str(e)))

            # 等待一定时间再发送下一帧
            if i != len(packets)-1:
                time.sleep((send_interval + 500) / 1000) # 加上500ms的传输延迟

    except socket.error as e:
        logging.error(str(e))

    finally:
        # 关闭socket连接
        client_socket.close()

# 打开文件对话框
def open_file_dialog():
    global packet_file_path
    packet_file_path = filedialog.askopenfilename(filetypes=(("Text files", "*.txt"),))
    file_path_label.config(text=packet_file_path)

# 创建GUI界面
root = tk.Tk()
root.title("TCP客户端")

# 服务器ip和端口号输入框
server_frame = tk.Frame()
server_frame.pack(side=tk.TOP, padx=10, pady=10)
server_ip_label = tk.Label(server_frame, text="服务器IP: ")
server_ip_label.pack(side=tk.LEFT)
server_ip_entry = tk.Entry(server_frame, width=15)
server_ip_entry.pack(side=tk.LEFT)
server_port_label = tk.Label(server_frame, text="服务器端口号: ")
server_port_label.pack(side=tk.LEFT)
server_port_entry = tk.Entry(server_frame, width=5)
server_port_entry.pack(side=tk.LEFT)

# 选择报文文件和发送间隔输入框
file_frame = tk.Frame()
file_frame.pack(side=tk.TOP, padx=10, pady=10)
open_file_button = tk.Button(file_frame, text="选择报文文件", command=open_file_dialog)
open_file_button.pack(side=tk.LEFT)
file_path_label = tk.Label(file_frame, text="尚未选择文件")
file_path_label.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=10)
send_interval_label = tk.Label(file_frame, text="发送间隔(ms): ")
send_interval_label.pack(side=tk.LEFT)
send_interval_entry = tk.Entry(file_frame, width=5)
send_interval_entry.pack(side=tk.LEFT)

# "发送每一帧报文之后都等待服务器回传数据"勾选框
wait_for_response_var = tk.BooleanVar()
wait_for_response_checkbox = tk.Checkbutton(root, text="发送每一帧报文之后都等待服务器回传数据", variable=wait_for_response_var)
wait_for_response_checkbox.pack(side=tk.TOP)

# 发送和接收框
text_frame = tk.Frame()
text_frame.pack(side=tk.TOP, padx=10, pady=10)
text = tk.Text(text_frame, height=10, width=50)
text.pack(side=tk.LEFT)
response_text = tk.Text(text_frame, height=10, width=50)
response_text.pack(side=tk.RIGHT)

# 发送按钮
send_button = tk.Button(root, text="发送", command=send_packets)
send_button.pack(side=tk.BOTTOM, pady=10)

root.mainloop()

''Hijab addresses the argument that the Quran, in Chapter 23 Verse 14, erroneously claims that human beings are made up of congealed blood. In his attempt to counter these claims, he presents two primary contentions.

The first contention he addresses relates to the meaning of the word “alaqah .” Hijab argues that critics have incorrectly interpreted this term. He points out that medieval scholars understood “alaqah ” to mean “attached” or “connected,” rather than congealed blood. As such, the critics’ interpretation of this concept is flawed.
The second contention Hijab discusses concerns the interpretation of the term “congealed blood.” He asserts that this understanding is unscientific and demonstrates a weak comprehension of the Arabic language. According to Hijab, the more accurate translation of the word “alaqah ,” as used in the Quran, is a “leech-like clot.” This term more closely aligns with the early stages of embryonic development, as shown by modern science.

To further bolster his argument, Hijab refers to a Hadith (Sunan Ibn Majah 3314) which discusses two things that have become permissible for Muslims: the liver (alcabid) and the spleen. In the Hadith, these organs are referred to as “two dems,” an Arabic term meaning “blood.” Although the liver is not composed primarily of blood, the Prophet Muhammad used the term to describe it due to its blood-like appearance. This example illustrates how religious texts often employ metaphorical language that should not be interpreted literally.
Hijab then questions why the Quran uses the term “janine” (fetus) instead of a phrase that more literally translates to “congealed clot of blood.” He posits that the Quran’s choice of language aligns better with modern embryology and reflects the multi-layered nature of the Quran, which aims to communicate with people from various times and places. This, according to Hijab, demonstrates how the Quran employs language that is adaptable and understandable across different epochs and cultures, rather than confining itself to the scientific knowledge of a specific.''

''The explanations provided by Hijab in defense of the apparent scientific inaccuracy in the Quran seem to be inadequate and cannot withstand careful examination.
Firstly, while it is true that “alaqah ” can mean “attached” or “connected,” it is also widely translated as “clot” or “congealed blood” in classical Arabic dictionaries and Islamic commentaries. An example is the respected 14th-century commentator Ibn Kathir, who explained that “alaqah ” refers to a “clot of blood that adheres to the womb.” As a result, the understanding of “alaqah ” as “congealed blood” is not a recent innovation but has been accepted by Islamic scholars for centuries.
Additionally, Hijab’s claim that the term “congealed blood” does not accurately depict the early stages of embryonic development is not entirely correct. In the early stages, the embryo is a tiny cluster of cells that indeed resembles a blood clot. It only takes on a more recognizable form as it develops further.
Hijab’s use of a Hadith to support his argument also seems to be flawed. The Hadith he cites does not reinforce his contention that metaphorical language in religious texts should not be taken literally. Instead, it demonstrates that the Prophet Muhammad employed a figure of speech to describe the liver and spleen as “two dems,” an Arabic term meaning “two bloods.” This example does not imply that the liver and spleen are literally made of blood, but rather that they have a similar appearance or function.
Lastly, Hijab’s argument that the Quran’s use of the term “janine” is more in line with modern embryology than a term more closely translating to “congealed clot of blood” is unfounded. While “janine” does mean “fetus” and is a more specific term than “clot,” it does not negate the fact that the Quran describes the early stages of embryonic development as resembling a blood clot.
In conclusion, Quran 23:14 presents a clear scientific error, and attempts to justify it through reinterpreting words or invoking metaphorical language are unconvincing.''

从刑法角度分析和比较这两个案例 并写成一篇文章 要求2500字以上这是第一个案例 昆山龙哥案：
基本案情
2018年8月27日21时30分许，于海明骑自行车在江苏省昆山市震川路正常行驶，刘某醉酒驾驶小轿车（经检测，血液酒精含量87mg/100ml）向右强行闯入非机动车道，与于海明险些碰擦。刘某的一名同车人员下车与于海明争执，经同行人员劝解返回时，刘某突然下车，上前推搡、踢打于海明。虽经劝解，刘某仍持续追打，并从轿车内取出一把砍刀（系管制刀具），连续用刀面击打于海明颈部、腰部、腿部。
刘某在击打过程中将砍刀甩脱，于海明抢到砍刀，刘某上前争夺，在争夺中于海明捅刺刘某的腹部、臀部，砍击其右胸、左肩、左肘。刘某受伤后跑向轿车，于海明继续追砍2刀均未砍中，其中1刀砍中轿车。刘某跑离轿车，于海明返回轿车，将车内刘某的手机取出放入自己口袋。民警到达现场后，于海明将手机和砍刀交给出警民警（于海明称，拿走刘某的手机是为了防止对方打电话召集人员报复）。刘某逃离后，倒在附近绿化带内，后经送医抢救无效，因腹部大静脉等破裂致失血性休克于当日死亡。于海明经人身检查，见左颈部条形挫伤1处、左胸季肋部条形挫伤1处。
2018年9月1日，江苏省昆山市公安局根据侦查查明的事实，依据《中华人民共和国刑法》第二十条第三款的规定，认定于海明的行为属于正当防卫，不负刑事责任，决定依法撤销于海明故意伤害案。其间，公安机关依据相关规定，听取了检察机关的意见，昆山市人民检察院同意公安机关的撤销案件决定 [1]  [4]  。
相关法条《中华人民共和国刑法》第二十条：正当防卫
为了使国家、公共利益、本人或者他人的人身、财产和其他权利免受正在进行的不法侵害，而采取的制止不法侵害的行为，对不法侵害人造成损害的，属于正当防卫，不负刑事责任。
正当防卫明显超过必要限度造成重大损害的，应当负刑事责任，但是应当减轻或者免除处罚。
对正在进行行凶、杀人、抢劫、强奸、绑架以及其他严重危及人身安全的暴力犯罪，采取防卫行为，造成不法侵害人伤亡的，不属于防卫过当，不负刑事责任。
条文注释
正当防卫的要件有以下五个方面：
（1）实际的不法侵害存在；
（2）不法侵害必须正在进行；
（3）必须是为了使国家、公共利益、本人或他人的人身、财产和其他权利免受不法侵害；
（4）防卫行为必须针对不法侵害人进行；
（5）防卫行为没有明显超过必要限度，造成重大损害。
“防卫过当”应当同时具备以下特征：
（1）“防卫过当”必须是明显地超过必要限度。所谓“必要限度”是指为有效地制止不法侵害所必需的防卫的强度。“明显超过必要限度”是指一般人都能够认识到其防卫强度已经超过了正当防卫所必需的强度；
（2）对不法侵害人造成了重大损害。“重大损害”是指由于防卫人明显超过必要限度的防卫行为造成不法侵害人或者其他人人身伤亡及其他能够避免的严重的损害。防卫过当的行为应当负刑事责任，但对防卫过当的行为毕竟不能等同于犯罪行为，应当减轻或者免除处罚。
本条第三款针对一些暴力犯罪的特点，对正当防卫的构成作了有条件的放宽。在法定的几种情况下，《刑法》允许正当防卫可以造成伤害和死亡的结果 [3]  。
裁判理由编辑 播报
根据通报，认定于海明属于正当防卫的主要理由之一是刘海龙的行为属于刑法意义上的“行凶”。根据《刑法》第二十条第三款规定，判断“行凶”的核心在于是否严重危及人身安全。司法实践中，考量是否属于“行凶”，不能苛求防卫人在应急反应情况下作出理性判断，更不能以防卫人遭受实际伤害为前提，而要根据现场具体情景及社会一般人的认知水平进行判断。本案中，刘海龙先是徒手攻击，继而持刀连续击打，其行为已经严重危及于海明人身安全，其不法侵害应认定为“行凶”。
此外，刘海龙的不法侵害是一个持续的过程。纵观本案，在同车人员与于海明争执基本平息的情况下，刘海龙醉酒滋事，先是下车对于海明拳打脚踢，后又返回车内取出砍刀，对于海明连续数次击打，不法侵害不断升级。刘海龙砍刀甩落在地后，又上前抢刀。刘海龙被致伤后，仍没有放弃侵害的迹象。于海明的人身安全一直处在刘海龙的暴力威胁之中。
最后，于海明的行为出于防卫目的。本案中，于海明夺刀后，7秒内捅刺、砍中刘海龙的5刀，与追赶时甩击、砍击的两刀（未击中），尽管时间上有间隔、空间上有距离，但这是一个连续行为。另外，于海明停止追击，返回宝马轿车搜寻刘海龙手机的目的是防止对方纠集人员报复、保护自己的人身安全，符合正当防卫的意图。
另根据昆山市检察院发布的通报称，检察机关认为，根据我国刑法第二十条第三款规定和已查明的事实，本案中，死者刘海龙持刀行凶，于海明为使本人人身权利免受正在进行的暴力侵害，对侵害人刘海龙采取制止暴力侵害的行为，属于正当防卫，其防卫行为造成刘海龙死亡，不负刑事责任。公安机关对此案作撤案处理符合法律规定。这是第二个案例：于欢案：2016年4月14日，由社会闲散人员组成的10多人的催债队伍多次骚扰苏银霞的工厂，辱骂、殴打苏银霞。案发前一天，吴学占在苏已抵押的房子里，指使手下拉屎，将苏银霞按进马桶里，要求其还钱。当日下午，苏银霞四次拨打110和市长热线，但并没有得到帮助 [1]  。
催债的手段升级，苏银霞和儿子于欢，连同一名职工，被带到公司接待室限制人身自由，11名催债人员围堵并控制了他们三人。其间，催债人员用不堪入耳的羞辱性话语辱骂苏银霞，并脱下于欢的鞋子捂在他母亲嘴上，甚至故意将烟灰弹到苏银霞的胸口。催债人员杜志浩甚至脱下裤子，露出下体，侮辱苏银霞，令于欢濒临崩溃。外面路过的工人看到这一幕，让于欢的姑妈于秀荣报警 [1]  。
警察接警后到接待室，说了一句“要账可以，但是不能动手打人”，随即离开。看到警察要离开，报警的于秀荣拉住一名女警，并试图拦住警车。“警察这时候走了，他娘俩只有死路一条。”于秀荣在后来接受记者采访时说。被催债人员控制的于欢看到警察要走，情绪崩溃，站起来试图冲到屋外唤回警察，被催债人员拦住。混乱中，于欢从接待室的桌子上摸到一把水果刀乱捅，致使杜志浩等四名催债人员被捅伤。其中，杜志浩因未及时就医导致失血性休克死亡，另两人重伤，一人轻伤 [1]  。
二审案件审理
1.从防卫意图看，于欢的捅刺行为是为了保护本人及其母亲合法的权益而实施的。为了保护合法的权益，这是正当防卫的目的性条件。合法的权益，并不限于生命健康，还包括人身自由、人格尊严等其他合法权益。本案中，于欢在认识到自己和母亲的人身自由、人格尊严受到严重不法侵害、人身安全受到严重威胁的情况下，持刀捅刺杜志浩等人的行为，正是为了保护自己和母亲的人身自由、人格尊严、人身安全等合法权益免受不法侵害而实施的。一审判决书认为，“对方均未有人使用工具、派出所已经出警、其生命健康权被侵犯的现实危险性较小”，这一法律评价虽关注到生命健康权，但忽视了对于欢及其母亲人身自由、人格尊严等合法权益的保护，是对正当防卫保护对象的错误理解。
2.从防卫起因看，本案存在持续性、复合性、严重性的现实不法侵害。针对不法侵害行为才能实施防卫，这是正当防卫的前提条件。这里的不法侵害，既可以是犯罪行为，也可以是一般违法行为，包括对非法拘禁，公民可以进行防卫。本案中，杜志浩等人并不是苏银霞高利贷借款的直接债权人，而是被赵荣荣纠集前去违法讨债。对讨债一方的不法侵害行为，必须整体把握。在案证据证实，讨债方存在持续进行的严重不法侵害行为，按时间顺序可分三个阶段：一是2016年4月1日赵荣荣等人非法侵入于欢家住宅、4月13日擅自将于欢住宅家电等物品搬运至源大公司堆放，吴学占将苏银霞头部强行按入马桶；二是2016年4月14日下午至当晚民警处警，讨债方采取盯守、围困等行为限制剥夺于欢、苏银霞人身自由，实施辱骂、脱裤暴露下体在苏银霞面前摆动侮辱等严重侵害于欢、苏银霞人格尊严的行为，采用扇拍于欢面颊、揪抓于欢头发、按压于欢不准起身等行为侵害于欢人身权利，收走于欢、苏银霞的手机，阻断其与外界的联系，在源大公司办公楼门厅前烧烤饮酒扰乱企业生产秩序；三是从处警民警离开接待室至于欢持刀捅刺之前，讨债方持续阻止于欢、苏银霞离开接待室，强迫于欢坐下，并将于欢推搡至接待室东南角。这三个阶段的多种不法侵害行为，具有持续性且不断升级，已经涉嫌非法拘禁违法犯罪和对人身的侵害行为。面对这些严重的不法侵害行为，于欢为了制止这些不法侵害，反击围在其身边正在实施不法侵害的加害人，完全具有防卫的前提。聊城市检察院起诉书没有认定作为防卫起因，聊城市中级法院一审判决书认为“不存在正当防卫意义的不法侵害前提”，是错误的。
3.从防卫时间看，于欢的行为是针对正在进行的不法侵害实施的。防卫适时，是正当防卫的时间性条件。本案中，处警民警离开接待室是案件的转折点。民警出警本应使事态缓和，不法侵害得到有效制止。但在案证据证实，杜志浩一方对于欢的不法侵害行为，没有因为民警出警得到控制和停止，相反又进一步升级。在苏银霞、于欢急于随民警离开接待室时，杜志浩一方为不让于欢离开，对于欢又实施了勒脖子、按肩膀等强制行为，并将于欢强制推搡到接待室的东南角，使于欢处于更加孤立无援的状态。于欢持刀捅刺杜志浩等人时，不法侵害的现实危险性不仅存在，而且不断累积升高，于欢面对的境况更加危险。如果他不持刀制止杜志浩一方的不法侵害，他遭受的侵害行为将会更加严重。于欢在持刀发出警告无效后，捅刺了围在身边的人。一审判决书认定“不存在防卫的紧迫性”，显然是对矛盾激化的原因作出了错误的判断，这也是在认定事实不全面情况下得出的错误认定。
4.从防卫对象看，于欢是针对不法侵害人本人进行的反击。针对不法侵害人本人实施防卫行为，这是正当防卫的对象性条件。这里的不法侵害人本人，是指不法侵害的实施者和共犯。本案中，于欢持刀捅刺的对象，包括了杜志浩、程学贺、严建军、郭彦刚四人。在案证据证实，这四人均属于参与违法讨债、涉嫌非法拘禁犯罪的共同行为人，杜志浩还在非法拘禁过程中实施了污秽语言辱骂和暴露阴部、扇拍于欢面部等严重侮辱行为。虽然没有证据证实严建军、郭彦刚、程学贺三人对于欢母子有言语侮辱和暴力殴打行为，但他们围挡在于欢身边且在杜志浩被捅刺后仍然没有走开，同样限制了于欢的人身自由，于欢为制止不法侵害而捅刺的四人，均是不法侵害人。
5.从防卫结果看，明显超过必要限度，造成重大损害。不能明显超过必要限度造成重大损害，这是正当防卫的适度性条件，也是区分防卫适当与防卫过当的重要标准。衡量必要限度时必须结合不法侵害的行为性质、行为强度和可能造成的危害后果等进行综合考量，既不能简单以结果论，也不能一出现死伤结果就认定是防卫过当。本案中，于欢的行为具有防卫的性质，采取的反制行为明显超出必要限度且造成了伤亡后果，应当认定为防卫过当。首先，于欢不具备特殊防卫的前提条件。刑法第20条第3款规定的特殊防卫，其适用前提是防卫人针对严重危及人身安全的暴力犯罪的加害人而实施防卫行为。本案中，虽然于欢母子的人身自由权遭受限制乃至剥夺、人格尊严权遭受言行侮辱侵犯、身体健康权遭受轻微暴力侵犯，但直至民警出警后均未遭遇任何针对生命权严重不法侵害，因而不具有实施特殊防卫的前提。其所采取的防卫行为是否正当，不得适用特殊防卫阻却刑事责任的法定评判标准。其次，本案属于违法逼债激发的防卫案件。本案中，杜志浩等人的目的就是把钱要回，手段相对克制，没有暴力殴打于欢母子的意思和行为；讨债一方（李忠）对杜志浩脱裤暴露下体的行为给予了制止；当于欢捅刺杜志浩、程学贺后，严建军、郭彦刚、么传行等人围站在于欢身边，也没有明显的暴力攻击。最后，防卫行为与不法侵害相比明显不相适应。本案中，于欢为了制止不法侵害，摆脱困境，使用致命性工具刺向加害人，造成一死、二重伤、一轻伤的后果，其行为结果明显属于“重大损害”。从不法侵害行为看，虽然加害人人数众多但未使用工具，未进行严重暴力攻击，于欢身上伤情甚至未达到轻微伤程度；从防卫紧迫性看，出警民警已到场，虽然离开接待室，但仍在源大公司院内寻找报警人、了解情况，从接待室可以清晰看到门前警车及警灯闪烁；从防卫行为保护的法益与造成结果体现的法益衡量看，要保护的是人身自由和人格尊严，造成结果体现的法益是生命健康，两者相比不相适应。从防卫行为使用的工具、致伤部位、捅刺强度及后果综合衡量看，于欢使用的是长26厘米的单刃刀，致伤部位为杜志浩身体的要害部位（肝脏），捅刺强度深达15厘米，造成1死2重伤1轻伤的严重后果，其防卫行为“明显超过必要限度”。 
近日，于欢案二审宣布宣判，山东省高级人民法院认定于欢属防卫过当，构成故意伤害罪，判处有期徒刑5年。

1. Introduction
In this recitation we will learn how to employ the newsvendor problem to solve a scheduling problem. More specifically, we want to find the optimal number of nurses to be scheduled to work on the quarantine & isolation team at Cornell Health to handle positive COVID tests.
Whenever a student tests positive, this team of nurses calls that student to check on their symptoms and to move them to a room at a hotel. The nursing team also calls students identified as close contacts of the positive student, and who may have already been infected, to move them into quarantine at a hotel. This is done to protect against the possibility that others would be infected by coming into contact with someone who is infectious. In many cases this is straightforward but it sometimes requires a substantial amount of work: the person being contacted might have a medical situation that requires extra attention (either from COVID or another condition); might not answer their phone (requiring repeated calls); might be belligerent or misrepresent the truth (requiring a longer conversation and sometimes the use of additional resources); or might simply have a great number of questions.
This is a newsvendor problem because demand is stochastic and there is a cost both for scheduling too few nurses, and for scheduling too many. If we schedule too many nurses, we create a burden for them and risk burning out the nursing team. If we schedule too few, we can ask unscheduled nurses to come on and work at the last minute, but this forces them to drop their personal life and start working (creating a burden and risking burnout). Also, scheduling too few nurses creates a risk that we might not be able to contact and move to quarantine / isolation all of the students who are potentially infectious, risking the creation of new COVID cases.
We focus on the arrival period for the Spring 2021 semester. During this period, students arriving from elsewhere were testing positive due to infections acquired at home. The rapid increase in arriving students and the changing nature of nationwide and international prevalence relative to the Fall 2020 semester made it difficult to plan based on past experience. This made mathematical modeling extremely useful. Indeed, code close to this recitation was actually used to make staffing decisions at Cornell Health over this period.

During the early 20th century, science fiction introduced the world to the concept of AI robots. By the 1950s, young British polymath Alan Turing explored the mathematical possibility of AI and how to build and test intelligent machines. However, computers needed fundamental changes and hefty expenses deterred investment in AI research.

The proof of concept for AI was initialized through Allen Newell, Cliff Shaw, and Herbert Simon’s Logic Theorist program in 1956, which catalyzed 20 years of AI research. AI flourished from 1957 to 1974, but several obstacles emerged mainly due to lack of computational power. In the 1980s, AI was reignited by new techniques such as deep learning and expert systems. Although ambitious goals of the Japanese government’s Fifth Generation Computer Project were not met, AI thrived during the 1990s and 2000s, achieving milestones like IBM’s Deep Blue defeating chess champion Gary Kasparov in 1997.

Improvements in computer storage enabled AI to make significant progress, and AI is now widely applied in fields such as technology, banking, marketing, and entertainment. The future of AI could involve AI language and driverless cars, while general intelligence and sentient robots remain long-term goals. As AI continues to advance, discussions about machine policy and ethics will be crucial.

That project really got him into image processing, and Rahul went on to work on more projects and internships in the field. At the time, he was using tools like MATLAB and, eventually, Python became a popular alternative.

Chapter 1: A New Beginning

The sun was setting in the Alola region, painting vivid colors across the sky as the warm ocean breeze rustled through the palm trees. In a small, cozy home nestled on the coast, Iolana stared excitedly at the calendar pinned to the wall of her colorful bedroom. It was hard to believe that tomorrow, she would embark on her adventure as a Pokémon trainer.

“I can’t believe it’s finally happening!” she exclaimed to no one in particular, her long grey hair bouncing as she jumped up and down with excitement. As she spun around, her bright cyan eyes caught a glimpse of the framed photograph over her desk.

The photograph was of Iolana’s adoptive parents, a young couple who had found her wandering alone in Unova almost a decade earlier. They had been on their honeymoon at the time, and had no idea of the secrets hidden within the little girl they had brought home. With no one else to care for her, they had taken her in and raised her as their own. Iolana had never known her past or the atrocities that she had unwittingly committed. She had grown up with no knowledge of her connection to the mysterious “Project Infinity” and the powerful Pokémon who had played a role in her creation.

As the darkness of night fell over the beautiful island, Iolana’s adoptive father knocked gently on her bedroom door. “Iolana, are you ready for dinner?” he asked, peeking in to see her sitting on the floor, surrounded by books and magazines about Pokémon.

“Just a minute, dad,” she replied, quickly organizing the scattered papers and books as she got to her feet. She joined her parents at the dinner table, her thoughts still filled with excitement and anticipation for the journey that lay ahead of her.

Throughout dinner, Iolana’s parents listened with pride as she detailed her hopes and dreams for her future as a Pokémon trainer. They were happy to see her so passionate about the adventure that lay ahead, but deep down, they knew that she would inevitably one day learn the truth about herself, the Unova government, and the dark history that had brought her to Alola.

After dinner, Iolana’s father handed her a neatly wrapped gift. “We wanted to give you something special before you head out on your big adventure,” he said, smiling warmly. Iolana excitedly tore the wrapping paper away, revealing a brand-new Pokédex and a letter of recommendation from the local Professor. Her eyes filled with tears of joy as she hugged her parents tightly. “Thank you both so much! This means the world to me.”

As the night wore on, Iolana laid in bed, unable to fall asleep, her mind racing with thoughts of her journey ahead. She couldn’t have been more thrilled to go and capture her own Pokémon, train them, and become the best Pokémon trainer she could be.

But far away from the peaceful island, hidden in the depths of the Unova government’s secrets archives, lay the knowledge of a catastrophic project and the quiet stirrings of a forgotten ambition. An ancient power long dormant within Iolana, waiting for the right moment to once again awake, even as the unsuspecting girl lay dreaming of her future.

The next day marked the beginning of Iolana’s greatest adventure, one filled with friendship, trials, and self-discovery. But it would also be an adventure that would lead her into a dangerous world of secrets and lies, where the power of an ancient Pokémon would threaten to change everything she thought she knew about herself and the world around her.


Chapter 2: A New Companion

The morning sun shone brightly over Alola as Iolana set out on her new adventure. Excited to meet her first partner Pokémon, she hurried to the laboratory of the local Pokémon Professor.

As Iolana arrived at the lab, she found her best friend, Kai, already waiting there for her, his face beaming with enthusiasm. They had grown up together, and despite Kai’s troubled past, he had remained a cheerful, upbeat boy with an unwavering love for Pokémon.

“Hey, Iolana!” Kai called, waving energetically. “I got here early so we could choose our starter Pokémon together! I’ve been dreaming about this moment for as long as I can remember.”

Smiling, Iolana replied, “Me too, Kai! Let’s go meet our new partners!”

The two friends entered the laboratory, eager to begin their adventure together. Inside, they were greeted by the Pokémon Professor. “Ah, you must be Iolana and Kai!” the Professor said warmly. “I’m Professor Koa, and I’ve been studying Pokémon here in Alola for many years. You must be excited to begin your journey. Now, let’s see which Pokémon you’ll be starting with.”

As Iolana and Kai examined the available Pokémon, they were instantly drawn to two very different creatures: Cyndaquil, a small fire-type Pokémon, and Oshawott, an adorable water-type. They knew they had made their choices as soon as their eyes met their future partners.

Excitedly, they accepted their new Pokémon partners from Professor Koa, who then handed them each a Pokédex. “This will help you keep track of the Pokémon you encounter and learn important information about them. Good luck on your journey, and remember to always treat your Pokémon with love and respect.”

With their new partners – Oshawott for Kai, and Cyndaquil for Iolana – and their trusty Pokédexes in hand, the two friends began their journey together in high spirits.

Despite the challenges brought on by Kai’s family situation, he had held onto his boundless enthusiasm and passion for Pokémon. As Iolana and Kai embarked on their adventure, they knew they could count on each other to make this a journey they would never forget.

However, as the two trainers set out to explore the world and grow stronger with their Pokémon, the shadows of Iolana’s past and Kai’s difficult family life continued to loom over their friendship. The trials that awaited them would challenge their bond and force them to confront the dark secrets buried deep within their own lives.

But for now, Iolana and Kai had each other, and their loyal Pokémon companions would accompany them every step of the way. Together, they would face whatever challenges awaited them with courage and an unbreakable bond of friendship.


Chapter 3: The First Battle

The sun blazed over the beautiful island of Alola as Iolana and Kai began their journey as Pokémon trainers. With their new partners by their side, they agreed that the best way to start their adventure was to test themselves in a friendly Pokémon battle. The excitement in the air was palpable as they found a suitable spot on the soft sands of a nearby beach.

“Alright, Iolana,” said Kai, his eyes filled with determination as he looked at his best friend. “Let’s promise that no matter what happens, we’ll help each other to grow stronger and become great Pokémon trainers.”

Iolana smiled, her eyes shimmering with confidence. “Absolutely, Kai! Let’s do this!”

With a nod of agreement, they took their positions on the sandy battleground. “Go, Cyndaquil!” Iolana shouted, releasing her new partner Pokémon from its Pokéball.

In response, Kai threw his Pokéball into the air, calling out his beloved Oshawott. “Alright, Oshawott, it’s showtime!”

As their Pokémon faced each other, Iolana and Kai couldn’t help but feel a sense of pride in their new partnership. “Cyndaquil, use Tackle!” Iolana commanded, watching as her Pokémon charged toward its opponent.

Kai quickly countered, “Oshawott, dodge and use Water Gun!” Oshawott swiftly sidestepped Cyndaquil’s attack and unleashed a powerful stream of water from its mouth, hitting the fire-type Pokémon with surprising accuracy.

The battle continued, with both trainers giving their all as they directed their Pokémon’s movements. With each attack, dodge, and block, Iolana and Kai learned more about their partners’ strengths, weaknesses, and potential.

After a heated exchange of moves, the young trainers eventually called a truce, realizing that their Pokémon were exhausted from their first battle. However, both Iolana and Kai had grown stronger through the experience, and they were more determined than ever to help each other grow.

As they tended to their weary Pokémon, Iolana looked at Kai and said, “We promised that we’d help each other become great trainers, and I know we’ll keep that promise. I’m so glad I have you beside me on this journey.”

Kai smiled back at her. “I feel the same way, Iolana. With you and our Pokémon, I know we’ll face any challenge that comes our way.”

As the friends continued their adventure, they were reminded of their unwavering commitment to each other and their Pokémon. Together, they would face countless battles, difficult trials, and the shadows of their own pasts.

But through it all, Iolana and Kai knew that no matter what obstacles they encountered, they could count on their friendship, and the love and support of their Pokémon, to see them through.


Chapter 4: A Bittersweet Moment

As Iolana and Kai ventured deeper into the Alolan landscape, they found themselves wandering through lush forests and exploring winding trails, all while strengthening their bond with each other and their Pokémon.

During a break in their journey, the two friends found themselves sitting down below the shade of a tall, leafy tree. They began a heartfelt conversation about their dreams, their pasts, and how much their friendship meant to each of them.

“I can’t imagine doing this without you, Iolana,” Kai confessed as they watched their Pokémon play nearby. “There have been so many times in my life where I felt completely alone, but having you by my side has made all the difference.”

Iolana’s heart ached for her friend, knowing all too well the pain of losing loved ones and the turmoil it had caused in his life. “We’ve always been there for each other, Kai. You’re like family to me, and I know that we can face anything together.”

The friends shared a quiet, comforting moment as they basked in the warmth of their bond. However, their peace was suddenly interrupted by the sound of Kai’s PokéGear ringing. He glanced at the screen and hesitated before answering the call.

“Hey, Dad,” Kai greeted tentatively, trying his best to sound cheerful. He listened intently for a moment before his face fell, disappointment creeping into his voice. “Oh, you can’t come home today? I understand… No, it’s alright. Don’t worry about me. I’ll see you soon.”

As he hung up the call, Kai bit his lip, struggling to contain his disappointment. He had been looking forward to showing his father his new partner, Oshawott, and sharing his excitement about his new journey as a Pokémon trainer.

Iolana looked at Kai with concern, sensing that their conversation had not gone well. “Is everything okay, Kai?” she asked gently.

With a shaky breath, Kai forced a smile onto his face, trying to mask his hurt. “Yeah, it’s fine. I was just hoping that my dad could see Oshawott, but he’s really busy with work at the Aether Foundation. It’s not a big deal.”

Iolana squeezed her friend’s hand reassuringly. “I’m sure your dad is proud of you, Kai. He just has a lot on his plate right now. And don’t forget, you’ve got me right here to share all of your journey’s best moments with.”

Kai’s eyes shimmered with gratitude as he genuinely smiled back at Iolana. “You’re right, Iolana. Thanks for always being there for me.”

As their journey continued, Iolana and Kai would find that their greatest strength lay not only in their Pokémon partners but also in the depth of their friendship and their unwavering support for one another. Amid the challenges of battles, the shadows of their pasts, and the unexpected trials that awaited them, they knew they could face anything, as long as they did it together.


Chapter 5: A Hidden Connection

Meanwhile, in the bustling city of Castelia, in the Unova region, a talented young journalist named Aelfrun fought to make a name for herself among the competitive throngs of the metropolis. With her keen intuition and ambition, Aelfrun had managed to carve out a fulfilling life for herself, albeit one weighed down by tragic memories and a haunting past.

Aelfrun’s parents had been brilliant scientists, working on something they referred to as “Project Infinity.” But beneath the mysterious name, they had never revealed the true nature of their work to their young daughter. When she was a baby, Aelfrun had suffered from a life-threatening heart condition, Tetralogy of Fallot. Desperate to save their daughter’s life, her parents had infused her with the very same experimental serum they had been studying—cells from the legendary Pokémon, Xerneas.

This dangerous procedure had left Aelfrun with not only a healthy heart but also the latent power of the Mythical Pokémon. What her parents never expected, however, was the unanticipated consequences of their experiment: Project Infinity had sown seeds of great tragedy in the lives of everyone it touched.

As a child, Aelfrun had dreamed of becoming a Pokémon Master. At the age of twelve, she had even managed to collect all of the elusive badges from Pokémon gyms throughout the Unova region. But when Aelfrun returned home from her journey, she was greeted with heartbreak. Her parents had perished in a devastating accident at the Research and Incubation Facility 06—the very same incident that had brought chaos to Iolana’s life.

The loss of her parents shattered Aelfrun’s dreams of becoming a Pokémon Master, and she had eventually turned her focus to journalism in an attempt to heal her grief-stricken heart. Yet, unknowingly, her life remained entangled with the remnants of Project Infinity and the fate of Iolana.

As Aelfrun continued her work, she began to uncover hints of secrets hidden within the Unovan government. Something drew her to investigate the matter further, a deep-rooted intuition that these secrets would lead her to the truth about the tragic fate of her parents and herself. Little did Aelfrun know that her investigation would connect her to Iolana’s past and uncover the buried connection between them—their shared legacy as the unwitting children of Project Infinity.

Through their shared journey towards self-discovery, Iolana, Kai, and Aelfrun would soon find themselves entwined in a powerful tale of destiny, friendship, and the ultimate courage required to embrace their true potential.

As they delved deeper into the mysteries of life, choices would have to be made, sacrifices faced, and only the most indomitable bond of friendship would see them through the challenges that lay ahead.


Chapter 6: Encountering the Unknown

The hustle and bustle of Castelia city never seemed to slow down, even as the evening sun dipped below the towering skyscrapers. Aelfrun’s life as a newspaper reporter in Unova’s largest metropolis left her little time to ponder the mysteries of her past or the suspicions lingering in her mind about Project Infinity.

One autumn evening, Aelfrun found herself sitting at her desk in the cramped office she shared with other ambitious reporters, sifting through a stack of stories and leads in search of something compelling to investigate. As she combed through the articles, one particular headline caught her eye: “UFO Sighting Reported in District 54—A Mysterious Connection?”

Intrigued, Aelfrun began to read the eyewitness accounts of strange lights seen streaking across the sky in the remote outskirts of Castelia. Those who had witnessed the phenomenon whispered rumors of government cover-ups, secret experiments, and “visitors” from other worlds. This was a far cry from Aelfrun’s usual investigative subjects, but something about the story spoke to her, resonating with a buried instinct she couldn’t quite grasp.

Following her intuition, she decided to investigate the UFO sightings and the urban legend surrounding District 54. Perhaps it would lead her to some answers about the elusive Project Infinity and the mysterious events that had upturned so many lives.

Over the following weeks, Aelfrun delved deeper into the tale of District 54, tirelessly scanning old newspapers, scouring local archives, and conducting interviews with those who claimed to have seen the mysterious lights. Slowly, she began to piece together a fragmented puzzle that hinted at hidden truths and long-forgotten connections.

As she delved further, she discovered that District 54 had once been home to a government research facility, which had been destroyed in a catastrophe—an event that had marked the end of Project Infinity. Aelfrun couldn’t help but feel a prickle run down her spine at the realization that this shared past with Iolana was no coincidence, and that their fates were somehow inextricably linked.

Unbeknownst to her, the threads of Aelfrun’s investigation were reaching across the seas to the Alolan region, weaving an intricate web that would soon entangle the lives of Iolana, Kai, and herself in a tumultuous tapestry of secrets, friendship, and an epic exploration of life’s true essence.

As Aelfrun continued to unravel the mysteries of District 54, she found herself drawn closer and closer to the truth about her own origins, the powerful bond that connected her with Iolana, and the destiny that awaited them both. Yet, as Aelfrun would soon learn, the path to clarity is seldom a straight one, and only the most steadfast hearts can navigate the treacherous turns that lay ahead.


Chapter 7: The Heart of Darkness

Aelfrun’s search for the hidden truths of District 54 brought her to the remains of Research and Incubation Facility 06. The abandoned structure loomed ominously, a haunting reminder of the tragedy and devastation that had occurred within its walls. Undeterred, Aelfrun ventured through the crumbling corridors, her heart pounding with each step further into the darkness.

The air was thick with an eerie silence, pierced only by Aelfrun’s ragged breath and anxious footfalls. As she delved deeper into the heart of the facility, Aelfrun stumbled upon a hidden chamber, its entrance concealed by the ruins and debris. Overcoming her overwhelming anxiety, Aelfrun approached the chamber cautiously.

Inside, she found it—the blood-red glowing feather, a symbol of power, despair, and a long-buried secret. As she reached out to touch it, an immense energy surged into her being, consuming her vision with searing images of the tragic day when both Project Infinity and her parents had met their tragic end.

In that moment of chaos and pain, Aelfrun’s connection to Iolana and the experiment that had bound them since childhood grew ever stronger. The intensity of the energy seared her skin, and she instinctively dropped the feather, gasping for breath amid the swirling shadows of her memories. Every fiber of her being ached to take the feather from this forsaken place and bring it to Iolana, but fate had other plans.

As Aelfrun turned her back on the dark chamber and the secrets that still lingered within, she was met with a sudden and violent force. The shock of the taser stunned her, and she crumpled to the ground, the pain washing over her like a crashing wave.

As the agents of the Ministry of Defence descended upon her, Aelfrun’s heart raced—filled with dread and determination. Her mind swam with questions and the enormity of what she had just discovered, but one thing was clear: she would not allow the feather or the secrets hidden within District 54 to remain hidden.

As the black hood descended over Aelfrun’s face, her fate intertwined ever more closely with that of Iolana and Kai. The trials to come would test their strength, courage, and their unshakeable bond, but together they would uncover the ultimate truth behind the mysteries that had shaped their lives—and perhaps, through their shared destiny, break the curse that had haunted them since the day their hearts had first begun to beat.


Chapter 8: Interrogation and Revelation

Aelfrun’s body ached with the intensity of the shock as she was hauled to her feet by her captors. She could feel the beads of sweat forming on her brow, her breathing shallow and labored under the black hood covering her face. Her mind raced with questions and fear but, beneath it all, a spark of indignant anger stoked a fire within her heart.

The agents of the Ministry of Defence guided Aelfrun through the winding facility, dragging her into a sterile, windowless room. As they forcibly seated her in a cold metal chair, the cacophony of their footsteps reverberated on the solid concrete floor. The slap of a floodlight snapping on pierced the darkness, even as her eyes remained shrouded under the hood.

A booming voice echoed throughout the room, “Miss Aelfrun Wyndsor, do you know where you are?” The smooth, abrasive tone put her on edge instantly.

For a brief moment, Aelfrun hesitated and considered remaining silent. But ultimately, she knew that to understand the depths of the mysteries that had plagued her entire life, she needed to respond. Her voice, defiant but trembling ever so slightly, responded, “Yes.”

A cruel laugh reverberated throughout the room. “You’ve come a long way in search of secrets, Miss Wyndsor,” the voice taunted. “But you’ve ventured too far and now find yourself imprisoned by those you sought to expose.”

The agents roughly lifted the hood from Aelfrun’s face. As her eyes struggled to adjust to the sudden influx of light, she squinted at the man before her—a tall man with balding gray hair, wearing a crisp military uniform. His eyes held an unsettling mixture of amusement and malice.

“You must know that digging into the Ministry’s buried secrets will only lead to pain and suffering,” he continued calmly. “So, tell me, Miss Wyndsor, what would you hope to gain by delving into the past?”

Aelfrun’s eyes flashed with determination, her voice unwavering as she responded, “Answers. I deserve to know the truth about Project Infinity, the research my parents were involved in, and how it has shaped my own existence. This tragedy has haunted me for years, and I am tired of living in the shadow of unanswered questions.”

The man smiled menacingly, leaning in closer to Aelfrun’s face. “Perhaps,” he said, his voice dripping with malice, “your questions are better left unanswered, Miss Wyndsor. The truth can sometimes be more devastating than the lies we cling to.”

For a moment, Aelfrun considered the possibility that he could be right, that the truth might be a burden she could not bear. But as she thought of Iolana and the journey that had led her to this moment, Aelfrun knew that she couldn’t allow the past to remain hidden any longer.

“I will face the truth, whatever it may be,” Aelfrun declared, her resolve unwavering. “I owe it to myself and everyone affected by Project Infinity to uncover the reality of what transpired and the secrets it still holds.”

The agent’s smile widened, a sinister gleam in his eyes. “Very well,” he conceded. “Let us see if you are truly prepared to face the darkness that has shaped your life.”

In the hours that followed, Aelfrun would face her deepest fears and most haunting memories, even as Kai and Iolana journeyed ever closer to the destiny that awaited them. The time for revelations had come, and the intertwining destinies of these three friends would finally collide in an epic struggle for truth, understanding, and redemption.


Chapter 9: The Chosen One

The cold steel of the metal chair chilled Aelfrun to the bone as they continued on with the interrogation. As the questions grew harsher, the chilling nature of the secret files laid bare before her, she began to realize she had stumbled upon far more than she bargained for. The Ministry of Defence’s involvement with Project Infinity went beyond the realm of anything she could have imagined.

The agent, leaning back against the wall, scrutinized Aelfrun with a penetrating gaze. “Do you want to know why you’re still alive, Miss Wyndsor?” he asked, his voice menacingly calm. “We’ve been monitoring your progress for a while now. Ever since you first touched the feather, we knew you were different. Others who have come into contact with it have either died from the energy radiation or have descended into madness from the hallucinations.”

Aelfrun shuddered at the words as a cold sense of dread crept over her. She had survived where others had perished, yet now found herself ensnared in the most dangerous web she could have imagined.

The agent continued, the smile never leaving his lips. “You see, Miss Wyndsor, you are the only one chosen by the feather. Your unique connection to it has protected you, and this makes you… valuable.”

He paused, allowing the weight of his revelation to sink in, and then added, “Our director would like to extend to you an offer—an opportunity to join our ranks and become an agent of the DHS Special Office of Project Infinity.”

Aelfrun’s heart pounded as she considered the appalling proposition. Was it possible she was meant to play a role in the very project that had shaped her life and haunted her existence? Could she face the darkness that it represented?

Yet, she knew that she needed to understand more about what had happened to her, and perhaps by joining the DHS, she would be able to gather their hidden knowledge and learn the truth she had been so desperately seeking.

With a defiant glint in her eyes, she nodded in agreement. “I will join you. I need to know the truth about my past and the real reason behind Project Infinity, no matter the cost.”

The agent’s smirk widened, and he extended a hand to help her up, his grip icy and unwavering. As Aelfrun rose to her feet, she knew that the path she had chosen would be fraught with danger and deception, and that her journey had only just begun.

Chapter 10: Dark Alliances

It was not long before Aelfrun found herself thrust into the secretive world of the DHS, working alongside the very people responsible for the tragedy that had befallen her family. As she delved deeper into the world of Project Infinity, she began to uncover secrets, lies, and the depths of the corruption within the Ministry of Defence.

Meanwhile, with each passing day, the thread connecting Aelfrun to Iolana and Kia grew stronger, causing a sense of foreboding to consume her. It was as if their fates were tied together, anchored by the very evil she was working against, and ultimately destined to clash in a battle for the truth.

Through her newfound position within the DHS, Aelfrun used the knowledge and resources at her disposal to continue her search for answers, and with each piece of the puzzle that fell into place, she felt a glimmer of hope for herself and her friends.

As the days turned to weeks, and the weeks to months, Aelfrun’s skills as an agent grew more refined, her determination unwavering as she pursued her ultimate goal. Despite the darkness that surrounded her, she clung desperately to the hope that one day, she would uncover the truth they had fought so hard to hide, and finally bring light to the lives of those who had suffered for so long.

But with each step closer to the truth, Aelfrun began to realize that the intertwined destinies of herself, Iolana, and Kai could no longer be ignored. Soon, they would face the depths of their own pasts and confront the secrets that had shaped their lives, and only the devotion of their friendship and the strength of their will could guide them through the darkness that awaited them in the heart of the Ministry’s web.


Chapter 11: A Journey’s Beginning

While Aelfrun was immersed in her dangerous pursuit for the truth, Iolana and Kai couldn’t be more excited to begin their first day as Pokémon trainers. The sun shone brightly upon the Alolan islands, the scent of tropical flowers filled the air, and new experiences awaited the friends and their cherished Pokémon partners.

The two set off from Hau’oli City, eager to explore the Alola region and the wonders it held. Oshawott and Cyndaquil, Kai and Iolana’s trusted partners, chirped and chattered with excitement as they journeyed alongside their trainers, sensing the beginning of a grand adventure.

However, the carefree first day of their journey was not without its challenges. As Iolana and Kai traveled along the Alola Route 1, they encountered their first wild Pokémon—an energetic Alolan Rattata. Eager to test their newfound skills, the friends decided to face the Rattata in a friendly battle.

“Okay, Oshawott! Use Water Gun!” Kai commanded enthusiastically, watching as the water-type Pokémon launched a powerful jet of water toward the ferocious Rattata.

“Counter with Ember, Cyndaquil!” Iolana called out, her eyes shining with determination. Bright red flames erupted from the small creature, colliding with Oshawott’s Water Gun attack.

The Rattata, having evaded the clash of the moves, counterattacked with incredible speed, landing a Quick Attack on the momentarily distracted Oshawott. However, Iolana’s Cyndaquil was not to be outdone, delivering a swift Tackle attack that sent the Rattata reeling.

Despite the relentless back-and-forth of the battle, both Kai and Iolana couldn’t help but feel a sense of camaraderie and enjoyment in facing a new and challenging opponent. The wild Rattata, exhausted but impressed by the duo’s teamwork, decided to join Iolana and Kai on their journey, becoming the first of many new friends and Pokémon partners.

As the sun began to set on their first day, Iolana and Kai knew one thing was certain: their journey together had only just begun. With their loyal Pokémon by their side, they would forge new friendships, overcome difficult obstacles, and unravel the threads that bound them to their mysterious pasts.

In the end, only time would tell how the intertwining fates of Iolana, Kai, and Aelfrun would collide, revealing the ultimate truth hidden deep within the heart of the Alolan region and the unnerving depths of Project Infinity. And only the strength of their unwavering bond could guide them through the darkness and toward a brighter future for themselves and their beloved Pokémon.


Chapter 12: The Alolan Trials

With their journey underway and spirits high, Iolana and Kai ventured deeper into the Alola region. Their next goal was to partake in the Island Trials of Alola, a test of skill and strength designed for trainers to demonstrate their abilities and create a deeper bond between themselves and their Pokémon.

The first trial they faced was located on Melemele Island, led by the stern yet wise Captain Ilima. In order to pass the trial, Iolana and Kai had to demonstrate their strategic thinking and teamwork by navigating a complex maze within Verdant Cavern, finding three hidden Alolan Rattata, and challenging the powerful Totem Gumshoos that ruled over the cave.

As the friends delved deeper into the cavern, their newfound skills were put to the test as they faced wild Pokémon and worked together to reach the Totem Gumshoos. Oshawott and Cyndaquil also displayed impressive growth and teamwork, excited to face the challenges presented by the trials.

Finally, with the help of their loyal Pokémon partners and their unwavering determination, Iolana and Kai managed to reach the Totem Gumshoos. In a heated battle, they managed to overcome the formidable opponent, earning themselves the approval of Captain Ilima and the privilege to advance further in the Island Trials.

As they exited the trial grounds, Captain Ilima approached the friends with a solemn expression. He shared a story of an ancient legend, one which spoke of three individuals whose destinies were intertwined, bound by a mysterious force. The legend foretold that these three would ultimately unite to face an unimaginable darkness and change the course of history.

Chills ran down the spines of Iolana and Kai as they listened to the legend. Their minds turned instinctively to their hidden pasts and the ongoing struggles of their far-off friend, Aelfrun, who was delving deeper into the darkness of the enigmatic Project Infinity.

Though they understood little about their connection to the age-old legend, Iolana and Kai knew one thing for certain: whatever challenges lay ahead, their bond of friendship and their love for their Pokémon partners would be the guiding light that would see them through the trials and tribulations to come.

As they continued to traverse the Alola region, facing the tests of the Island Trials and uncovering the secrets hidden within the world around them, the threads binding their destinies grew ever stronger. In the end, it would take the combined strength of Iolana, Kai, and Aelfrun to face the heart of darkness and unravel the truth of their intertwined fates.

Analyze the following:

King's Quest

A long, long time ago, when unicorns still roamed the forests and the merfolk still dwelt in the shallow waters frequented by men, there ruled in the kingdom of Daventry King Edward and his lovely Queen. The people of Daventry were prosperous and happy, and everywhere peace reigned. But the King and Queen were sad because they were childless. They had no son to inherit the throne, nor a daughter to gladden their hearts.

One bright, sunny day King Edward the Benevolent (for so he was called) and his Queen were walking in the castle garden when suddenly before them appeared a powerful sorcerer. "I know your problem and I can cast a spell that will bring you a child," he said.

"Oh, great sorcerer, if you can help us, we will be everlastingly grateful," said the Queen.

"We will bestow upon you many honors, and great riches," said the King.

"I have no use for honors or riches. My payment will not be so great. All I ask in return is the mahogany-framed Mirror that hangs in your private chamber."

The sorcerer's words gave them pause, for that Mirror was priceless. It had the power to read the future, and helped to keep Daventry prosperous. The royal couple used it to foretell the weather for planting and harvest, as had the kings and queens before them. It had been hundreds of years since a crop had been planted before the last frost, or had been ruined by autumn rain. What the sorcerer desired was indeed valuable. The King and Queen retired to their chamber to consult the magic Mirror.

King Edward and his wife gazed into the Mirror's depths and saw a young princely figure with a gold crown upon his head. Imagining the youth to be the son they yearned for, the royal couple gladly bestowed the Mirror upon the sorcerer. He took it to his dwelling, where he set one of his beasts to guard over it.

The months passed and the Queen did not conceive a child. For the first time in four hundred years, Daventry lost the harvest to an early autumn rainstorm. The King and Queen wept, and everyone tightened their belts. Instead of having excess produce to sell to neighboring kingdoms, the people of Daventry had to supplement their stores with food bought elsewhere.

With famine came the dreaded Plague, and the Queen was stricken. For three days she lay in the grip of a great fever, with Edward maintaining a constant vigil by her side.

On the fourth day of the Queen's illness, a diminutive figure pushed his way between the legs of the castle guards. "I have a cure for the Queen," he claimed. Quickly the courtiers ushered him into the Queen's chamber, where the King despaired.

"I have traveled a great distance to bring relief to your dear wife. This powerful root known only to dwarves will cure any plague."

The dwarf leaned over the Queen and touched the root to her lips. Her eyes fluttered open and she smiled at Edward.

The Queen's attendants looked at each other in wonderment. "Only a touch revived her," they whispered. "Imagine how fast she will recover when given the whole root!"

"Ask any reward for this miraculous gift, oh small one," exclaimed King Edward.

"I ask in repayment the Shield left you by your father when he died," said the dwarf softly.

The King paled at the thought. The Shield, made of titanium and set with emeralds, was traditionally carried in battle by the ruler of Daventry. Legend held that he who bore the Shield was invincible, and his army always victorious. Thus there had been no successful attack on the kingdom of Daventry for over five hundred years.

"Ask again little man. I will give you your weight in gold, but please do not ask for the Shield," said the King.

"You do not appear to value your wife's life, your highness," said the dwarf. "I will take no other reward than that which I have requested." Haughtily he turned to go.

"Come back," Edward called. "I'll give you the Shield." The Dwarf took the Shield, and secreted it away in a hole in the ground, in the way of Dwarves.

The Queen partook of the root, but to no avail. She worsened and died. Daventry's church bells tolled in mourning, and the King vowed vengeance against the false dwarf. Years passed, and the news of the loss of the Shield spread. Armies attacked the weakened Daventry, and the King went out to lead his armies without the Shield. Never before did they have need of the Mirror to foretell enemy moves. Now, that too was gone.

Many years passed, and the King was very lonely. One day, while out riding with his courtiers, Edward came upon a pack of wolves tearing at the lower limbs of a big tree. When the group approached, the wolves scattered to reveal a beautiful young woman perched in the tree.

She descended regally. "I thank you for the rescue, kind sirs. I am the Princess Dahlia, of Cumberland. I was traveling through this land when that pack of wolves fell upon my group. My bodyguard fled in terror from their fangs, leaving me quite alone. I owe you my life, and my heartfelt gratitude."

The King was charmed with the Princess Dahlia, and brought her back to his castle to visit. He felt new life coursing through his veins, and knew it was because he had met someone who might fill the loneliness left by his late Queen.

In due time Edward asked Dahlia to marry him, and she accepted. The people of Daventry were wildly excited at the prospect of a new Queen (and hopeful again of an heir), and made preparations for a glorious wedding celebration.

On the night before the wedding, when the air was thick with toasts and merriment, Princess Dahlia bid Edward good night. He never noticed her hand stealing up to his belt and extracting the ring of keys hanging there. Much later, the Royal treasurer approached the King with alarming news.

He had discovered the treasury door standing open, with the King's own key in the lock. The Princess Dahlia had been inside, holding a small Chest of gold.

The treasurer stood frozen to the spot. The Princess' bright laughter changed to a witch's cackle as her form grew old and withered. She grasped the Chest and mounted her broom to fly out the open window. The treasurer watched in dismay as she swooped up through the clouds and disappeared.

When the King heard the news, he wept in despair. That Chest was magic, and the last great treasure remaining in Daventry. No matter how much was taken from it, the Chest always remained brimful of golden coins. Without the Chest he could buy no more food, pay no more soldiers.

Many more years passed, and Daventry grew poor and weak. King Edward was old and feeble, and saw that his end was near. Fearing that the country would fall into even greater disorder when he died, he sent for his favorite knight, Sir Graham.

"You are the bravest and truest knight in my kingdom, Sir Graham. Long ago I envisioned your form in my magic Mirror, and thought I was seeing my son and heir. The years have proven me at least half wrong. But the prophecy may yet be fulfilled.

"To prove yourself worthy of my crown, I command you to journey out into the world and retrieve the three great treasures taken from Daventry by treachery and stealth. Succeed in this great quest, and you shall become King upon my death. Fail, and our beautiful Daventry will grow ever weaker until it is invaded and conquered by unfriendly nations. This I promise by all that is honorable and right.

"May you return victorious, Sir Graham!"

Become Sir Graham and travel through lands of myth and magic to recover the great treasures. You must retrieve them all, for only the combined magic of the three will restore Daventry to its former glory.

Look to the fables and fairy stories of yore for clues. Leave no stone unturned, no avenue unexplored, and you will triumph in your Quest. Along the way, collect as many treasures as you can. The kingdom of Daventry will need everything you can bring back. And you will profit from the experience.

It may be possible to accomplish each task in more than one way. The more imaginative your solutions, the better fitted you will be to rule Daventry.

MAP YOUR PROGRESS

You will not be able to fulfill the prophecy, Sir Graham, without charting your progress. Create a map showing objects and landmarks you see along the way. You'll want to note dangerous areas, in particular.

Above all, try every direction and map all of the different possibilities. If you overlook an area, you might miss an important clue or necessary tool.

The road you must travel is long and perilous; it is beset by many dangerous beings. You must have the wisdom to know when to stand and fight and when to flee from superior strength. But take heart -- you may receive help in unexpected places.

Go now, Sir Graham. And in the words of King Edward, "May you return victorious!"

# PID controller for position accuracy

class PIDController:

    def __init__(self, kp, ki, kd, limit):
        self.kp = kp
        self.ki = ki
        self.kd = kd
        self.limit = limit
        self.prev_error = 0
        self.integral = 0

    def update(self, error, dt):
        derivative = (error - self.prev_error) / dt
        self.integral += error * dt
        self.integral = max(min(self.integral, self.limit), -self.limit)  # Clamp the integral term
        output = self.kp * error + self.ki * self.integral + self.kd * derivative
        self.prev_error = error
        return output



pid_lat = PIDController(kp, ki, kd, pid_limit)
pid_lon = PIDController(kp, ki, kd, pid_limit)

# master and follower drone connction
