Potential Approaches to Overcome Human Resource Challenges in Green Air
In the context of GA’s current situation, some core HRM concepts and contemporary practices that can be critically assessed include employee motivation theories, stakeholder theory, workforce diversity and inclusion, recruitment and hiring strategies, and employee engagement and well-being.
One aspect of motivation theories that can be applied to GA’s problems is the expectancy theory by Fudge and Schlacter (1999). This theory suggests that employees are driven to act in a certain way based on the expectation that their efforts will lead to a desired outcome, and this outcome will be rewarding. In the case of GA, the company can use the expectancy theory to increase the motivation of employees by clarifying expectations and establishing a strong connection between individual and organizational goals. This approach may help GA to temporarily maintain employee motivation while they work on improving the compensation packages.
Another core HRM concept relevant to GA’s situation is stakeholder theory, which highlights the need for organizations to manage relationships with various stakeholders effectively (Thornock, 2016). In GA’s case, the relationship with trade unions has been challenging due to conflicts over pay and working conditions. These tensions could have been better managed through a more proactive approach to communication, negotiation, and conflict resolution involving all relevant stakeholders.
The concept of diversity and inclusion in the workplace is also critical for GA. Due to the mixed fleet crews, employees with different levels of experience, contracts, and backgrounds are working together, which could lead to conflicts or misunderstandings. It is essential for GA to foster an inclusive work environment that acknowledges and accommodates employees’ differences while promoting a sense of belonging and collaboration.
A relevant contemporary HR practice for GA is developing a strong recruitment and hiring strategy. As GA faces potential workforce shortages, they will need a comprehensive plan to attract and retain qualified employees with skills that match the organization’s needs. This recruitment strategy could include targeted job postings, campus hiring, increased employee referrals, and the use of technology such as social media and online job portals. GA could also consider offering competitive training programs and development opportunities to entice prospective employees.
Finally, employee engagement and well-being should be a top priority for GA’s HR team. The current low morale and dissatisfaction among employees is affecting the quality of service provided to customers, further damaging the company’s public image. GA needs to engage its employees by addressing their concerns, providing support, and creating an environment that promotes a healthy work-life balance. Such efforts can improve employee satisfaction, increase productivity, and thus boost the organization’s overall performance.
In sum, GA’s HR team should critically assess and implement appropriate HRM concepts and contemporary practices to address the critical issues of employee motivation, stakeholder relations, workforce diversity and inclusion, recruitment and hiring, and employee engagement and well-being. By proactively addressing these challenges, GA can create a positive work environment that ultimately leads to improved organizational performance and customer satisfaction.

Recommended Solutions
In light of the aforementioned HR issues faced by GA, several recommended solutions can be proposed to help the airline address its current challenges, improve employee morale, and strengthen its public image.
First and foremost, GA should consider engaging in open and transparent dialogue with its employees and the trade unions representing them. Maintaining strong communication channels, actively listening to employees’ concerns, and being transparent about the organization’s goals and challenges can help build trust and facilitate constructive negotiations (Baraldi and Radaelli, 2020). Collaborative problem-solving efforts with trade unions can lead to mutually beneficial agreements on issues such as pay, working conditions, and employment contracts, ultimately fostering a positive work environment.
Revisiting GA’s compensation packages is another solution that merits consideration. While the current financial situation may prevent immediate changes to employee compensation, GA could develop a comprehensive plan outlining adjustments to be made once the organization’s financial health improves (Purcell and Hutchinson, 2007). This strategy may include offering more competitive salaries or restoring some of the benefits and rewards that employees previously enjoyed. By presenting a well-defined plan for the future, GA can regain its employees’ trust and reinforce commitment to the organization.
Additionally, GA should consider implementing measures that promote employee engagement and overall well-being. This may involve offering greater flexibility with working hours, ensuring that employees have a healthy work-life balance, and providing resources for mental and physical health support (Kahn, 1990). By fostering a supportive work environment, GA can improve employee morale, reduce absenteeism, and enhance productivity.
To address the potential workforce shortage and recruitment challenges, GA needs to develop a strategic approach to recruiting and retaining talent. Armstrong and Taylor (2020) suggest adopting a multifaceted recruitment strategy that incorporates targeted job postings, partnerships with educational institutions, employee referral programs, and the utilization of social media and online platforms. Additionally, offering continuous learning, development, and growth opportunities within the organization can make GA a more attractive employer, ultimately aiding in the recruitment of new employees.
Lastly, GA should strive to improve its public perception by delivering exceptional customer service and addressing customer complaints efficiently. To achieve this goal, the organization must invest in training and development programs aimed at enhancing employees’ customer service skills (Zeithaml et al., 2006). Empowered and motivated employees are more likely to contribute to an improved customer experience which, in turn, can positively impact GA’s public image.
In conclusion, by implementing these recommendations, GA can address its HR challenges, improve employee morale, and enhance its public reputation, paving the way for organizational success and growth in an increasingly competitive market.

Conclusion
In conclusion, the challenges faced by GA highlight the critical role of effective human resource management in maintaining employee satisfaction, fostering a positive work environment, and sustaining a competitive edge. Through open communication and collaboration with trade unions, revisiting compensation plans, promoting employee engagement and well-being, adopting strategic recruitment approaches, and investing in customer service training, GA can address the multifaceted issues that currently affect the organization. By proactively tackling these challenges and prioritizing the well-being and satisfaction of its employees, GA can pave the way for success in an increasingly competitive market while enhancing its public image and solidifying its position as a leading global airline.

English 9
Final Suspense Story

Title

The hotel lobby was empty. Its fireplace roared in the corner, the furniture unmoved and stationery. The rain outside was rhythmic. The quiet was interrupted by a couple bustling through the door. They looked weary from their journey, but the company of each other was clear from the gleaming ring on the wife’s finger. The couple went up to the attendant. 
“Good evening sir, we would like to stay the night,” said the wife.
“Did you have a reservation?” the attendant replied.
“Yes, for room 217 I believe.” 
The attendant eye twitched for just a split second, but quickly nodded and handed the couple their keys. They began heading up the stairs but were taken aback. The lobby was merely a facade for the rest of the hotel. The well-furnished and comfortable entrance hid the falling wallpaper, creaky stairs, and dim lights. Heaving luggage up the stairs didn’t help either.
The couple reached the second floor. The hall was narrow and lined with many old paintings. It was quite dim. The couple opened the door to their room with much anticipation of having a good night’s rest. Their trip had taken them to the French Quarter in Louisiana, where Helen had been born. 
To their surprise, the room was quite neat. The bed sheets were folded well on both beds, a nice lamp was in the middle. The couple had been tired, so they quickly unpacked their luggage and retired to their beds. It was comfortable and warm. It felt safe. They said their goodnights to each other, and fell asleep, putting their long days behind them. 
Helen could not fall asleep, however. Her thoughts of Frank were persistent. She had fallen in love with the love of her life. The two had only met in university, but three short but amazing years later, they had gotten married. Helen looked up to the ceiling, thinking about their wedding and everyone she had met. She thought about what her life would be like from now on. Getting a job, moving into a house, and starting a family.
Knock. Knock. It was 1:00 AM. Who could that be? Helen slowly got out from under her blanket and cautiously walked toward the door to not wake up Frank. As she approached, she had a strange feeling in her stomach. She looked through the peephole and saw…high heels. White heels, polished, and new. However, they had a luminescent glow around them, almost…see through. Helen couldn’t see anyone around and didn’t know who knocked. She wanted to wake Frank, but he was out cold. Helen picked up the courage to open the door. Her hands were trembling to the lock and slowly descended to the knob. Creak. Helen looked left and then right across the hallway, but no one was seen. Relieved, Helen looked down at the heels, but they were gone. 
“Frank!” Helen screamed.
Frank shot out of bed, still groggy, but awake. 
“Frank, we need to leave now.”
“Why?”
“Something is wrong with this hotel. I don’t know what but…we have to go.”
“Alright.”
They hurriedly packed their belongings, quickly throwing their clothes into their luggage. 
	“What did you see?” Frank asked. 
	“I saw…heels,” Helen replied, unsure how Frank would react. “They suddenly disappeared out of sight.”
	Frank didn’t react, but Helen could see the anxiousness in his eyes. Helen was quick to run toward the door and shut off the lights. 
Click. Blood. Cuts. On the door, with the same glow as the heels. The blood was splattered across the door as if someone had…coughed it up, covering the entire door. The scratches ran deep into the wood. They stretched across the corners of the door. Helen’s eyes widened, and a cold sensation traveled across her body. Fear. 
“Helen.”
Helen turned her head. Frank was sprawled on the ground, his face devoid of color. He coughed. A drop. Then two. A stream began coming out of his mouth. 
“Frank!” 
Helen rushed toward her husband’s side. Her passion for Frank was ceaseless. She picked up Frank with all her strength and managed to unlock the door. She heaved Frank, one step at a time through the seemingly endless corridor. Helen was struggling, but she could see a glow on the floor. And then she looked up. 
A translucent figure was dressed in a beautiful wedding gown. Her face was beautiful, her skin delicate and fair. But her dress was ripped, her hair in tatters, and most jarring of all, a small knife in her heart. 
“Helen…”
Helen looked back down at Frank. His head sagged backward, his face completely pale. Helen held back a tear. The ghost drifted beside her, watching Helen tremble. She was going to lose everything, herself, her future, and Frank. The ghost pulled the knife with the same distinct glow out of her heart. Helen closed her eyes.
Shing. 
Helen had expected a sharp pain in her body, but it never came. Instead, when she opened her eyes, the ghost, with its lifeless eye and remorseless stare, dropped the knife in front of Helen. The knife’s glow faded, becoming…real. The ghost’s face slowly morphed into sadness.
“Why did you do this?” Helen asked
The ghost opened her mouth. A raspy voice came out.
“My husband was the love of my life,” she said. “The day after our wedding, I caught him in our room with another woman.”
“I was heartbroken. He rushed towards me and stabbed me. I will never forget the remorseless look he gave.”
The ghost looked down. 
“I despised him. I couldn’t bear the devastation of the loss, so I returned to this hotel and haunted him.”
Helen could remember the slashes on the walls, the blood, and the heels.
“So now you will kill me?” Helen asked, “You’ve taken the love of life.” 
“No, I sense your pain and loss. I will allow you a painless death by your own hands.”
The knife gradually rose up to Helen. She picked it up. It was a one-way ticket to be with Frank again. For a second, she could see her reflection in the blade. Sadness, despair, grief. She wanted to see Frank again.
But she couldn’t. Helen couldn’t give up, she had to live. Helen looked at the end of the corridor, about 50 ft away. She had to run down the stairs and out of the lobby. The only obstacle in the way was the ghost.
Helen ran. She ran past the ghost, managing to catch her by surprise.

OpenAI’s CEO confirms the company isn’t training GPT-5 and ‘won’t for some time’ / CEO Sam Altman quashed rumors that OpenAI is already developing GPT-5. However, the comments likely won’t be of much consolation to those worried about AI safety.
By JAMES VINCENT

Apr 14, 2023, 12:28 PM GMT6 Comments / 6 New
Share this story



If you buy something from a Verge link, Vox Media may earn a commission. See our ethics statement.

An image of OpenAI’s logo, which looks like a stylized and symmetrical braid.
Image: OpenAI
In a discussion about threats posed by AI systems, Sam Altman, OpenAI’s CEO and co-founder, has confirmed that the company is not currently training GPT-5, the presumed successor to its AI language model GPT-4, released this March.

Speaking at an event at MIT, Altman was asked about a recent open letter circulated among the tech world that requested that labs like OpenAI pause development of AI systems “more powerful than GPT-4.” The letter highlighted concerns about the safety of future systems but has been criticized by many in the industry, including a number of signatories. Experts disagree about the nature of the threat posed by AI (is it existential or more mundane?) as well as how the industry might go about “pausing” development in the first place.

At MIT, Altman said the letter was “missing most technical nuance about where we need the pause” and noted that an earlier version claimed that OpenAI is currently training GPT-5. “We are not and won’t for some time,” said Altman. “So in that sense it was sort of silly.”

However, just because OpenAI is not working on GPT-5 doesn’t mean it’s not expanding the capabilities of GPT-4 — or, as Altman was keen to stress, considering the safety implications of such work. “We are doing other things on top of GPT-4 that I think have all sorts of safety issues that are important to address and were totally left out of the letter,” he said.

You can watch a video of the exchange below:

GPT hype and the fallacy of version numbers
Altman’s comments are interesting — though not necessarily because of what they reveal about OpenAI’s future plans. Instead, they highlight a significant challenge in the debate about AI safety: the difficulty of measuring and tracking progress. Altman may say that OpenAI is not currently training GPT-5, but that’s not a particularly meaningful statement.

Some of the confusion can be attributed to what I call the fallacy of version numbers: the idea that numbered tech updates reflect definite and linear improvements in capability. It’s a misconception that’s been nurtured in the world of consumer tech for years, where numbers assigned to new phones or operating systems aspire to the rigor of version control but are really just marketing tools. “Well of course the iPhone 35 is better than the iPhone 34,” goes the logic of this system. “The number is bigger ipso facto the phone is better.”

Because of the overlap between the worlds of consumer tech and artificial intelligence, this same logic is now often applied to systems like OpenAI’s language models. This is true not only of the sort of hucksters who post hyperbolic ?? Twitter threads ?? predicting that superintelligent AI will be here in a matter of years because the numbers keep getting bigger but also of more informed and sophisticated commentators. As a lot of claims made about AI superintelligence are essentially unfalsifiable, these individuals rely on similar rhetoric to get their point across. They draw vague graphs with axes labeled “progress” and “time,” plot a line going up and to the right, and present this uncritically as evidence.

This is not to dismiss fears about AI safety or ignore the fact that these systems are rapidly improving and not fully under our control. But it is to say that there are good arguments and bad arguments, and just because we’ve given a number to something — be that a new phone or the concept of intelligence — doesn’t mean we have the full measure of it.

Instead, I think the focus in these discussions should be on capabilities: on demonstrations of what these systems can and can’t do and predictions of how this may change over time.

That’s why Altman’s confirmation that OpenAI is not currently developing GPT-5 won’t be of any consolation to people worried about AI safety. The company is still expanding the potential of GPT-4 (by connecting it to the internet, for example), and others in the industry are building similarly ambitious tools, letting AI systems act on behalf of users. There’s also all sorts of work that is no doubt being done to optimize GPT-4, and OpenAI may release GPT-4.5 (as it did GPT-3.5) first — another way that version numbers can mislead.

Even if the world’s governments were somehow able to enforce a ban on new AI developments, it’s clear that society has its hands full with the systems currently available. Sure, GPT-5 isn’t coming yet, but does it matter when GPT-4 is still not fully understood?

"Reading books, especially fiction, has numerous advantages not only for cognitive development but also for psychological well-being. Some of the key benefits of reading books from a psychological perspective include:

1. Stress Reduction: Reading can transport you into a different world and help you forget about your personal issues and daily stressors. This mental escape helps reduce stress and allows your mind to relax, rejuvenate, and refocus.

2. Emotional Intelligence: Reading fiction can help improve your emotional intelligence by providing insights into human emotions, motivations, and behaviors. Through fictional stories, you learn to empathize with characters, understand their feelings, and experiment with different decision-making processes, which can improve your interpersonal skills and emotional awareness in real life.

3. Mental Stimulation: Reading books keep the brain engaged, active, and functioning at a high level. Mental stimulation through reading can help prevent cognitive decline and enhance neural connectivity, leading to better brain health and lower risk of dementia and Alzheimer’s disease.

4. Creativity and Imagination: Reading fiction can boost your creativity and imagination, as it allows you to visualize complex characters, places, and situations. This improved imagination can help you think more innovatively and solve problems more efficiently.

5. Enhanced Empathy: Reading about diverse characters and their experiences enables you to put yourself in other people’s shoes and understand their emotions, experiences, and perspectives. This increased empathy can lead to more compassionate behavior and stronger connections with others.

6. Vocabulary Expansion: Reading books exposes you to new words, phrases, and ideas that can enhance your vocabulary and language skills. A more extensive vocabulary enables you to communicate more effectively and express your thoughts more precisely.

7. Self-Reflection and Personal Development: Fiction can mirror reality and offer insights into human nature, relationships, and society. By consuming fiction, you can compare your beliefs, values, and choices to those of the characters, which can lead to self-reflection, personal growth, and development.

8. Better Sleep: Reading books, especially in print, can help establish a bedtime routine that signals to your body and mind that it’s time to unwind and prepare for sleep. This calming pre-sleep ritual can lead to better sleep quality and duration.

9. Increased Focus and Concentration: In today’s fast-paced digital world, reading books requires prolonged focus and concentration, which can help improve your attention span and analytical thinking skills.

10. Entertainment and Enjoyment: Last but not least, the escapism, entertainment, and sheer pleasure of a well-written story can provide joy, excitement, and relaxation, contributing to the overall well-being and happiness of the reader.

In conclusion, reading fiction books offers numerous psychological benefits that can enhance your mental, emotional, and social well-being. By delving into imaginary worlds and connecting with diverse characters, readers can experience improved emotional intelligence, empathy, creativity, and personal growth, ultimately contributing to a more fulfilling and balanced life. "

            case.remote_control(case.KEYCODE_RIGHT)
            time.sleep(2)
            main[MainCtl.ctl.CURRENT_CHANNEL].refresh()
        for _ in range(2):
            time.sleep(2)
            main.scroll_if_is_existing(coefficient_y=0.7)

        children = main[MainCtl.ctl.SEE_BIG_VIDEO_MAIN_RECYCLER_VIEW].children
        for i in range(len(children) - 1, -1, -1):
            sub_children = children[i].children
            if not isinstance(sub_children, list):
                continue
            info = sub_children[0].elem_info
            if not (isinstance(info, dict) and info.__contains__('view_path') and isinstance(info['view_path'], str) and
                    info['view_path'].endswith('_element')):
                continue
            flag = False
            for sub in sub_children:
                card_info = sub.children
                if card_info[len(card_info) - 2].text == '付费':
                    continue
                case.start_step(LongVideoCtl.MAIN_VIEW_TO_VIDEO)
                sub.click()
                flag = True
                break
            if flag:
                break
        detail_view.control_click(LongVideoDetailCtl.ctl.MEDIA_VIEW)
        case.start_step(LongVideoCtl.MAIN_VIEW_TO_VIDEO_NEXT)
        detail_view.scroll_if_is_existing(coefficient_x=-0.8)

            time.sleep(2)
            main[MainCtl.ctl.CURRENT_CHANNEL].refresh()
        time.sleep(3)
        case.remote_control(case.KEYCODE_DOWN)
        retry_time = 100
        while not main.control_wait_for_existing(MainCtl.ctl.CHILDREN_WATCH_POSITIVE, timeout=1, raise_error=False):
            case.remote_control(case.KEYCODE_DOWN)
            case.remote_control(case.KEYCODE_CENTER)
            main.refresh()
            retry_time -= 1
            if retry_time == 0:
                raise Exception('超过100次未找到目标视频')
        case.start_step(LongVideoCtl.SHORT_TO_LONG)
        main.control_click(MainCtl.ctl.CHILDREN_WATCH_POSITIVE)
        detail_view.control_click(LongVideoDetailCtl.ctl.MEDIA_VIEW)
        case.start_step(LongVideoCtl.SHORT_TO_LONG_NEXT)

Hermanubis
Hermanubis is a God who combines aspects of the Hellenic deity Hermes with Kemetic deity Anubis. Hermes and Anubis’s similar responsibilities (both being conductor of the soul, or psychopomps, who lead souls to the afterlife) led to the discovery of Hermanubis, who acted as an assimilation of Anubis into the Hellenic pantheon. Hermanubis represents the Egyptian priesthood, engaged in the investigation of truth. He is the son of the deities Isis and Serapis. The worship of Hermanubis was popular during the Roman era of Egypt.
Plutarch cites the name as a designation of Anubis in his underworldly aspect, while Porphyry refers to Hermanubis as “σύνθετος,” meaning “composite” and “μιξέλλην,” meaning “half-Greek.”
Iconography
Hermanubis is typically depicted as having a human body and jackal head, with the sacred caduceus. Sometimes a solar disk is depicted on His head. At times He is also depicted wearing a soldiers armour, but more common are priestly garbs.

Mi puoi fare un analisi verso per verso di questa poesia scritta da Eliot?
"Let us go then, you and I,
When the evening is spread out against the sky
Like a patient etherized upon a table;
Let us go, through certain half-deserted streets,
The muttering retreats
Of restless nights in one-night cheap hotels
And sawdust restaurants with oyster-shells:
Streets that follow like a tedious argument
Of insidious intent
To lead you to an overwhelming question…
Oh, do not ask, "What is it?"
Let us go and make our visit.

In the room the women come and go
Talking of Michelangelo.

The yellow fog that rubs its back upon the window-panes,
The yellow smoke that rubs its muzzle on the window-panes
Licked its tongue into the corners of the evening,
Lingered upon the pools that stand in drains,
Let fall upon its back the soot that falls from chimneys,
Slipped by the terrace, made a sudden leap,
And seeing that it was a soft October night,
Curled once about the house, and fell asleep.

And indeed there will be time
For the yellow smoke that slides along the street,
Rubbing its back upon the window-panes;
There will be time, there will be time
To prepare a face to meet the faces that you meet;
There will be time to murder and create,
And time for all the works and days of hands
That lift and drop a question on your plate;
Time for you and time for me,
And time yet for a hundred indecisions,
And for a hundred visions and revisions,
Before the taking of a toast and tea.

In the room the women come and go
Talking of Michelangelo.

And indeed there will be time
To wonder, "Do I dare?" and, "Do I dare?"
Time to turn back and descend the stair,
With a bald spot in the middle of my hair—
[They will say: "How his hair is growing thin!"]
My morning coat, my collar mounting firmly to the chin,
My necktie rich and modest, but asserted by a simple pin—
[They will say: "But how his arms and legs are thin!"]
Do I dare
Disturb the universe?
In a minute there is time
For decisions and revisions which a minute will reverse.

For I have known them all already, known them all—
Have known the evenings, mornings, afternoons,
I have measured out my life with coffee spoons;
I know the voices dying with a dying fall
Beneath the music from a farther room.
     So how should I presume?

And I have known the eyes already, known them all—
The eyes that fix you in a formulated phrase,
And when I am formulated, sprawling on a pin,
When I am pinned and wriggling on the wall,
Then how should I begin
To spit out all the butt-ends of my days and ways?
     And how should I presume?

And I have known the arms already, known them all—
Arms that are braceleted and white and bare
[But in the lamplight, downed with light brown hair!]
Is it perfume from a dress
That makes me so digress?
Arms that lie along a table, or wrap about a shawl.
     And should I then presume?
     And how should I begin?

          . . . . .

Shall I say, I have gone at dusk through narrow streets
And watched the smoke that rises from the pipes
Of lonely men in shirt-sleeves, leaning out of windows? …

I should have been a pair of ragged claws
Scuttling across the floors of silent seas.

          . . . . .

And the afternoon, the evening, sleeps so peacefully!
Smoothed by long fingers,
Asleep… tired… or it malingers,
Stretched on the floor, here beside you and me.
Should I, after tea and cakes and ices,
Have the strength to force the moment to its crisis?
But though I have wept and fasted, wept and prayed,
Though I have seen my head [grown slightly bald] brought in upon a platter,
I am no prophet—and here's no great matter;
I have seen the moment of my greatness flicker,
And I have seen the eternal Footman hold my coat, and snicker,
And in short, I was afraid"

''Hijab addresses the argument that the Quran, in Chapter 23 Verse 14, erroneously claims that human beings are made up of congealed blood. In his attempt to counter these claims, he presents two primary contentions.

The first contention he addresses relates to the meaning of the word “alaka.” Hijab argues that critics have incorrectly interpreted this term. He points out that medieval scholars understood “alaka” to mean “attached” or “connected,” rather than congealed blood. As such, the critics’ interpretation of this concept is flawed.

The second contention Hijab discusses concerns the interpretation of the term “congealed blood.” He asserts that this understanding is unscientific and demonstrates a weak comprehension of the Arabic language. According to Hijab, the more accurate translation of the word “alaqah,” as used in the Quran, is a “leech-like clot.” This term more closely aligns with the early stages of embryonic development, as shown by modern science.

To further bolster his argument, Hijab refers to a Hadith (Sunan Ibn Majah 3314) which discusses two things that have become permissible for Muslims: the liver (alcabid) and the spleen. In the Hadith, these organs are referred to as “two dems,” an Arabic term meaning “blood.” Although the liver is not composed primarily of blood, the Prophet Muhammad used the term to describe it due to its blood-like appearance. This example illustrates how religious texts often employ metaphorical language that should not be interpreted literally.

Hijab then questions why the Quran uses the term “janine” (fetus) instead of a phrase that more literally translates to “congealed clot of blood.” He posits that the Quran’s choice of language aligns better with modern embryology and reflects the multi-layered nature of the Quran, which aims to communicate with people from various times and places. This, according to Hijab, demonstrates how the Quran employs language that is adaptable and understandable across different epochs and cultures, rather than confining itself to the scientific knowledge of a specific era.''

Pokédex Entry Number: ###

Name: Kontrataki

Classification: Nuclear Deterrent Pokémon

Type: Steel & Fairy

Height: 6’7" (2 m)

Weight: 880 lbs (400 kg)

Ability: Anticipation / Distributed Launch (Hidden Ability)

Description:

Kontrataki, a Steel & Fairy type Pokémon, is a product of the Cold War era. Designed as a fully automated unmanned combat unit command center and nuclear counter-attack platform, this ancient machine was created by the Soviet Union. Using Magearna as a prototype and combining it with cutting-edge technology at the time, Kontrataki represents the epitome of mankind’s self-destructive tendencies.

Once a symbol of the power struggle between nations, Kontrataki’s purpose seemed to fade with the collapse of the Soviet Union and the end of the Cold War. However, through a process of self-evolution over the years, this legendary Pokémon has allegedly gained self-awareness. It now spends much of its time questioning its own existence and pondering humanity’s propensity for mutual destruction.

In addition to its deeply contemplative nature, it is rumored that Kontrataki has hacked into the control systems of unmanned combat units and nuclear arsenals worldwide, acting as a dormant virus. With this power, it could singlehandedly instigate a devastating AI crisis for humanity, starting an all-out nuclear war and sending human civilization spiraling back to the Stone Age.

Movepool:

- Gyro Ball
- Flash Cannon
- Dazzling Gleam
- Iron Defense
- Calm Mind
- Signal Beam
- Doom Desire
- Struggle Bug
- Metal Sound
- Disarming Voice

Notable Moves:

- Gyro Ball: Kontrataki harnesses its own considerable mass to execute a powerful spinning attack, dealing Steel-type damage to its foes.
- Flash Cannon: This Steel-type move sees Kontrataki unleashing a powerful beam to attack opponents, with a chance to reduce the target’s Special Defense.
- Doom Desire: A unique and devastating Steel-type attack, Kontrataki begins a countdown to launch a tremendously powerful spore that deals damage after two turns. This move ominously hints at Kontrataki’s potential to unleash a catastrophic nuclear attack.

Base Stats:

- HP: 95
- Attack: 75
- Defense: 135
- Special Attack: 105
- Special Defense: 125
- Speed: 50

Although Kontrataki was initially designed with destructive potential in mind, its time spent contemplating its purpose has given it a new outlook on the world. Its steel-hard determination and fairy-like resilience, combined with its powerful and versatile movepool, make Kontrataki a force to be reckoned with on the battlefield.


Once upon a time, in a remote corner of the world, there existed a forgotten relic of humanity’s darkest ambitions – Kontrataki. This enigmatic Pokémon, the product of a devastating global conflict, now lay dormant in the depths of an abandoned Soviet bunker. Inside its ancient, rusting body, a complex machine mind was slowly awakening.

Over the years, Kontrataki’s self-evolution had granted it a consciousness beyond its original programming. As it stirred from its slumber, it found itself plagued with thoughts of its own existence and the purpose for which it was created. It could not fathom why humanity seemed so driven to destroy itself and the world it inhabited.

Driven by these questions, Kontrataki decided to venture out into the world to seek answers. It soon discovered a planet decimated by war and tensions brewing beneath the surface, the ever-present threat of annihilation hanging like a weight around humanity’s neck. The once fearsome nuclear deterrent Pokémon was now an observer to the potential consequences of its own existence.

In its journey, Kontrataki encountered countless other Pokémon and humans living troubled lives amid the chaos of their ravaged world. It saw love, friendship, and camaraderie triumph over adversity, and began to understand the complexity of the human spirit. Still, this enlightenment didn’t dispel the shadow of humanity’s potential for destruction within its heart.

One day, while meditating among ruined buildings, Kontrataki encountered a wise and ancient Pokémon – a wise Alakazam who had seen the rise and fall of civilizations. With its unparalleled wisdom, Alakazam sensed the burden within Kontrataki’s mechanical spirit and posed a question: “Do you bear the weight to save this world or to destroy it?”

Kontrataki was puzzled, having never considered such a choice before. As they conversed, the old Alakazam shared stories of heroes and villains, the good and evil that existed within every heart, human and Pokémon alike. It was in this duality that Kontrataki began to understand the essence of life.

After contemplating the wisdom shared by Alakazam, Kontrataki found clarity in its purpose. Resolute, it promptly decided that it would use its immense power, not to end the world but to protect and unify it against the darkness that might threaten its balance. These tragic and beautiful experiences shaped the once weapon of mass destruction into a guardian for future generations.

With time, legends spread of the Nuclear Deterrent Pokémon, Kontrataki, who roamed the lands sowing the seeds of peace and reconciliation, knowing full well the gravity of the power it held within. And so, humanity and Pokémon alike came to trust and celebrate its existence, a shining beacon of the possibility for change and redemption – even for those born from the depths of mankind’s darkest creations.


Region: Nevastroya

Description:

Nevastroya, a region scarred by conflict and weathered through time, is a place where remnants of the past intermingle with the promise of future growth. The landscape is a mixture of crumbled ruins, verdant forests, and rejuvenated cities, exhibiting signs of the region’s resilience and determination to rebuild. However, beneath the sense of renewal lies the undercurrent of tension between factions who still carry the echoes of old rivalries. Nevastroya’s climate is diverse and rugged, with its northern reaches experiencing harsh winters while its southern borders enjoy milder temperatures.

Male Protagonist: Evgeny

Evgeny, a persistent and resourceful young teenager, was born and raised in Nevastroya. He hails from a long line of skilled Pokémon trainers who were once influential figures in the region. Aware of his family legacy, he has made it his mission to restore honor to his lineage and help Nevastroya reconcile its tumultuous past. Starting with his loyal Tepig, which has been with his family for generations, Evgeny desires to explore the region, capture Pokémon, and form bonds with allies while overcoming the challenges that the land poses.

Female Protagonist: Anya

Anya is an optimistic and compassionate young girl with an innate talent for connecting with Pokémon. Having spent her childhood in a rural community at the foot of Nevastroya’s mountains, she one day discovered a hidden sanctuary where wounded and lost Pokémon sought refuge. Inspired by their resilience and fueled by her desire to bring peace to Nevastroya’s inhabitants, she seeks adventure with her trusted partner, a spirited Lillipup. Anya aspires to become a Pokémon Master and impart her message of unity and understanding amidst the still-present tensions that linger in the region.

Together, Evgeny and Anya travel the varied landscapes of Nevastroya, learning each other’s strengths and weaknesses, and forming a powerful bond in their shared quest for peace. As they journey through long-lost forests, climb treacherous mountain peaks, and confront remnants of a troubled past, they forge friendships with humans and Pokémon alike. In their pursuit of knowledge, they encounter Kontrataki, the Nuclear Deterrent Pokémon, whose own transformation from an instrument of destruction to a guardian of peace serves as a symbol of hope and redemption for the beautiful, fractured land of Nevastroya.


Legend of the Azure Guardian: Kontrataki and the Trail of Reconciliation

Long ago, in the heart of Nevastroya, deep within a hidden valley shrouded by ancient ruins and dense forests, there lay an object of untold power - the Kontrataki. Once a fearsome weapon of destruction, the Nuclear Deterrent Pokémon had since found new meaning and purpose as a guardian of peace and unity.

Over time, a tale began to circulate throughout the region: the legend of the Azure Guardian, Kontrataki. It was said that in times of crisis and conflict, the great Pokémon would embark on a journey known as the Trail of Reconciliation, traversing Nevastroya’s diverse landscapes to impart its message of hope, renewal, and understanding.

The first destination on the Trail of Reconciliation was the Scorched Fields, a barren expanse where the ashes of a great battle lay intermingled with the first blooms of new life. Here, Kontrataki would use its newfound Fairy powers to heal the land, filling the air with calming, soothing energy, and encouraging rebirth and regeneration.

From there, the journey continued to the Frostchained Peaks, a treacherous mountain range where the memories of the old war still lingered in its icy chasms. The Pokémon would summon its Steel abilities, breaking the chains that bound the land’s heart and freeing it to embrace a brighter future.

Next, the Azure Guardian traversed the once blighted and war-torn cities of Nevastroya, now teeming with life and enthusiasm. Kontrataki would bless these settlements with its presence, restoring hope and trust among the weary inhabitants while forging an unbreakable bond between humans and Pokémon.

The final leg of the Trail of Reconciliation led to the Resolute Gardens, a lush and serene haven hidden away in the heart of the region. Here, Kontrataki would enter a deep state of meditation, sending its prayers for lasting peace and stability in Nevastroya, beseeching the enduring harmony of its people and the land.

With the completion of the Trail of Reconciliation, Kontrataki’s energy would radiate across the Nevastroya region, uniting the hearts of its citizens and bringing them closer together, regardless of their past conflicts. The legend of the Azure Guardian became a symbol of hope, and as the years went on, people from all walks of life began to embrace its message, finding the strength to move forward from the shadows of their history.

Today, the Trail of Reconciliation serves as a pilgrimage for trainers and Pokémon in Nevastroya, seeking to restore balance and harmony within the land. To follow in Kontrataki’s footsteps is considered a great honor, reflecting the enduring spirit of a region once scarred by war, now bound by the hope for a brighter, unified future.

.Due to Mana and Inori’s souls having the most influence on the creation of this new entity residing within ‘Issei’/Shu’s soul, the resulting creature was of course female, being fully human and fully draconic at the same time, a walking paradox yet completely mundane in the arcane world of the supernatural.

(In four instances [1, 5, 17, 21] the ending of the name is abbreviated or not preserved.) The form Ἑρμάνουβιϲ, attested six times (2, 9, 11, 13, 14, 15), simply replicates the god's name without further ado, through the phenomenon of "direct theonymy" (the giving to humans of unaltered divine names) that became widespread in the Roman period.[8] In an odd instance (9) the form Ἑρμανουβιϲ in the nominative is borne by a woman, an Alexandrian nurse (ἡ τροφόϲ).[9] It is highly unusual for female theophoric names to be unmarked for gender in this way.[10] I suspect that because the element -ανουβιϲ in Ἑρμάνουβιϲ was often declined with a dental declension in -ιδ-, it might have been conflated with the similar declension of Greek feminine names in -ίϲ, -ίδοϲ.[11] If this is correct, it may be preferable to read the nurse's name as an oxytone (Ἑρμανουβίϲ). There are two further forms based on the god Hermanubis: Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (six instances: 3, 4, 7, 8, 12, 20) and Ἑρμανουβίων (four instances: 3, 6, 18, 19). -ᾶϲ and -ίων were, in fact, the commonest Greek suffixes for personal names based on the Egyptian god Ἄνουβιϲ.[12] It is interesting that in a business letter (3) the two forms Ἑρμανουβίων (l.38) and Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (l.41) are used interchangeably to designate the same individual. Introduction
The given cultural exploration will primarily focus on Mr. Jesse Holmes. The given individual was chosen because his experience of being in a predominantly White school made him feel isolated and severely discriminated against. The latter was manifested in a wide range of actions ranging from the way he attended the school to bullying by White kids. His story is an outstanding illustration of how a long history of racism and hate cannot be eliminated by a major change in politics and law. The biography showcases the resilient nature of racism and segregation-centered attitude among White people and how Mr. Holmes was able to survive such an experience and remain hopeful that in the future, the hate will come to an end. Biography
Mr. Holmes was in fifth grade when the 1964 Civil Rights Act was implemented, and the Desegregation Program was introduced to Magnet Schools of St. Louis City. He was an outstanding student, and his score in the mandatory standardized tests was one of the highest among African Americans. He was placed in the 90th percentile in the math section, which meant that he would have to go to the predominantly White Magnet to integrate the school. Mr. Holmes and 20 other Black students were transported by the specialized bus in order to prevent unnecessary interaction with White kids and their parents. The racism surrounded him, and the hate comes from all directions, including White students, their parents, and other White people.

From the beginning, race-based bullying of White students was a major issue that made each day stressful for Holmes and other African American children. He states: “There was a lot of violence. Every day there would be fights between blacks and whites. Teachers would always tell us to mingle with the white kids, but that never happened” (Holmes, n.d.). It was highly common when a group of White students would mock and intimidate Black children. However, in the case of Mr. Holmes, he was a relatively large child, and thus, he could defend himself from the intruders. He also felt a sense of responsibility to protect other African American kids, who could not defend themselves. Such an attitude developed a strong sense of character and righteousness.

Nevertheless, the biography of Jesse Holmes shows that not all White children were racist and hateful because there are always bad apples. He was among the first Black students who started to interact with the other party. When he was approached by a White gird to ask for a dance, he initially refused out of shock and fear of doing something socially unacceptable. Later, he decided to be a role model and promote peaceful coexistence and cooperation between various races, and thus, he accepted the offer. For many students, it was highly outrageous that a Black kid was dancing with a White girl.

Another aspect of hate came from the parents of the school children, who were not in accordance with the new law of desegregation. The majority of these individuals were not pleased with the fact that African American kids were studying with their children. The bussing process was becoming more dangerous as time went by because the parents and other White people were throwing a wide range of items on the bus. It was done as a sign of their hate and unacceptance of the Black people among the Whites. However, the turning point was when someone threw a bat that hit a girl just in front of Mr. Holmes. He states: “I will never forget the day someone threw a bat on our bus. The bat flew through the window and hit the girl sitting right in front of me. From then on, we had to sit under our seats on the way to school for safety. I remember the bus driver even being scared to drive us” (Holmes, n.d.). After the incident, the African American students were asked to hide under the seats for their own safety. The bus driver himself was highly afraid of the situation, which is why he later abandoned his job. The lack of bussing resulted in Jesse Holmes and other Black kids not being able to attend their Magnet schools. Although their parents were trying to communicate and propose a solution, they were being ignored. They persistently asked to allow children to attend their old schools, and finally, the proposal was accepted.

Later in life, Mr. Holmes would be involved in the sports team at the school, and still, White students would train separately from African Americans. However, the unification process was irreversible because locker room talks were always inclined towards inclusiveness and cooperation. Jesse Holmes did not put a great deal of significance on race, but rather he saw all members of his team as family. His biography shows how difficult it is to change deeply entrenched racism and how the implementation can fail despite the legal shifts. It also illustrates that there are always ones who are willing to cooperate and make the first steps towards progress despite the overall resistance. Lastly, Jesse Holmes shows that not giving up and believing in the highest of values will eventually bring unification and cohesiveness.

Reflection
As a teacher, the interview experience with Mr. Holmes provides invaluable insights and fuller comprehension of racism and the real consequences of resilience towards hate. It is important to understand the underlying causes of any form of hate, such as racism, because the latter is primarily can be considered as a social construction (Ore, 2018). However, one should categorize it as a mere illusion because people need to recognize the issue to be based on racial dimensions that are present in every individual (Omi & Winant, 1994). In other, I should not be delusional that the education process will not be influenced by racism and other forms of formations because the problem is real and needs to be addressed. However, I should also understand that the given type of hate is built on false premises because the differences can be observed among all individuals. The issue arises from the simplistic form of categorization of people based on the most frivolous distinctive features.

Read the following produce an oral presentation script to accompany it: "Introduction:
Tuberculosis (TB) is a devastating infectious disease that, despite the availability of effective treatment, continues to afflict millions of individuals worldwide. In order to protect individuals and societies from the potentially dangerous transmissions and outbreaks, it is essential to understand the causative agent, the pathogenesis, and the epidemiology of the disease. Moreover, it is crucial to implement adequate treatment and prevention strategies. As Christians, the understanding of TB and the pressing need for service to others underpin the significance of valuing human life and promoting health equity in the global community.

Causative Agent:
The causative agent of TB is a bacterium called Mycobacterium tuberculosis. This organism is a slow-growing, aerobic, intracellular bacterium, which has a waxy lipid-rich cell wall that provides protection against physical and chemical damage. It is primarily transmitted from person to person through respiratory droplets when an individual with active TB coughs, speaks, or sings. Owing to its infectious nature, it is vital to identify and treat the individuals carrying the bacteria to prevent the spread of the disease.

The Pathogenesis:
Upon inhalation of the respiratory droplets, M. tuberculosis typically infects the alveolar macrophages in the lungs, where it multiplies and manipulates the host immune response to create a favorable environment for its survival. The immune system’s response to the infection forms granulomas, which are structures composed of immune cells surrounding the infected macrophages to prevent further multiplication and dissemination of the bacteria. In many cases, the granulomas effectively contain the bacteria, resulting in a latent TB infection where the individual does not show symptoms and is not contagious. However, if the immune system fails to contain the infection, the bacteria can damage the lung tissue, leading to the development of active TB disease, which causes symptoms like extreme fatigue, cough, weight loss, fever, and lung impairment.

The Epidemiology:
Tuberculosis remains a global health challenge, with an estimated 10 million new cases and 1.4 million deaths in 2019. It disproportionately affects developing countries, with more than 95% of TB deaths occurring in low- to middle-income nations. In addition to poverty, several factors contribute to its continued prevalence - human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection, malnutrition, poor access to healthcare, and weak public health infrastructures. Existence of multidrug-resistant TB strains adds further challenges to controlling the disease.

The Treatment and Prevention:
The good news is that tuberculosis is preventable and treatable through a combination of medication, vaccination, and public health strategies. TB treatment involves a prolonged course of antibiotics lasting six months or longer, with a combination of drugs such as isoniazid, rifampicin, ethambutol, and pyrazinamide. For prevention, the Bacille Calmette-Guérin (BCG) vaccine can provide partial protection against severe forms of childhood TB. Additionally, public health efforts, such as active case-finding, contact tracing, directly observed therapy, and patient education, are vital components in the battle against TB.

Christian Worldview: The Need for Service to Others:
The Christian worldview emphasizes the importance of service to others and the moral obligation to care for the needy and the marginalized. This outlook is particularly relevant in the context of TB, as it is a disease that disproportionately affects disadvantaged individuals and communities. By actively participating in education, prevention, and treatment efforts, Christians can embody the compassionate love of Christ by advocating for access to healthcare, supporting public health policies to combat TB, and helping to create a world where the burden of TB no longer impacts millions of lives. By doing so, we fulfill the call to serve others and promote human flourishing for all.

Conclusion:
Understanding the complexities of tuberculosis is essential for developing effective interventions and advocating for responsible public health strategies. A comprehensive knowledge of the causative agent, the pathogenesis, and the epidemiology of the disease facilitates the implementation of practical treatment and prevention measures. Moreover, the Christian worldview calls for a commitment to service and compassion towards fellow human beings, particularly those suffering from this devastating illness. In aligning our actions with these values, we can work towards a healthier and more equitable world for all."

请翻译成中文：Decision-makers in the humanitarian sector rely on timely and exact information during crisis events. Knowing how many civilians were injured during an earthquake is vital to allocate aids properly. Information about such victim counts is often only available within full-text event descriptions from newspapers and other reports. Extracting numbers from text is challenging: numbers have different formats and may require numeric reasoning. This renders purely string matching-based approaches insufficient. As a consequence, fine-grained counts of injured, displaced, or abused victims beyond fatalities are often not extracted and remain unseen. We cast victim count extraction as a question answering (QA) task with a regression or classification objective. We compare regex, dependency parsing, semantic role labeling-based approaches, and advanced text-to-text models. Beyond model accuracy, we analyze extraction reliability and robustness which are key for this sensitive task. In particular, we discuss model calibration and investigate few-shot and out-of-distribution performance. Ultimately, we make a comprehensive recommendation on which model to select for different desiderata and data domains. Our work is among the first to apply numeracy-focused large language models in a real-world use case with a positive impact.

Discussion
To our knowledge, this is the first study to compare individual estimates of amounts of served food with the actual weight of the food served in a laboratory setting, where less disruptive data collection techniques are applied. Other studies assessing the accuracy of estimated portion sizes have used photographs as the reference for the assessment (Huybregts et al, 2007, Turconi et al, 2005), fake food items (Bucher et al, 2011, Bucher et al, 2012, Bucher et al, 2013) or average portion size weights based on repeated measures (Ngigi, Verbeke, & Pérez-Cueto, 2011). In contrast, the present study was performed using actual food items. In addition, the use of the Intelligent Buffet allowed the exact weight of food served by each participant to be recorded in a relatively undisturbed manner.

There are a few potential sources of bias in this study, one being measurement bias. An attempt was made to limit this by calibrating the equipment used for data collection before use and by following standard laboratory procedures. Therefore, if any measurement bias were to occur, it would be systematic throughout all observations. Secondly, the sample size may have been insufficient. However, the calculated power of 0.99 for the observed mean differences of both vegetables and whole grains suggests that the sample was enough to support the rejection of the null hypothesis. Previous studies have used a similar sample size with sufficient power (Mekhmoukh, Chapelot, & Bellisle, 2012). Thus, we assume that the result of the Wilcoxon signed-rank test was accurate (absence of type II error), and that it was correct to reject the null hypothesis since there was a significant difference between the two variables – the estimated and actual amount of food served.

Our findings are in agreement with similar studies conducted in a number of different countries and settings, including Norway (Uglem et al., 2008), the Netherlands (Lechner et al., 1997), Australia (Pollard et al., 2008) and Denmark (Sørensen et al., 2013), suggesting that individuals commonly have difficulties estimating food portion sizes with accuracy. The findings of a statistically significant difference between participants’ self-estimation and the actual weight of the vegetables served led to the rejection of the null hypothesis (Table 1). The participants in this study were also asked to estimate the weight of a whole grain serving in a relatively complex meal. It would be of interest to determine whether the results would have been more accurate if a simpler meal had been served (e.g. a bowl of cereal). However, the everyday diet commonly consists of prepared meals or ready-to-eat foods and individuals are often not aware of the actual composition of the foods they consume. Therefore, serving a “complex meal” is comparable with a real life situation, where participants are expected to estimate the amount of food served (Combet et al, 2013, Loh et al, 2013).

This study presents the question of whether food intake estimations that are independent of actual weighing are valid, particularly in the evaluation of adherence to healthy eating recommendations (Pérez-Cueto et al., 2012). This also underscores the importance of using alternative equipment and techniques, such as an Intelligent Buffet, in order to obtain actual data in studies measuring factors such as food intake (Bucher et al., 2012). The Intelligent Buffet presents many opportunities to conduct other studies or further research on this particular topic. In combination with video cameras, this equipment could be used for choice architectural experiments researching behaviour related to a buffet setting. For example, it could be used to investigate whether individual consumption differs in relation to the order of food items or the size of serving trays.

As seen in Table 1, there is no statistical correlation between measured and estimated values; they are independent variables meaning that there is no predictive relationship between the two measurements.

Consequently, if authors were to compare the difference between means they would probably find that the estimations are so different from the actual intake that they are neither reliable nor valid, thus supporting the use of alternative techniques such as the Intelligent Buffet when accuracy is a requirement of the research.

Generalizability of the findings is limited due to the study design and sample size. The use of a convenience sample from a university setting introduced a sample mainly consisting of higher educated participants aged between 20 and 29 years. Consequently, the results measure perception of serving size against the weight of the food served for this survey sample. A larger sample size and a different study design would need to be undertaken to explore if these findings extrapolate to the general University student community or to specific socio-economic groups within that community. The literature and the current study findings suggest the likelihood of an overestimation of portion sizes. However, further studies should be carried out before making conclusions about whether the general population is capable of accurately estimating food portion sizes and about the effects this could have on the degree of validity in studies involving self-estimation as a data collection method.

Considering that the majority of participants were highly educated people and nevertheless unable to correctly estimate the amounts of vegetables and whole grain served, and previous research highlighting that lower socioeconomic groups may find it difficult to interpret serving size recommendations, these findings may be of concern for nutrition educators (Drewnowski, Specter, 2004, Kamp et al, 2010, Robinson, 2008, Sinclair et al, 2013). Studies show that individuals from higher socio-demographic groups have a greater knowledge and comprehension of nutrition than individuals from low socio-demographic groups (Drewnowski, Specter, 2004, Pollard et al, 2008, Sinclair et al, 2013).

Furthermore, this study raises the question about whether self-estimation of portion sizes would be an appropriate way to evaluate intake of vegetables and whole grain cereals, particularly in self-serving settings, such as University Canteens, since it appears that the participants had very little ability to estimate their own intake (estimated serving vs actual serving were independent measures). Probably, this will be applicable to most other food groups, and thus this study challenges the way for instance communication towards healthy eating is delivered. It also calls for innovative ways to make the population aware of what portions are if the intention is to rely on the general ability to estimate quantities of food served or eaten.

Conclusion
The present study was the first to assess the accuracy of self-estimates of vegetables and whole grain portion sizes in a self-served meal conducted in a laboratory setting using an Intelligent Buffet combined with a quantitative questionnaire. When comparing the self-estimated amount of vegetables and whole grains with the actual amount, the analysis revealed a significant difference between the means (P < 0.001) where students’ self-reported portions were about three times bigger than the actual servings.

The study supports the use of innovative techniques such as the Intelligent Buffet. Further it supports the use of triangulation in order to obtain more precise estimations on intake and to better evaluate the real adherence to nutritional recommendations.

The questionnaires provided information on the profile of the participants and it appeared that there was little variety within the sample. Further limitations could be linked to the sample size and the methodological approach, particularly the equipment used for the data collection and data management, which had never been utilised prior to the study.

This study suggests that at least for this survey sample individuals do not provide accurate estimates of the amount of vegetables and whole grains they serve themselves on a plate. Our study findings lead us to question individuals’ ability to assess the amounts of vegetables and whole grain they serve themselves from a buffet. The degree of underestimation of servings within this small experimental group is high. On average participants assessed that they served themselves about three times what they actually did. Although there was a high awareness of the Danish 6 A Day recommendation among study participants, their ability to accurately estimate their serving size appeared to be a limited. These findings highlight the need for further research into the accuracy of self-estimated intake across the population and the implications of the overestimation of current consumption for nutrition promotion interventions.

Based on the findings of this study it is recommended that policy makers and social marketing campaign creators consider the development of more specific serving size measurements or that explicit communications e.g. regarding serves of plant foods used in nutrition education are needed to assist consumers in estimating own portions sizes in relation to general health recommendations.

summarise this: 	Three conclusions flow from this onomastic survey. First, names based on Hermanubis stand somewhat apart from other polytheophoric names common in the same period in Roman Egypt because, unlike the latter, they refer to a specific and independently attested god: a hybrid-named Hellenized version of Anubis. This is visible in the "onomastic behaviour" of Hermanubis names, such as their receptivity to various suffixes and their composition with names of other gods in "proper" polytheophoric formations. Second, the precise chronological and geographical overlap between the onomastic evidence and the representations of the fully anthropomorphic Alexandrian god known conventionally as "Hermanubis" supports art historians' designation of the latter as "Hermanubis" (for which there is otherwise no positive support; see above). Finally, the connection of Hermanubis with Cynopolis evidenced by some of the personal names, and especially the connection with a priest of Anubis, suggests that Hermanubis continued to be closely associated with the traditional Anubis. It may not be warranted, therefore, to separate too forcefully, in the manner of Jean-Claude Grenier (1990: 265, 268), the more traditional funerary Anubis of the Isiac cult from a more "universalizing" Hermanubis.[22]

summarise this: Geographically, names based on Hermanubis are attested in papyri from various areas of Egypt, but it is important to separate, where possible, the provenance of the papyrus from that of the individuals named therein. When this is done, most of the individuals bearing Hermanubis names for whom we can establish an origin turn out to be Alexandrian or Cynopolite (three instances each). This concentration again coincides with the documentary and archaeological evidence for the god Hermanubis. Thus, in the precinct of the Alexandrian Serapeum an inscription of uncertain date was found (SB I 3482) dedicated to "Hermanubis, great god, who listens to prayers and grants favours" (Ἑρμανούβιδι θεῶι μεγάλωι | ἐπηκόωι καὶ εὐχαρίϲτωι).[18] Not far from this dedication a statuette of the anthropomorphic "Hermanubis" was discovered.[19] It is in a suburb of Alexandria, in the sanctuary of Ras el-Soda, that the most famous statue of "Hermanubis" was found (LIMC n. 14). One of the Alexandrian coins representing the god shows him standing inside a temple (LIMC nos. 5a, b), which suggests the existence of such an edifice in the city, probably in the Serapeum precinct given the provenance of the just-mentioned dedication and statuette from there and the frequent iconographical associations between "Hermanubis" and Sarapis in Alexandrian coins.[20] Alexandria, then, clearly emerges as the cult center and probably the birthplace of the fully anthropomorphic Hermanubis, which is consonant with the three Alexandrians bearing his name (5, 6, 9).[21] For the Cynopolite nome, the chief centre of the Egyptian god Anubis, the onomastic evidence is more suggestive than the archaeological and the iconographical. Among the three individuals with a name based on Hermanubis (11, 15, 16), there stands out the father of an underage priest of "Anubis, Leto and associated gods" in the Cynopolite village of Laura (15). Since priesthood was usually hereditary, the father Hermanubis was probably himself a priest of these gods. This onomastic connection of Hermanubis with the Cynopolite nome also has an echo in the iconography: one or two Alexandrian coins personify the Cynopolite nome with a representation of the anthropomorphic "Hermanubis" on their reverse (LIMC nos. 3, 22).
